1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Ta-Seen-Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic language – Allah, and to whomever he reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Ta-Sin-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings.]

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: These are the verses of the perspicuous Book.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: These are verses of the clear Book.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: Those are the signs of the Manifest Book.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: These are the verses of a Book luminous.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: These are the Verses of the manifest Book [this Quran, which was promised by Allah in the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel), makes things clear].

2.author: Itani
2.text: These are the Verses of the Clarifying Book.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: These are the verses of the Clear Book.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: These are Ayat of the Book Mubin.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: These are revelations of the Scripture that maketh plain.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: These are the signs of the Manifest Book.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: Those are the verses of the clear Book.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: These are the verses of the clear Book.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: These are the verses of the illustrious Book.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: These are the verses of the Book that makes (things) clear.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: These are the verses of the Book that makes things clear.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: These are verses of the Book that makes (things) clear.

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: You may perhaps wear out your heart because they do not come to belief.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Possibly you may risk your life (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) by grieving, because they did not believe.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Perchance thou consumest thyself that they are not believers.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Belike thou shalt kill thyself with grief because they become not believers.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: It may be that you (O Muhammad SAW) are going to kill yourself with grief, that they do not become believers [in your Risalah (Messengership) and in your Message of Islamic Monotheism].

3.author: Itani
3.text: Perhaps you will destroy yourself with grief, because they do not become believers.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: (O Muhammad), you will perhaps grieve yourself to death because these people do not believe.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: It may be that you are going Bakhi` yourself, that they do not become believers.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: It may be that thou tormentest thyself (O Muhammad) because they believe not.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: You are liable to imperil your life [out of distress] that they will not have faith.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Perhaps you consume yourself that they are not believers.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Perhaps, [O Muhammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: You will perhaps kill yourself with anguish because they are not accepting the faith.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Perhaps you will kill yourself with grief because they do not believe.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: It may be that you will destroy yourself with grief because they will not believe.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: It may be thou frettest thy soul with grief, that they do not become Believers.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: LaAAallaka bakhiAAun nafsaka alla yakoonoo mumineena

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: We could send down from the heavens a sign to them if We pleased, before which their heads would remain bowed.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: If We will, We can send down on them a sign from the sky so that their leaders would remain bowed before it.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: If We will, We shall send down on them out of heaven a sign, so their necks will stay humbled to it.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: If We list, We can send down unto them a sign from the heaven so that their necks would became to it submissive.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: If We will, We could send down to them from the heaven a sign, to which they would bend their necks in humility.

4.author: Itani
4.text: If We will, We can send down upon them a sign from heaven, at which their necks will stay bent in humility.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: If We will, We can send down a Sign to them from heaven, so their necks will be humbled to it.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: If We will, We could send down to them from the heaven a sign, to which they would bend their necks in humility.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: If We will, We can send down on them from the sky a portent so that their necks would remain bowed before it.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: If We wish We will send down to them a sign from the sky before which their heads will remain bowed in humility.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: If We will, We can send down on them a sign from heaven before which their necks will remain humbled.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: Had We wanted, We would have sent them a miracle from the sky to make their heads hang down in submission.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: If We please, We should send down upon them a sign from the heaven so that their necks should stoop to it.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: But if We had so willed, We could have sent down to them a sign from the heavens so that their heads would be bowed down before it in utter humility.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: If (such) were Our Will, We could send down to them from the sky a Sign, to which they would bend their necks in humility.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: In nasha nunazzil AAalayhim mina alssamai ayatan fathallat aAAnaquhum laha khadiAAeena

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Never does a new reminder come to them from Ar-Rahman but they turn away from it.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And never does a new advice come to them from the Most Gracious, but they turn away from it.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: But never fresh remembrance comes to them from the All-merciful, except they turn away from it.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And there cometh not unto them any fresh admonition from the Compassionate but they are wont to be there from backsliders.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And never comes there unto them a Reminder as a recent revelation from the Most Beneficent (Allah), but they turn away therefrom.

5.author: Itani
5.text: No fresh reminder comes to them from the Most Merciful, but they turn their backs at it.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Never does there come to them an admonition from the Merciful Lord but they turn away from it.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: And never comes there unto them a Reminder as a recent revelation from the Most Gracious, but they turn away therefrom.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Never cometh there unto them a fresh reminder from the Beneficent One, but they turn away from it.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: There does not come to them any new reminder from the All-beneficent but that they disregard it.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: A fresh remembrance has never come to them from the Merciful, except they turn away from it.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And no revelation comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Whenever a new message comes to them from the Beneficent God, they turn away from it.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And there does not come to them a new reminder from the Beneficent Allah but they turn aside from it.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Whenever there comes to them any fresh warning from the Merciful, they always turn their backs on it:

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: But there comes not to them a newly-revealed Message from (Allah) Most Gracious, but they turn away therefrom.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Wama yateehim min thikrin mina alrrahmani muhdathin illa kanoo AAanhu muAArideena

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Surely they have done with denying; soon will come to them the news of what they were laughing at.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: Indeed they have denied, so now soon coming upon them are the tidings of what they used to scoff at.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: So they have cried lies; therefore assuredly tidings will come to them of that they mocked at.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: So they have surely belied; wherefore anon there shall come unto them the truth of that whereat they were wont to mock.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: So they have indeed denied (the truth - this Quran), then the news of what they mocked at, will come to them.

6.author: Itani
6.text: They have denied the truth, but soon will come to them the news of what they ridiculed.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: They will soon come to know the truth of that which they have been scoffing at.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: So, they have indeed denied, then the news of what they mocked at will come to them.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Now they have denied (the Truth); but there will come unto them tidings of that whereat they used to scoff.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: They have already denied [the truth], but soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: So, they belied, but surely the tidings of that they mocked will come to them.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: They have called (our revelation) lies. They will soon learn the consequences of what they mocked.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: So they have indeed rejected (the truth), therefore the news of that which they mock shall soon come to them.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: they have indeed rejected the message. But the truth of what they laughed to scorn will dawn upon them before long.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: They have indeed rejected (the Message): so they will know soon (enough) the truth of what they mocked at!

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Faqad kaththaboo fasayateehim anbao ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Do they not see the earth, how We grow all kinds of noble things upon it?

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Have they not seen the earth, that how many honourable pairs We have created in it?

7.author: Arberry
7.text: What, have they not regarded the earth, how many therein We have caused to grow of every generous kind?

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Observe they not the earth, how much We make to grow therein of every fruitful kind?

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Do they not observe the earth, how much of every good kind We cause to grow therein?

7.author: Itani
7.text: Have they not seen the earth, and how many beautiful pairs We produced therein?

7.author: Maududi
7.text: Do they not look at the earth, how We caused a variety of fine vegetation to grow from it (in abundance)?

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Do they not observe the earth -- how much of every good kind We cause to grow therein

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Have they not seen the earth, how much of every fruitful kind We make to grow therein?

7.author: Qarai
7.text: Have they not regarded the earth, how many of every splendid kind [of vegetation] We have caused to grow in it?

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Have they not seen the earth, how many We caused to grow in it every generous kind?

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Have they not seen the earth in which We have made gracious plants grow?

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Do they not see the earth, how many of every noble kind We have caused to grow in it?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Do they not see the earth, and what beneficial kinds of things We have caused to grow in it?

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Do they not look at the earth,- how many noble things of all kinds We have produced therein?

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Awalam yaraw ila alardi kam anbatna feeha min kulli zawjin kareemin

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Verily there are signs in this; yet many of them do not believe.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Indeed in this is a sign; and most of them will not accept faith.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Verily therein is a sign; yet most of them are not believers.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Verily, in this is an Ayah (proof or sign), yet most of them (polytheists, pagans, etc., who do not believe in Resurrection) are not believers.

8.author: Itani
8.text: Surely in this is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Verily, in this is an Ayah, yet most of them are not believers.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Lo! herein is indeed a portent; yet most of them are not believers.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Surely, in this there is a sign yet most of them do not believe.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: In this there is, certainly, evidence (of the Truth). But most of them have no faith.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Most surely there is a sign in that, but most of them will not believe.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Surely in this there is a sign, yet most of them would not believe:

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Verily, in this is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Your Lord is surely mighty and merciful.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And indeed your Lord – indeed only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And verily thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And verily, your Lord! He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

9.author: Itani
9.text: Most surely, your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Verily Your Lord is Infinitely Mighty, Most Compassionate.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And verily your Lord, He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And lo! thy Lord! He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Your Lord, He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Your Lord is the Majestic and the All-merciful.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: truly, your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And verily, thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: When your Lord called upon Moses: "Go to the wicked people,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And (remember) when your Lord said to Moosa, “Go to the unjust people.”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: And when thy Lord called to Moses, 'Go to the people of the evildoers,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And recall what time thy Lord called unto Musa, saying: go thou unto the wrong-doing people.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And (remember) when your Lord called Musa (Moses) (saying): "Go to the people who are Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doing),

10.author: Itani
10.text: Your Lord called to Moses, “Go to the tyrannical people.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: (Recount to them about the time) when Your Lord called Moses: "Go to the wrong-doing people,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And (remember) when your Lord called Musa (saying): "Go to the people who are wrongdoers."

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And when thy Lord called Moses, saying: Go unto the wrongdoing folk,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: When your Lord called out to Moses: [saying,] ‘Go to those wrongdoing people,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: And when your Lord called to Moses, saying: 'Go to the harmdoing nation,

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], "Go to the wrongdoing people -

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: When Your Lord told Moses to go to the unjust people of the Pharaoh

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And when your Lord called out to Musa, saying: Go to the unjust people,

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: When your Lord called out to Moses, saying, "Go to the wrongdoing people,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Behold, thy Lord called Moses: "Go to the people of iniquity,-

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Waith nada rabbuka moosa ani iti alqawma alththalimeena

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear and take heed?"

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: “The nation of Firaun; will they not fear?”

11.author: Arberry
11.text: the people of Pharaoh; will they not be godfearing?'

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: The people of Fir'awn; fear they Me not?

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: The people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh). Will they not fear Allah and become righteous?"

11.author: Itani
11.text: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear?”

11.author: Maududi
11.text: the people of Pharaoh: do they have no fear?"

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: "The people of Fira`wn. Will they not have Taqwa"

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: The folk of Pharaoh. Will they not ward off (evil)?

11.author: Qarai
11.text: the people of Pharaoh. Will they not be wary [of Allah]?’

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: the nation of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Me'

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allah?"

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: and ask them, "Why do you not fear God?"

11.author: Shakir
11.text: The people of Firon: Will they not guard (against evil)?

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: the people of Pharaoh, will they not fear God?

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: "The people of the Pharaoh: will they not fear Allah?"

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: : He said: "O Lord, I fear that they will deny me.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: He said, “My Lord, I fear that they will deny me.”

12.author: Arberry
12.text: He said, 'My Lord, I fear they will cry me lies,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: He said: my Lord! verily I fear they shall belie me.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: He said: "My Lord! Verily, I fear that they will belie me,

12.author: Itani
12.text: He said, “My Lord, I fear they will reject me.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: He said: "My Lord! I fear that they will brand me a liar.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: He said: "My Lord! Verily, I fear that they will deny me,"

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: He said: My Lord! Lo! I fear that they will deny me,

12.author: Qarai
12.text: He said, ‘My Lord! I fear they will impugn me,

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: 'My Lord' he replied, 'I fear they will belie me

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: He said, "My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: He replied, "Lord, I am afraid that they will call me a liar.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: He said: O my Lord! surely I fear that they will reject me;

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: Moses replied, "My Lord, I fear they will reject me,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: He said: "O my Lord! I do fear that they will charge me with falsehood:

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Qala rabbi innee akhafu an yukaththibooni

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: My heart is constrained, my tongue falters, so delegate Aaron;

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: “I feel hesitant, and my tongue does not speak fast, therefore make Haroon also a Noble Messenger.”

13.author: Arberry
13.text: and my breast will be straitened, and my tongue will not be loosed; so send to Aaron.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And my breast straiteneth, and my tongue moveth not quickly: so send for Harun.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: "And my breast straitens, and my tongue expresses not well. So send for Harun (Aaron) (to come along with me).

13.author: Itani
13.text: And I become stressed, and my tongue is not fluent, so send Aaron too.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: My breast is constricted and my tongue is not fluent, so endow Messengership on Aaron.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: "And my breast straitens, and my tongue expresses not well. So send for Harun."

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And I shall be embarrassed, and my tongue will not speak plainly, therefor send for Aaron (to help me).

13.author: Qarai
13.text: and I will become upset and my tongue will fail me. So send for Aaron [to join me].

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: and my chest will become constricted and my tongue will not be loosed (in my speech), therefore, send to Aaron.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: I feel nervous and my tongue is not fluent, so send Aaron with me.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And by breast straitens, and my tongue is not eloquent, therefore send Thou to Haroun (to help me);

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and "my breast is straitened and my tongue is not fluent; so send Aaron as well;

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: "My breast will be straitened. And my speech may not go (smoothly): so send unto Aaron.

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Wayadeequ sadree wala yantaliqu lisanee faarsil ila haroona

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And they have a charge (of murder) against me. So I fear that they will kill me."

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: “And they have an accusation against me, so I fear that they may kill me.”

14.author: Arberry
14.text: They also have a sin against me, and I fear they will slay me.'

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And they have a crime against me, so I fear that they shall slay me.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: "And they have a charge of crime against me, and I fear they will kill me."

14.author: Itani
14.text: And they have a charge against me, so I fear they will kill me.”

14.author: Maududi
14.text: As for me, they hold the charge of a crime against me. I fear they will put me to death."

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: "And they have a charge of crime against me, and I fear they will kill me. "

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And they have a crime against me, so I fear that they will kill me.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: Besides, they have a charge against me, and I fear they will kill me.’

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: They hold a sin against me, and I fear that they will kill me'

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me."

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: They have charged me [with a crime] for which I am afraid they will kill me."

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And they have a crime against me, therefore I fear that they may slay me.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: besides, they accuse me of a crime, and I fear that they may put me to death."

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: "And (further), they have a charge of crime against me; and I fear they may slay me."

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Walahum AAalayya thanbun faakhafu an yaqtulooni

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: (It was) said: "By no means. Go with My signs, both of you. I am verily with you and I hear.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: He said, “Not like this (any more); both of you go with Our signs, We are with you, All Hearing.”

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Said He, 'No indeed; but go, both of you, with Our signs, and We assuredly shall be with you, listening.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: He said: by no means. so go ye twain with Our signs; verily We shall be with you listening.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Allah said: "Nay! Go you both with Our Signs. Verily! We shall be with you, listening.

15.author: Itani
15.text: He said, “No. Go, both of you, with Our proofs. We will be with you, listening.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: He said: "Certainly not! So go both of you with Our Signs. We shall be with you listening to everything.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: (Allah) said: "Nay! Go you both with Our signs. Verily, We shall be with you, listening."

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: He said: Nay, verily. So go ye twain with Our tokens. Lo! We shall be with you, Hearing.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: He said, ‘Certainly not! Let both of you go with Our signs: We will indeed be with you, hearing [everything].

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: He said: 'Never so, go both of you with Our signs; We shall be with you, listening

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: [Allah] said, "No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: The Lord said, "Have no fear, both of you go with Our miracles. We shall closely listen to you."

15.author: Shakir
15.text: He said: By no means, so go you both with Our signs; surely We are with you, hearing;

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: God said, "Indeed not; go both of you with Our signs, We shall be with you, listening [to your call].

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Allah said: "By no means! proceed then, both of you, with Our Signs; We are with you, and will listen (to your call).

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Qala kalla faithhaba biayatina inna maAAakum mustamiAAoona

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Go to the Pharaoh and tell him: 'We bring a message from the Lord of all the worlds

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: “Therefore approach Firaun then proclaim, ‘We both are Noble Messengers of the Lord Of The Creation.’

16.author: Arberry
16.text: So go you to Pharaoh, and say, "Verily, I am the Messenger of the Lord of all Being;

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: So go ye twain unto Fir'awn and say: verily we are the apostles of the Lord of the Worlds,

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: "And when you both come to Fir'aun (Pharaoh), say: 'We are the Messengers of the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists),

16.author: Itani
16.text: Go to Pharaoh, and say, ‘We are the Messengers of the Lord of the Worlds.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: Go, then, to Pharaoh and say to him: 'The Lord of the Universe has sent us

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: "And go both of you to Fira`wn, and say: `We are the Messengers of the Lord of Al-`Alamin,'

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And come together unto Pharaoh and say: Lo! we bear a message of the Lord of the Worlds,

16.author: Qarai
16.text: So approach Pharaoh and say, ‘‘We are indeed envoys of the Lord of the worlds

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: go both to Pharaoh and both of you say to him: 'We are (each) a Messenger from the Lord of all the Worlds.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: They came to the Pharaoh and said, "We are the Messengerss of the Lord of the Universe.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: Then come to Firon and say: Surely we are the apostles of the Lord of the worlds:

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Go to Pharaoh, both of you, and say, 'We are messengers from the Lord of the Worlds:

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: "So go forth, both of you, to Pharaoh, and say: 'We have been sent by the Lord and Cherisher of the worlds;

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Fatiya firAAawna faqoola inna rasoolu rabbi alAAalameena

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: That you should send the children of Israel with us.'

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: ‘That you let the Descendants of Israel go with us.’”

17.author: Arberry
17.text: so send forth with us the Children of Israel."

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Send with us the Children of Isra'il?

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: "So allow the Children of Israel to go with us.' "

17.author: Itani
17.text: Let the Children of Israel go with us.'“

17.author: Maududi
17.text: that you let the Children of Israel go with us.'"

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: `So allow the Children of Israel to go with us."'

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: (Saying): Let the Children of Israel go with us.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: that you let the Children of Israel leave with us.’’ ’

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Send forth with us the Children of Israel'

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: [Commanded to say], "Send with us the Children of Israel."'"

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Then send with us the children of Israel.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: let the Children of Israel go with us!'"

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: "'Send thou with us the Children of Israel.'"

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: (The Pharaoh) said: "Did we not bring you up as a child? And you lived a number of years of your life with us.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Said Firaun, “Did we do not raise you amongst us, as a child? And you spent many years of your life among us!”

18.author: Arberry
18.text: He said, 'Did we not raise thee amongst us as a child? Didst thou not tarry among us years of thy life?

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Fir'awn said: brought we not thee up amongst us as a child? And thou tarriedst amongst us for many years of thy life?

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: [Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said [to Musa (Moses)]: "Did we not bring you up among us as a child? And you did dwell many years of your life with us.

18.author: Itani
18.text: He said, “Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you stayed among us for many of your years?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: Pharaoh said: "Did we not bring you up among us when you were a child?

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: (Fir`awn) said (to Musa): "Did we not bring you up among us as a child And you did dwell many years of your life with us."

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: (Pharaoh) said (unto Moses): Did we not rear thee among us as a child? And thou didst dwell many years of thy life among us,

18.author: Qarai
18.text: He [i.e. Pharaoh] said, ‘Did we not rear you as a child among us, and did you not stay with us for years of your life?

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: He (Pharaoh) said (to Moses): 'Did We not bring you up when you were a child? And have you not spent years of your life amongst us?

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: [Pharaoh] said, "Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: The Pharaoh said, "Did we not bring you up in our home as an infant and did you not live with us for many years

18.author: Shakir
18.text: (Firon) said: Did we not bring you up as a child among us, and you tarried among us for (many) years of your life?

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: Pharaoh said to Moses, "Did we not bring you up among us as a child? And you spent several years of your life with us.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: (Pharaoh) said: "Did we not cherish thee as a child among us, and didst thou not stay in our midst many years of thy life?

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Qala alam nurabbika feena waleedan walabithta feena min AAumurika sineena

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: And you commited what you did, and you are ungrateful!"

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: “And you committed the deed of yours that you committed, and you were ungrateful.”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: And thou didst the deed thou didst, being one of the ungrateful!'

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And thou didst that thy deed which thou didst; and thou art of the ingrates.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: "And you did your deed, which you did (i.e. the crime of killing a man). And you are one of the ingrates."

19.author: Itani
19.text: And you committed that deed you committed, and you were ungrateful.”

19.author: Maududi
19.text: You spent many years of your life among us and then you committed that deed of yours. You are very ungrateful indeed."

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: "And you did your deed, which you did. While you were one of the ingrates."

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And thou didst that thy deed which thou didst, and thou wast one of the ingrates.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Then you committed that deed of yours, and you are an ingrate.’

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Yet you were ungrateful and have done the deed you did'

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful."

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: and you did the deed which you did. You are certainly ungrateful."

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And you did (that) deed of yours which you did, and you are one of the ungrateful.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Yet you committed the deed you did, surely you are one of the ingrates."

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: "And thou didst a deed of thine which (thou knowest) thou didst, and thou art an ungrateful (wretch)!"

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: WafaAAalta faAAlataka allatee faAAalta waanta mina alkafireena

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: (Moses) replied: "I did do that and I was in the wrong,

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Said Moosa, “I did that at a time when I was unaware of the consequences.” (In anger – See verse 28:15)

20.author: Arberry
20.text: Said he, 'Indeed I did it then, being one of those that stray;

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: He said: I did it then when I was mistaken.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: Musa (Moses) said: "I did it then, when I was an ignorant (as regards my Lord and His Message).

20.author: Itani
20.text: He said, “I did it then, when I was of those astray.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Moses replied: "I committed that act erringly.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: Musa said: "I did it then, when I was in error."

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: He said: I did it then, when I was of those who are astray.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: He said, ‘I did that when I was astray.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: He (Moses) replied: 'Indeed, I did that when I was among those who stray.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: [Moses] said, "I did it, then, while I was of those astray.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: Moses said, "I did do it and I made a mistake.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: He said: I did it then while I was of those unable to see the right course;

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Moses replied, "I did that when I was one of the misguided,

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Moses said: "I did it then, when I was in error.

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Qala faAAaltuha ithan waana mina alddalleena

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: So I ran away from you out of fear. But my Lord has given me wisdom, and made me an apostle.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: “I therefore went away from you as I feared you – so my Lord commanded me and appointed me as one of the Noble Messengers.”

21.author: Arberry
21.text: so I fled from you, fearing you. But my Lord gave me Judgment and made me one of the Envoys.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Then I fled from you when I feared you, and my Lord bestowed on me wisdom and made me one of the sent ones.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: "So I fled from you when I feared you. But my Lord has granted me Hukman (i.e. religious knowledge, right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood), and appointed me as one of the Messengers.

21.author: Itani
21.text: And I fled from you when I feared you; but my Lord gave me wisdom, and made me one of the messengers.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Then I fled for fear of you. Then my Lord bestowed wisdom and authority on me and made me one of the Messengers.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: "So, I fled from you when I feared you. But my Lord has granted me Hukm, and made me one of the Messengers."

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Then I fled from you when I feared you, and my Lord vouchsafed me a command and appointed me (of the number) of those sent (by Him).

21.author: Qarai
21.text: So I fled from you, as I was afraid of you. Then my Lord gave me sound judgement and made me one of the apostles.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: I fled from you because I feared you. But my Lord has given me judgment and made me one of the Messengers.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and prophethood and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Then I ran away from you in fear, but my Lord granted me the law and has appointed me as a Messenger.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: So I fled from you when I feared you, then my Lord granted me wisdom and made me of the apostles;

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: and I fled from you because I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and made me one of the messengers.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: "So I fled from you (all) when I feared you; but my Lord has (since) invested me with judgment (and wisdom) and appointed me as one of the messengers.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Fafarartu minkum lamma khiftukum fawahaba lee rabbee hukman wajaAAalanee mina almursaleena

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: But the favour you oblige me with is that you have enslaved the children of Israel."

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: “And is this some great reward for which you express favour upon me – that you have enslaved the Descendants of Israel?”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: That is a blessing thou reproachest me with, having enslaved the Children of Israel.'

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: And that favour wherewith thou didst oblige me was that thou hadst enslaved the Children of Isra'il?

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: "And this is the past favour with which you reproach me, that you have enslaved the Children of Israel."

22.author: Itani
22.text: Is that the favor you taunt me with, although you have enslaved the Children of Israel?”

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Now this is the favour that you tauntingly remind me of: that you enslaved the Children of Israel!"

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: "And this is the past favor with which you reproach me, -- that you have enslaved the Children of Israel."

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: And this is the past favour wherewith thou reproachest me: that thou hast enslaved the Children of Israel.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: That you have enslaved the Children of Israel—is that the favour with which you reproach me?’

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: Is this then the blessing with which you reproach me, that you have the Children of Israel for worshipers'

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: And this is the favor with which you oblige me: You have made the Israelites your slaves.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: And is it a favor of which you remind me that you have enslaved the children of Israel?

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: And this is the favour with which you taunt me -- that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: "And this is the favour with which thou dost reproach me,- that thou hast enslaved the Children of Israel!"

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Watilka niAAmatun tamunnuha AAalayya an AAabbadta banee israeela

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: The Pharaoh asked: "And what is the Lord of all the worlds?"

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Said Firaun, “And what is the Lord Of The Creation?

23.author: Arberry
23.text: Pharaoh said, 'And what is the Lord of all Being?'

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Fir'awn said. and what is the Lord of the worlds?

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "And what is the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)?"

23.author: Itani
23.text: Pharaoh said, “And what is the Lord of the Worlds?”

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Pharaoh said: "And who is this Lord of the Universe?"

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Fir`awn said: "And what is the Lord of Al-`Alamin"

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Pharaoh said: And what is the Lord of the Worlds?

23.author: Qarai
23.text: He said, ‘And what is ‘‘the Lord of all the worlds?’’ ’

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: Pharaoh said: 'And what is the Lord of the Worlds'

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Said Pharaoh, "And what is the Lord of the worlds?"

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: The Pharaoh asked, "Who is the Lord of the Universe?"

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Firon said: And what is the Lord of the worlds?

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: Pharaoh said, "What is this: Lord of the Universe?"

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Pharaoh said: "And what is the 'Lord and Cherisher of the worlds'?"

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: (Moses) said: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, if you can believe."

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Said Moosa, “Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them; if you believe.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: He said, 'The Lord of the heavens and earth, and what between them is, if you have faith.'

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: He said: Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is in-between, if ye seek to be convinced.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Musa (Moses) said: "Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, if you seek to be convinced with certainty."

24.author: Itani
24.text: He said, “The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and everything between them, if you are aware.”

24.author: Maududi
24.text: Moses answered: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth and of all that is between them, if you were only to believe."

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: (Musa) said: "The Lord of the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, if you seek to be convinced with certainty."

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: (Moses) said: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, if ye had but sure belief.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: He said, ‘The Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them,—should you have conviction.’

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: 'He' (Moses) replied: 'is the Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, if you believe'

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: [Moses] said, "The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced."

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: Moses replied, "The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, if you want to be certain".

24.author: Shakir
24.text: He said: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, if you would be sure.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: Moses said, "Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is between them, if only you would be convinced."

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: (Moses) said: "The Lord and Cherisher of the heavens and the earth, and all between,- if ye want to be quite sure."

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Qala rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma in kuntum mooqineena

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: (The Pharaoh) said to those around him: "Do you hear?"

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: Said Firaun to those around him, “Are you not listening with attention?”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Said he to those about him, 'Do you not hear?'

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Fir'awn said unto those around him: hear ye not?

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said to those around: "Do you not hear (what he says)?"

25.author: Itani
25.text: He said to those around him, “Do you not hear?”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Pharaoh said to those around him: "Do you hear (what he says)?"

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: (Fir`awn) said to those around: "Do you not hear (what he says)"

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: (Pharaoh) said unto those around him: Hear ye not?

25.author: Qarai
25.text: He said to those who were around him, ‘Don’t you hear?!’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: 'Do you not hear' said he (Pharaoh) to those around him.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, "Do you not hear?"

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: The Pharaoh said to the people around him, "Did you hear that?"

25.author: Shakir
25.text: (Firon) said to those around him: Do you not hear?

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Pharaoh said to those around him, "Did you hear?"

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: (Pharaoh) said to those around: "Did ye not listen (to what he says)?"

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: (Moses continued): "Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers before you."

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: Said Moosa, “Your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers preceding you.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: He said, 'Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers, the ancients.'

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: He said: your Lord and the Lord of your ancient fathers.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Musa (Moses) said: "Your Lord and the Lord of your ancient fathers!"

26.author: Itani
26.text: He said, “Your Lord and the Lord of your ancestors of old.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Moses said: "(He is) Your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers of yore."

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: (Musa) said: "Your Lord and the Lord of your ancient fathers!"

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: He said: Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: He said, ‘Your Lord, and the Lord of your forefathers!’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: He said, 'Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers, the ancients'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: [Moses] said, "Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers."

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Moses continued, "He is the Lord and the Lord of your forefathers."

26.author: Shakir
26.text: He said: Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers of old.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Moses went on, "He is your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers."

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: (Moses) said: "Your Lord and the Lord of your fathers from the beginning!"

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: (The Pharaoh) said (to his nobles): "Your apostle who has been sent to you, is certainly mad."

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Said Firaun, “This (Noble) Messenger of yours, who has been sent towards you, has no intelligence!”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Said he, 'Surely your Messenger who was sent to you is possessed!'

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Fir'awn said: verily your apostle who hath been sent unto you is mad.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "Verily, your Messenger who has been sent to you is a madman!"

27.author: Itani
27.text: He said, “This messenger of yours, who is sent to you, is crazy.”

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Pharaoh said to the audience: "This Messenger of yours who has been sent to you is simply mad."

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: (Fir`awn) said: "Verily, your Messenger who has been sent to you is a madman!"

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: (Pharaoh) said: Lo! your messenger who hath been sent unto you is indeed a madman!

27.author: Qarai
27.text: He said, ‘Indeed your messenger, who has been sent to you, is surely crazy!’

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: (Pharaoh) said: 'Surely, the Messenger who has been sent to you is mad!'

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: [Pharaoh] said, "Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad."

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: The Pharaoh said, "The Messengers who has been sent to you is certainly insane".

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Said he: Most surely your Apostle who is sent to you is mad.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: Pharaoh said, "This messenger who has been sent to you is surely possessed!"

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: (Pharaoh) said: "Truly your messenger who has been sent to you is a veritable madman!"

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Qala inna rasoolakumu allathee orsila ilaykum lamajnoonun

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: (Moses) said: "The Lord of the East and the Lord of the West and all that lies between them, if you have sense."

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: He said, “The Lord of the East and the West and all that is between them; if you have sense.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: He said, 'The Lord of the East and West, and what between them is, if you have understanding,'

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: He said: Lord of the east and the west and whatsoever is in-between, if ye understand.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: Musa (Moses) said: "Lord of the east and the west, and all that is between them, if you did but understand!"

28.author: Itani
28.text: He said, “Lord of the East and the West, and everything between them, if you understand.”

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Moses continued: "(He is) the Lord of the east and the west, and all between them. If you only had any understanding!"

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: (Musa) said: "Lord of the east and the west, and all that is between them, if you did but understand!"

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: He said: Lord of the East and the West and all that is between them, if ye did but understand.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: He said, ‘The Lord of the east and the west and whatever is between them—should you exercise your reason.’

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: 'He is the Lord of the East and the West' said he (Moses), 'and all that is between them, if you could understand'

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: [Moses] said, "Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason."

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Moses continued, "He is the Lord of the East and West and all that is between them, if only you would think".

28.author: Shakir
28.text: He said: The Lord of the east and the west and what is between them, if you understand.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: Moses said, "He is the Lord of the East and the West, and all that lies between them, if only you could understand."

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: (Moses) said: "Lord of the East and the West, and all between! if ye only had sense!"

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Qala rabbu almashriqi waalmaghribi wama baynahuma in kuntum taAAqiloona

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: (The Pharaoh) said: "If you took another god apart from me I will have you incarcerated."

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: Said Firaun, “If you ascribe any one else as a God other than me, I will surely imprison you.”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Said he, 'If thou takest a god other than me, I shall surely make thee one of the imprisoned.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Fir'awn said: if thou wilt take a god other than me, I shall surely place thee among the prisoners.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "If you choose an ilah (god) other than me, I will certainly put you among the prisoners."

29.author: Itani
29.text: He said, “If you accept any god other than me, I will make you a prisoner.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: Pharaoh said: "If you take any god other than me, I will certainly make you one of those (who are rotting) in prison."

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: He said: "If you choose a god other than me, I will certainly put you among the prisoners."

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: (Pharaoh) said: If thou choosest a god other than me, I assuredly shall place thee among the prisoners.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: He said, ‘If you take up any god other than me, I will surely make you a prisoner!’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: 'If you take any other god except myself' he (Pharaoh) replied, 'you shall be thrown into prison'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: [Pharaoh] said, "If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: Pharaoh said, "If you put forward any god other than me, I will surely put you in prison".

29.author: Shakir
29.text: Said he: If you will take a god besides me, I will most certainly make you one of the imprisoned.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: Pharaoh said, "If you take any deity other than myself, I will throw you into prison,"

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: (Pharaoh) said: "If thou dost put forward any god other than me, I will certainly put thee in prison!"

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Qala laini ittakhathta ilahan ghayree laajAAalannaka mina almasjooneena

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: (Moses) said: "Even though I have brought to you something convincing?"

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Said Moosa, “Even if I bring to you something clear?”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: He said, 'What, even though I brought thee something so manifest?'

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: He said: even though I bring unto thee something manifest?

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Musa (Moses) said: "Even if I bring you something manifest (and convincing)?"

30.author: Itani
30.text: He said, “What if I bring you something convincing?”

30.author: Maududi
30.text: Moses said: "Even if I were to bring a Clear Sign to you?"

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: He said: "Even if I bring you something manifest"

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: He said: Even though I show thee something plain?

30.author: Qarai
30.text: He said, ‘What if I bring you something [as an] unmistakable [proof]?’

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: 'What, even if I brought you something clear' said he (Moses).

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: [Moses] said, "Even if I brought you proof manifest?"

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: Moses asked, "What if I were to bring you clear proof (of the existence of God)?"

30.author: Shakir
30.text: He said: What! even if I bring to you something manifest?

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: and Moses asked, "Even if I show you a clear sign?"

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: (Moses) said: "Even if I showed you something clear (and) convincing?"

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: (The Pharaoh) said: "Then bring it, if you speak the truth."

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: Said Firaun, “Then bring it, if you are of the truthful.”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: Said he, 'Bring it then, if thou art of the truthful.'

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Fir'awn said: forth with it then, if thou art of the truth- tellers.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "Bring it forth then, if you are of the truthful!"

31.author: Itani
31.text: He said, “Bring it, if you are being truthful.”

31.author: Maududi
31.text: Pharaoh said: "Then bring it if you are truthful at all."

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: He said: "Bring it forth then, if you are of the truthful!"

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: (Pharaoh) said: Produce it then, if thou art of the truthful!

31.author: Qarai
31.text: He said, ‘Then bring it, should you be truthful.’

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: He (Pharaoh) replied: 'Show us your sign, if you are of the truthful'

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: [Pharaoh] said, "Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful."

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: The Pharaoh replied, "Bring it, if you are telling the truth."

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Said he: Bring it then, if you are of the truthful ones.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: Pharaoh said, "Show it then, if you are telling the truth!"

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: (Pharaoh) said: "Show it then, if thou tellest the truth!"

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Qala fati bihi in kunta mina alssadiqeena

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: So (Moses) cast his staff, and lo, it turned into a living serpent.

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: So Moosa put down his staff and it became a visible serpent.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: So he cast his staff, and behold, it was a serpent manifest.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: Then he cast down his staff, and lo! it was a serpent manifest.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: So [Musa (Moses)] threw his stick, and behold, it was a serpent, manifest.

32.author: Itani
32.text: So he cast his staff; and it was a serpent, plain to see.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: (No sooner had he said this than) Moses threw down his rod and behold, it was a veritable serpent,

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: So he threw his stick, and behold, it was a serpent, manifest.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: Then he flung down his staff and it became a serpent manifest,

32.author: Qarai
32.text: Thereat he threw down his staff, and behold, it was a manifest python.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: He cast down his staff and thereupon it was a clear serpent.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: Moses threw his staff and suddenly it became a serpent.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: So he cast down his rod, and lo! it was an obvious serpent,

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: So Moses threw down his staff and suddenly it appeared as a serpent, plainly visible.

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: So (Moses) threw his rod, and behold, it was a serpent, plain (for all to see)!

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Faalqa AAasahu faitha hiya thuAAbanun mubeenun

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: And he drew forth his hand, and lo, it looked white to the beholders.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And he drew forth his hand, thereupon it shone bright before the beholders.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: And he drew forth his hand, and lo, it was white to the beholders.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And he drew forth his hand, and lo! it was white unto the beholders.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to all beholders!

33.author: Itani
33.text: And he pulled his hand; and it was white, for all to see.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: and he drew his hand (out of his armpit) and lo! it had become a shining object to the beholders.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to all beholders!

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And he drew forth his hand and lo! it was white to the beholders.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: Then he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white and bright to the onlookers.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Then he drew out his hand, and it was luminous to the onlookers.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: Then he uncovered his hand and it was sheer white to the onlookers.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And he drew forth his hand, and lo! it appeared white to the onlookers.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: And he drew out his hand, and it appeared [shining] white to the beholders.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: And he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to all beholders!

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: WanazaAAa yadahu faitha hiya baydao lilnnathireena

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: The Pharaoh said to the chiefs around him: "He is certainly a clever magician.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: Said Firaun to the court members around him, “He is indeed an expert magician.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Said he to the Council about him, 'Surely this man is a cunning sorcerer

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Fir'awn said unto the chiefs around him: verily this is a magician knowing.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: [Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said to the chiefs around him: "Verily! This is indeed a well-versed sorcerer.

34.author: Itani
34.text: He said to the dignitaries around him, “This is a skilled magician.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: Pharaoh said to the nobles around him: "Surely this man is a skilled magician

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: He said to the chiefs around him: "Verily, this is indeed a well-versed sorcerer."

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: (Pharaoh) said unto the chiefs about him: Lo! this is verily a knowing wizard,

34.author: Qarai
34.text: He said to the elite [who stood] around him, ‘This is indeed an expert magician

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: 'This', said he (Pharaoh) to his Council, 'is a cunning sorcerer

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, "Indeed, this is a learned magician.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: The Pharaoh said to the people around him, "He is certainly a skillful magician.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: (Firon) said to the chiefs around him: Most surely this is a skillful magician,

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: Pharaoh said to the chiefs around him, "Surely this man is a skilful sorcerer.

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: (Pharaoh) said to the Chiefs around him: "This is indeed a sorcerer well-versed:

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Qala lilmalai hawlahu inna hatha lasahirun AAaleemun

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic. So, what do you bid?"

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: “He wishes to expel you out of your land by his magic; so what is your advice?”

35.author: Arberry
35.text: who desires to expel you from your land by his sorcery; what do you command?'

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: He would drive you out of your land through his magic; what then is it ye command?

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: "He wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery, then what is it your counsel, and what do you command?"

35.author: Itani
35.text: He intends to drive you out of your land with his magic, so what do you recommend?”

35.author: Maududi
35.text: who wants to drive you out of your land by his magic. Tell us, what do you advise us?"

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: "He wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery: what is it then that you command"

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Who would drive you out of your land by his magic. Now what counsel ye?

35.author: Qarai
35.text: who seeks to expel you from your land with his magic. So what do you advise?’

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: who seeks to expel you from your land by his sorcery. What is your counsel'

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?"

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: He wants to expel you from your land through his magic. What is your opinion?"

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Who desires to turn you out of your land with his magic; what is it then that you advise?

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Who wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery. Now what do you advise?"

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: "His plan is to get you out of your land by his sorcery; then what is it ye counsel?"

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Yureedu an yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihi famatha tamuroona

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: They said: "Put him and his brother off awhile, and send summoners to the cities to gather

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: They said, “Stop him and his brother, and send gatherers to the cities.”

36.author: Arberry
36.text: They said, 'Put him and his brother off a while, and send among the cities musterers,

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: They said: put off him and his brother, and send unto the cities callers.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: They said: "Put him off and his brother (for a while), and send callers to the cities

36.author: Itani
36.text: They said, “Delay him and his brother, and send recruiters to the cities.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: They said: "Detain him and his brother for a while and send forth heralds to the cities

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: They said: "Put him off and his brother (for a while), and send callers to the cities;"

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: They said: Put him off, (him) and his brother, and send into the cities summoners

36.author: Qarai
36.text: They said, ‘Put him and his brother off for a while, and send heralds to the cities

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: They replied: 'Put him and his brother off for a while, and send heralds to your cities

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: They said, "Hold him and his brother off for a while

36.author: Shakir
36.text: They said: Give him and his brother respite and send heralds into the cities

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: They said, "Let him and his brother wait a while, and send heralds into the cities,

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: They said: "Keep him and his brother in suspense (for a while), and dispatch to the Cities heralds to collect-

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Qaloo arjih waakhahu waibAAath fee almadaini hashireena

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: And bring the ablest magicians to you."

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: “To bring to you every expert great magician.”

37.author: Arberry
37.text: to bring thee every cunning sorcerer.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Thot they may bring to thee every magician knowing.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: "To bring up to you every well-versed sorcerer."

37.author: Itani
37.text: To bring you every experienced magician.”

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: "To bring up to you every well-versed sorcerer."

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Who shall bring unto thee every knowing wizard.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: to bring you every expert magician.’

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician."

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: and summon every skillful magician from all the cities."

37.author: Shakir
37.text: That they should bring to you every skillful magician.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: who shall bring to you every skilful sorcerer."

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: "And bring up to thee all (our) sorcerers well-versed."

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: So the magicians were assembled at a stated time and place on an appointed day.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: So the magicians were gathered at a set time on a day appointed.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: So the sorcerers were assembled for the appointed time of a fixed day.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: So the magicians were assembled at a set time on a day made known.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: So the sorcerers were assembled at a fixed time on a day appointed.

38.author: Itani
38.text: So the magicians were gathered for the appointment on a specified day.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: So the magicians were brought together on a particular day at a set time

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: So, the sorcerers were assembled at a fixed time on a day appointed.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: So the wizards were gathered together at a set time on a day appointed.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: So the magicians were gathered for the tryst of a known day,

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: The sorcerers were gathered at the appointed time on a wellknown day,

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: So all the magicians gathered together

38.author: Shakir
38.text: So the magicians were gathered together at the appointed time on the fixed day,

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: So the sorcerers were gathered on the appointed day

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: So the sorcerers were got together for the appointment of a day well-known,

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: And the people were also asked to assemble.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And it was said to the people, “Have you (also) gathered?”

39.author: Arberry
39.text: The people were asked, 'Will you assemble?

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And it was said unto the people: are ye going to assemble.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And it was said to the people: "Are you (too) going to assemble?

39.author: Itani
39.text: And it was said to the people, “Are you all gathered?

39.author: Maududi
39.text: and the people were told: "Will you join the assembly?

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And it was said to the people: "Are you (too) going to assemble"

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: And it was said unto the people: Are ye (also) gathering?

39.author: Qarai
39.text: and the people were told: ‘Will you all gather?!’

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: and the people were asked: 'Will you gather

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And it was said to the people, "Will you congregate

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And it was said to the people: Will you gather together?

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: and the people were told, "Will you also gather together,

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: And the people were told: "Are ye (now) assembled?-

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: (They said): "We may haply follow the magicians if they are victorious."

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: The people said, “Perhaps we may follow the magicians if they are victorious.”

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Haply we shall follow the sorcerers if it should be they are the victors.'

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Belike we may follow the magicians if they are the winners?

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: "That we may follow the sorcerers [who were on Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) religion of disbelief] if they are the winners."

40.author: Itani
40.text: That we may follow the magicians, if they are the winners.”

40.author: Maududi
40.text: We may perhaps follow the religion of the magicians if they triumph."

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: "That we may follow the sorcerers if they are the winners."

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: (They said): Aye, so that we may follow the wizards if they are the winners.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: ‘Maybe we will follow the magicians, if they are victors!’

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: in order that we shall follow the sorcerers if they are the victors'

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: and the people were asked, "Will you all be there so that we may follow the magicians if they become victorious?"

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Haply we may follow the magicians, if they are the vanquishers.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: so that we may follow the magicians, if they be the winners."

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: "That we may follow the sorcerers (in religion) if they win?"

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: LaAAallana nattabiAAu alssaharata in kanoo humu alghalibeena

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: When the magicians arrived, they said to the Pharaoh: "Is there a reward for us if we are victorious?" --

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: So when the magicians came, they said to Firaun, “Will we get some reward if we are victorious?”

41.author: Arberry
41.text: Then, when the sorcerers came, they said to Pharaoh, 'Shall we indeed have a wage, if we should be the victors?'

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: Then when the magicians came, they said unto Fir'awn: will there surely be a big hire for us if we are the winners?

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: So when the sorcerers arrived, they said to Fir'aun (Pharaoh): "Will there surely be a reward for us if we are the winners?"

41.author: Itani
41.text: When the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, “Is there a reward for us, if we are the winners?”

41.author: Maududi
41.text: When the magicians came forth (for the encounter) they said to Pharaoh: "Is there a reward for us if we triumph?"

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: So, when the sorcerers arrived, they said to Fir`awn: "Will there surely be a reward for us if we are the winners"

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And when the wizards came they said unto Pharaoh: Will there surely be a reward for us if we are the winners?

41.author: Qarai
41.text: So when the magicians came, they said to Pharaoh, ‘Shall we have a reward if we were to be the victors?’

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Then, when the sorcerers came to Pharaoh, they said: 'Shall we receive a wage if we win'

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: When the magicians came, they asked the Pharaoh, "Will there be any reward for us if we win?"

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And when the magicians came, they said to Firon: Shall we get a reward if we are the vanquishers?

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: When the magicians came, they asked Pharaoh, "Shall we have a reward, if we are the winners?"

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: So when the sorcerers arrived, they said to Pharaoh: "Of course - shall we have a (suitable) reward if we win?
