41.text: Falamma jaa alssaharatu qaloo lifirAAawna ainna lana laajran in kunna nahnu alghalibeena

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: "Yes," he replied. "You 'will be among the honoured."

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: He said, “Yes, and you will then become close to me.”

42.author: Arberry
42.text: He said, 'Yes indeed; and you shall then be among the near-stationed.'

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: He said: yea; and ye shall verily then be of those brought nigh.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: He said: "Yes, and you shall then verily be of those brought near (to myself)."

42.author: Itani
42.text: He said, “Yes, and you will be among those favored.”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: He said: "Yes, you will then become those near-stationed to me."

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: He said: "Yes, and you shall then verily be of those brought near (to myself)."

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: He said: Aye, and ye will then surely be of those brought near (to me).

42.author: Qarai
42.text: He said, ‘Of course; and you will be among members of my inner circle.’

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: 'Yes, indeed' he answered, 'and you shall become among those who are near stationed'

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: He replied, "You will then be my closest associates".

42.author: Shakir
42.text: He said: Yes, and surely you will then be of those who are made near.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: He replied, "Certainly, in that case you will join my inner circle."

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: He said: "Yea, (and more),- for ye shall in that case be (raised to posts) nearest (to my person)."

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Qala naAAam wainnakum ithan lamina almuqarrabeena

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: Moses said to them: "Cast what you have to cast."

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Moosa said to them, “Cast whatever you intend to cast.”

43.author: Arberry
43.text: Moses said to them, 'Cast you down what you will cast.'

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Musa said unto them: cast whatsoever ye have to cast.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: Musa (Moses) said to them: "Throw what you are going to throw!"

43.author: Itani
43.text: Moses said to them, “Present what you intend to present.”

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Moses said to them: "Throw down whatever you wish to throw."

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: Musa said to them: "Throw what you are going to throw!"

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Moses said unto them: Throw what ye are going to throw!

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Moses said to them, ‘Throw down whatever you have to throw!’

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Moses said to them: 'Cast down what you cast'

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: (Moses) asked the magicians, "Cast down what you want to".

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Musa said to them: Cast what you are going to cast.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Moses said to the magicians, "Throw down whatever you are going to throw."

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Moses said to them: "Throw ye - that which ye are about to throw!"

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Qala lahum moosa alqoo ma antum mulqoona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: So they cast their ropes and rods, and said: "By the glory of Pharaoh, we shall be victorious."

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: So they threw down their ropes and their staves and exclaimed, “By the honour of Firaun, indeed victory is ours!”

44.author: Arberry
44.text: So they cast their ropes and their staffs, and said, 'By the might of Pharaoh we shall be the victors.'

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Then they cast their cords and their staves, and said: by the might of Fir'awn, verily we! we shall be the winners.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: So they threw their ropes and their sticks, and said: "By the might of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), it is we who will certainly win!"

44.author: Itani
44.text: So they threw their ropes and their sticks, and said, “By the majesty of Pharaoh, we will be the winners.”

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Thereupon they threw down their ropes and their rods, and said: "By the glory of Pharaoh, we shall prevail."

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: So, they threw their ropes and their sticks, and said: "By the might of Fir`awn, it is we who will certainly win!"

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Then they threw down their cords and their staves and said: By Pharaoh's might, lo! we verily are the winners.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: So they threw down their sticks and ropes, and said, ‘By the might of Pharaoh, we shall surely be victorious!’

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: So they cast their ropes and staffs, saying: 'By Pharaoh's might, we shall be the victors'

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: So they cast down their ropes and staffs saying, "By the honor of the Pharaoh we shall certainly become the winners".

44.author: Shakir
44.text: So they cast down their cords and their rods and said: By Firon's power, we shall most surely be victorious.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: So they cast down their ropes and staffs, and said, "By Pharaoh's honour, we shall surely win."

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: So they threw their ropes and their rods, and said: "By the might of Pharaoh, it is we who will certainly win!"

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Faalqaw hibalahum waAAisiyyahum waqaloo biAAizzati firAAawna inna lanahnu alghaliboona

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Then Moses threw down his staff, and lo, it swallowed up their conjurations.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: Therefore Moosa put forth his staff – so it immediately began swallowing all their fabrications.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Then Moses cast his staff and lo, it forthwith swallowed up their lying invention;

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Then Musa cast down his staff, and lo! it swallowed up that which they had devised.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: Then Musa (Moses) threw his stick, and behold, it swallowed up all the falsehoods which they showed!

45.author: Itani
45.text: Then Moses threw his staff, and behold, it began swallowing their trickery.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Thereafter, Moses threw down his rod and behold, it went about swallowing up all the false devices they had contrived.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Then Musa threw his stick, and behold, it swallowed up all that they falsely showed!

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Then Moses threw his staff and lo! it swallowed that which they did falsely show.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Thereat Moses threw down his staff, and behold, it was swallowing what they had faked.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Then Moses cast down his staff and it swallowed up their lying invention,

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: Then Moses cast down his staff and suddenly it swallowed up what they had falsely invented.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Then Musa cast down his staff and lo! it swallowed up the lies they told.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Then Moses threw down his staff, and it swallowed up all that they had conjured into being.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Then Moses threw his rod, when, behold, it straightway swallows up all the falsehoods which they fake!

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Faalqa moosa AAasahu faitha hiya talqafu ma yafikoona

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Then the magicians fell prostrating in adoration,

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: The magicians therefore fell down prostrate.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: so the sorcerers were cast down, bowing themselves.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: Then the magicians flung themselves prostrate.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And the sorcerers fell down prostrate.

46.author: Itani
46.text: And the magicians fell down prostrating.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: Thereupon the magicians fell down in prostration,

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And the sorcerers fell down prostrate.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And the wizards were flung prostrate,

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Thereat the magicians fell down prostrating.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: so the sorcerers were cast down, prostrating themselves,

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allah].

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: The magicians fell down in adoration

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And the magicians were thrown down prostrate;

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: Then did the sorcerers fall down, prostrate in adoration,

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: And said: "We (affirm and) believe in the Lord of all the worlds,

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: They said, “We have accepted faith in the Lord Of The Creation.”

47.author: Arberry
47.text: They said, 'We believe in the Lord of all Being,

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: They said: we believe in the Lord of the worlds.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: Saying: "We believe in the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

47.author: Itani
47.text: They said, “We have believed in the Lord of the Worlds.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: saying: "We (now) believe in the Lord of the Universe,

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: Saying: "We believe in the Lord of Al-`Alamin."

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Crying: We believe in the Lord of the Worlds,

47.author: Qarai
47.text: They said, ‘We believe in the Lord of all the worlds,

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: saying: 'We believe in the Lord of the Worlds,

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: saying, "We believe in the Lord of the Universe

47.author: Shakir
47.text: They said: We believe in the Lord of the worlds:

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: saying, "We believe in the Lord of the Worlds,

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Saying: "We believe in the Lord of the Worlds,

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."

48.author: Arberry
48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.'

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: The Lord of Musa and Harun.

48.author: Itani
48.text: The Lord of Moses and Aaron.”

48.author: Maududi
48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron."

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: The Lord of Moses and Aaron.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron.’

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron'

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: and the Lord of Moses and Aaron."

48.author: Shakir
48.text: The Lord of Musa and Haroun.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: the Lord of Moses and Aaron."

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Said (the Pharaoh): "You have come to believe in him before I gave you leave. He is surely your chief, who taught you magic. You will soon come to know: I will have your hands and feet cut off on alternate sides, and have every one of you crucified."

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: Said Firaun, “You accepted faith in him before I permitted you! He is indeed your leader who taught you magic; so now you will come to know; I swear, I will certainly cut off your hands and your feet from alternate sides, and crucify all of you.”

49.author: Arberry
49.text: Said Pharaoh, 'You have believed him before I gave you leave. Why, he is the chief of you, the same that taught you sorcery; now you shall know! I shall assuredly cut off alternately your hands and feet, then I shall crucify you all together.'

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: Fir'awn said: ye believed in him ere I gave you leave. Verily he is your chief who hath taught you magic, so ye shall surely come to know. Surely I shall cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides and surely I shall crucify you all.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: [Fir'aun (Pharaoh)] said: "You have believed in him before I give you leave. Surely, he indeed is your chief, who has taught you magic! So verily, you shall come to know. Verily, I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you all."

49.author: Itani
49.text: He said, “Did you believe in Him before I have given you permission? He must be your chief, who taught you magic. You will soon know. I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you all.”

49.author: Maududi
49.text: Pharaoh said: "You accepted the word of Moses even before I granted you the leave to do so. Surely he is your chief who has taught you magic. Soon shall you come to know. I shall cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides and shall crucify all of you."

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: He said: "You have believed in him before I give you leave. Surely, he indeed is your chief, who has taught you magic! So verily, you shall come to know. Verily, I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will crucify you all."

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: (Pharaoh) said: Ye put your faith in him before I give you leave. Lo! he doubtless is your chief who taught you magic! But verily ye shall come to know. Verily I will cut off your hands and your feet alternately, and verily I will crucify you every one.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: He [i.e. Pharaoh] said, ‘Do you profess faith in Him before I should permit you? He is indeed your chief who has taught you magic! Soon you will know! I will cut off your hands and feet from opposite sides, and I will crucify you all.’

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: He (Pharaoh) said: 'You have believed him before I have given you permission. He is the chief of you who has taught you sorcery. But you shall know. I will surely cut off on opposite sides a hand and a foot, and crucify you all.'

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed Moses before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all."

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: The Pharaoh said, "You believed without my permission? He seems to be your chief who has taught you magic. But you will soon know (the result of what you have done).

49.author: Shakir
49.text: Said he: You believe in him before I give you permission; most surely he is the chief of you who taught you the magic, so you shall know: certainly I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and certainly I will crucify you all.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Pharaoh said, "Have you come to believe in him, before I have given you permission? He is surely your master who has taught you magic. But you shall see. I will cut off your hands and feet on alternate sides and crucify you all."

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Said (Pharaoh): "Believe ye in Him before I give you permission? surely he is your leader, who has taught you sorcery! but soon shall ye know! Be sure I will cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will cause you all to die on the cross!"

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Qala amantum lahu qabla an athana lakum innahu lakabeerukumu allathee AAallamakumu alssihra falasawfa taAAlamoona laoqattiAAanna aydiyakum waarjulakum min khilafin walaosallibannakum ajmaAAeena

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: They said: "There will be no harm. We shall return to our Lord.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: They said, “No harm (in it), we will return to our Lord.”

50.author: Arberry
50.text: They said, 'There is no harm; surely unto our Lord we are turning.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: They said: no harm! verily unto our Lord we are to return.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: They said: "No harm! Surely, to our Lord (Allah) we are to return;

50.author: Itani
50.text: They said, “No problem. To our Lord we will return.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: They said: "We do not care, for we are bound to return to our Lord,

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: They said: "No harm! Surely, to our Lord we are to return."

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: They said: It is no hurt, for lo! unto our Lord we shall return.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: They said, ‘[There is] no harm [in that]! Indeed, we shall return to our Lord.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: 'There is no harm' they replied, 'for surely to our Lord we are turning.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: I shall certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides and crucify you all together."

50.author: Shakir
50.text: They said: No harm; surely to our Lord we go back;

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: They said, "There is no harm. To our Lord we shall return.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: They said: "No matter! for us, we shall but return to our Lord!

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Qaloo la dayra inna ila rabbina munqaliboona

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: We certainly hope our Lord will forgive our sins as we are the first to believe."

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: “We hope that our Lord will forgive us our mistakes, as we are the first to believe.”

51.author: Arberry
51.text: We are eager that our Lord should forgive us our offences, for that we are the first of the believers.'

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: Verily we long that our Lord shall forgive us our faults because we have been the first of the believers

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: "Verily! We really hope that our Lord will forgive us our sins, as we are the first of the believers [in Musa (Moses) and in the Monotheism which he has brought from Allah]."

51.author: Itani
51.text: We are eager for our Lord to forgive us our sins, since we are the first of the believers.”

51.author: Maududi
51.text: and we surely expect that Our Lord will forgive us our sins for we are the first ones to believe."

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: "Verily, we really hope that our Lord will forgive us our sins, as we are the first of the believers."

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: Lo! we ardently hope that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we are the first of the believers.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Indeed we hope our Lord will forgive us our offences for being the first to believe.’

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: We are eager that Our Lord should forgive us our offenses, for we are the first of the believers'

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers."

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: They said, "It does not matter. We shall be returning to our Lord.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: Surely we hope that our Lord will forgive us our wrongs because we are the first of the believers.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: We hope our Lord will forgive us, as we are the first of the believers."

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: "Only, our desire is that our Lord will forgive us our faults, that we may become foremost among the believers!"

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Inna natmaAAu an yaghfira lana rabbuna khatayana an kunna awwala almumineena

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: We said to Moses: "Travel by night with My creatures. You will surely be pursued."

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: And We sent the divine revelation to Moosa that, “Journey along with My bondmen within the night, for you will be pursued.”

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Also We revealed unto Moses, 'Go with My servants by night; surely you will be followed.'

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: And We revealed unto Musa saying: go by night with My bondmen verily ye shall be pursued.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: And We inspired Musa (Moses), saying: "Take away My slaves by night, verily, you will be pursued."

52.author: Itani
52.text: And We inspired Moses: “Travel with My servants by night. You will be followed.”

52.author: Maududi
52.text: We revealed to Moses: "Set forth with My servants by night for you will be pursued."

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: And We revealed to Musa, saying: "Depart by night with My servants, verily, you will be pursued."

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: And We inspired Moses, saying: Take away My slaves by night, for ye will be pursued.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: Then We revealed to Moses, [saying],‘Set out with My servants at night, for you will be pursued.’

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Also, We revealed to Moses, saying: 'Go with My worshipers by night, for you will be followed'

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: And We inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued."

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: We hope that our Lord will forgive us for our sins; we were not believers at first."

52.author: Shakir
52.text: And We revealed to Musa, saying: Go away with My servants travelling by night, surely you will be pursued.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: Then We revealed Our will to Moses, saying, "Set forth with My servants, in the night, for you will surely be pursued."

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: By inspiration we told Moses: "Travel by night with my servants; for surely ye shall be pursued."

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Waawhayna ila moosa an asri biAAibadee innakum muttabaAAoona

53.text: Then the Pharaoh sent announcers to the cities,

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: So Firaun sent gatherers into the cities.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities musterers:

53.author: Daryabadi

53.author: Hilali & Khan

53.author: Maududi

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: Then Fir`awn sent callers to (all) the cities.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers

53.author: Shakir
53.text: So Firon sent heralds into the cities;

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: Then Pharaoh sent heralds to (all) the Cities,

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: (Proclaiming): "Surely they are a small band (of fugitives).

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: They announced “These people are a small group.”

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: Verily these are but a band small.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: (Saying): "Verily! These indeed are but a small band.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: saying: "These (Israelites) are only a small band of people

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: (Saying): "Verily, these indeed are but a small band."

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: (Who said): Lo! these indeed are but a little troop,

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: [And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: The Pharaoh sent word to all the cities saying,

54.author: Shakir
54.text: Most surely these are a small company;

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: "These," they said, "are only a small band,

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: “And indeed they have angered all of us.”

55.author: Arberry
55.text: and indeed they are enraging us;

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: And verily they have enraged US.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: "And verily, they have done what has enraged us;

55.author: Maududi
55.text: who have certainly provoked our wrath.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: "And verily, they have done what has enraged us."

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: And lo! they are offenders against us.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: And indeed, they are enraging us,

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: "There is a small group of people

55.author: Shakir
55.text: And most surely they have enraged us;

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: "And they are raging furiously against us;

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: But we are a fully accounted army."

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: “And indeed we are an alert army.”

56.author: Arberry
56.text: and we are a host on our guard.'

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: And verily we are host well-provided.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: "But we are host all assembled, amply fore-warned."

56.author: Itani
56.text: But we are a vigilant multitude.”

56.author: Maududi
56.text: But we are a numerous host, ever on guard."

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: "But we are a host all assembled, amply forewarned."

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: And lo! we are a ready host.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: and we are alert and fully prepared.’

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: and we are a host on our guard'

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: And indeed, we are a cautious society... "

56.author: Shakir
56.text: And most surely we are a vigilant multitude.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: we are a large and watchful force."

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: "But we are a multitude amply fore-warned."

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: So We made them leave the gardens and springs of water,

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: We therefore got them out from the gardens and water springs.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: So We expelled them from gardens and fountains,

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: Then We drave them forth from gardens and springs.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: So, We expelled them from gardens and springs,

57.author: Itani
57.text: So We drove them out of gardens and springs.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: Thus did We drive them out of their gardens and springs

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: So, We expelled them from gardens and springs,

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: Thus did We take them away from gardens and watersprings,

57.author: Qarai
57.text: So We took them out of gardens and springs,

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: As such We expelled them from their gardens and their fountains,

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: So We removed them from gardens and springs

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: We are warning all of you about them."

57.author: Shakir
57.text: So We turned them out of gardens and springs,

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: So We made them leave their gardens and springs,

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: So We expelled them from gardens, springs,

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And from treasures and nice houses.

58.author: Arberry
58.text: and treasures and a noble station;

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And treasures and a station noble.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: Treasures, and every kind of honourable place.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: and their treasures and excellent dwellings.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: Treasures, and every kind of honorable place.

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: And treasures and a fair estate.

58.author: Qarai
58.text: and [made them leave behind] treasures and stately homes.

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: their treasures and a noble station.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: We deprived them (the unbelievers) of gardens, springs,

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: their treasures and their noble dwellings --

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: Treasures, and every kind of honourable position;

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: Thus it was; and We made the children of Israel possess them.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: So it was; and We made the Descendants of Israel its inheritors.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: even so, and We bequeathed them upon the Children of Israel.

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Even so. And We caused the Children of Isra'il to inherit them.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Thus [We turned them (Pharaoh's people) out] and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit them.

59.author: Itani
59.text: So it was. And We made the Children of Israel inherit them.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: This happened with them; (but on the other hand), We enabled the Children of Israel to inherit those bounties.

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Thus and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit them.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Thus (were those things taken from them) and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit them.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: So it was; and We bequeathed them to the Children of Israel.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: As such we gave it to the Children of Israel.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: Thus. And We caused to inherit it the Children of Israel.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: Even so. And We gave them as a heritage to the children of Israel.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: and We made the Children of Israel inheritors of these bounties.

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: Thus it was, but We made the Children of Israel inheritors of such things.

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: But they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: So the Firaun’s people followed them at sunrise.

60.author: Arberry
60.text: Then they followed them at the sunrise;

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: Then they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: So they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Itani
60.text: And they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: At sunrise they set off in pursuit of them

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: So, they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: And they overtook them at sunrise.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: Then they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: So they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: Thus we let the Israelites inherit them all.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: Then they pursued them at sunrise.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: Pharaoh and his people pursued them at sunrise,

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: So they pursued them at sunrise.

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: When the two forces drew within seeing distance of each other, the people of Moses said: "We shall certainly be overtaken."

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: And when the two groups saw each other, those with Moosa said, “They have caught us.”

61.author: Arberry
61.text: and, when the two hosts sighted each other, the companions of Moses said, 'We are overtaken!'

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: And when the two parties saw each other, the companions of Musa said: verily we are overtaken.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: And when the two hosts saw each other, the companions of Musa (Moses) said: "We are sure to be overtaken."

61.author: Itani
61.text: When the two groups sighted each other, the followers of Moses said, “We are being overtaken.”

61.author: Maududi
61.text: and when the groups came face to face, the companions of Moses cried out: "We are overtaken!"

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: And when the two hosts saw each other, the companions of Musa said: "We are sure to be overtaken."

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: And when the two hosts saw each other, those with Moses said: Lo! we are indeed caught.

61.author: Qarai
61.text: When the two hosts sighted each other, the companions of Moses said, ‘Indeed we have been caught up.’

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: And when the two hosts came in view of each other, Moses' companions said: 'We have been reached'

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: And when the two companies saw one another, the companions of Moses said, "Indeed, we are to be overtaken!"

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: The people of the Pharaoh pursued them at sunrise.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: So when the two hosts saw each other, the companions of Musa cried out: Most surely we are being overtaken.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: and when the two groups saw each other, Moses' companions said, "We are sure to be overtaken."

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: And when the two bodies saw each other, the people of Moses said: "We are sure to be overtaken."

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Falamma taraa aljamAAani qala ashabu moosa inna lamudrakoona

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: Moses replied: "By no means. My Lord is with me. He will show me the way."

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: Said Moosa, “Never! Indeed my Lord is with me, He will now show me the way.”

62.author: Arberry
62.text: Said he, 'No indeed; surely my Lord is with me; He will guide me.'

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: Musa said: by no means; verily with me is my Lord; He shall guide me.

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: [Musa (Moses)] said: "Nay, verily! With me is my Lord, He will guide me."

62.author: Itani
62.text: He said, “No; my Lord is with me, He will guide me.”

62.author: Maududi
62.text: Moses said: "Certainly not. My Lord is with me; He will direct me."

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: (Musa) said: "Nay, verily with me is my Lord. He will guide me."

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: He said: Nay, verily! for lo! my Lord is with me. He will guide me.

62.author: Qarai
62.text: He said, ‘Certainly not! Indeed my Lord is with me. He will guide me.’

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: 'No, indeed' he replied, 'my Lord is with me and He will guide me'

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: [Moses] said, "No! Indeed, with me is my Lord; He will guide me."

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: When the two groups came close to each other, the companions of Moses said, "We will be caught".

62.author: Shakir
62.text: He said: By no means; surely my Lord is with me: He will show me a way out.

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: Moses replied, "No, My Lord is with me, and He will guide me."

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: (Moses) said: "By no means! my Lord is with me! Soon will He guide me!"

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Qala kalla inna maAAiya rabbee sayahdeeni

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: We commanded Moses: "Smite the sea with your staff." And it parted, and every parting was like a lofty mountain.

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: So We sent the divine revelation to Moosa that, “Strike the sea with your staff”; thereupon the sea parted; so each part was like a huge mountain.

63.author: Arberry
63.text: Then We revealed to Moses, 'Strike with thy staff the sea'; and it clave, and each part was as a mighty mount.

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: Then We revealed unto Musa, saying: smite thou the sea with thy staff. So it became separated, and each part was like unto a cliff mighty.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: Then We inspired Musa (Moses) (saying): "Strike the sea with your stick." And it parted, and each separate part (of that sea water) became like the huge, firm mass of a mountain.

63.author: Itani
63.text: We inspired Moses: “Strike the sea with your staff.” Whereupon it parted, and each part was like a huge hill.

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Then We revealed to Moses, (commanding him): "Strike the sea with your rod." Thereupon the sea split up, and then each became like the mass of a huge mount.

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: Then We revealed to Musa (saying): "Strike the sea with your stick." And it parted, and each separate part became like huge mountain.

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Then We inspired Moses, saying: Smite the sea with thy staff. And it parted, and each part was as a mountain vast.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: Thereupon We revealed to Moses: ‘Strike the sea with your staff!’ Whereupon it parted, and each part was as if it were a great mountain.

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: Then We revealed to Moses: 'Strike the sea with your staff', so it divided and each part was as a mighty mount.

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: Then We inspired to Moses, "Strike with your staff the sea," and it parted, and each portion was like a great towering mountain.

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: Moses said, "Certainly not. My Lord is with me and He will certainly guide me."

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Then We revealed to Musa: Strike the sea with your staff. So it had cloven asunder, and each part was like a huge mound.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: Then We bade Moses strike the sea with his staff. And it parted, and each part was like a huge mountain.

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Then We told Moses by inspiration: "Strike the sea with thy rod." So it divided, and each separate part became like the huge, firm mass of a mountain.

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Faawhayna ila moosa ani idrib biAAasaka albahra fainfalaqa fakana kullu firqin kaalttawdi alAAatheemi

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: Then We brought the others to that place.

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: And We brought the others close to that place.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: And there We brought the others on,

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: And We brought near thither the others.

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: Then We brought near the others [Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) party] to that place.

64.author: Itani
64.text: And there We brought the others near.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: We also brought the other party close to the same spot,

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: Then We brought near the others to that place.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Then brought We near the others to that place.

64.author: Qarai
64.text: There, We brought the others near,

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: And there We brought the others on,

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: And We advanced thereto the pursuers.

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: We sent a revelation to Moses saying, "Strike the sea with your staff." The sea was rent asunder and each side stood high up like a huge mountain.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: And We brought near, there, the others.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: In the meantime We made the others approach that place.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: And We made the other party approach thither.

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: We delivered Moses and every one with him,

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: And We saved Moosa and all those with him.

65.author: Arberry
65.text: and We delivered Moses and those with him all together;

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: And We delivered Musa and those with him together.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: And We saved Musa (Moses) and all those with him.

65.author: Itani
65.text: And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: and We delivered Moses and his companions, all of them,

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: And We saved Musa and all those with him.

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: And We saved Moses and those with him, every one;

65.author: Qarai
65.text: and We delivered Moses and all those who were with him.

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: and We saved Moses and those who were with him together,

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: Then We brought the two parties closer.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: And We saved Musa and those with him, all of them.

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: We delivered Moses and all those who were with him,

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: We delivered Moses and all who were with him;

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: We saved Moses and all the people with him

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: Verily there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: Indeed in this is surely a sign, and most of them were not Muslims.

67.author: Arberry
67.text: Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: Verily herein is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: Verily! In this is indeed a sign (or a proof), yet most of them are not believers.

67.author: Itani
67.text: In that there is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: Lo! herein is indeed a portent, yet most of them are not believers.

67.author: Qarai
67.text: There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them do not have faith.

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: Surely, in that there is a sign; yet most of them do not believe.

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

67.author: Shakir
67.text: Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: Surely in that there is a sign; yet most of them do not believe:

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: Verily in this is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: Indeed your Lord is mighty and merciful.

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: And indeed your Lord – only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

68.author: Arberry
68.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: And verily thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: And verily, your Lord! He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

68.author: Itani
68.text: Surely, your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

68.author: Maududi
68.text: Verily your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Ever Compassionate.

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: And verily your Lord, He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: And lo, thy Lord! He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: Surely, your Lord is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: In this there was certainly evidence (of the Truth), but most of them did not have any faith.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: truly, your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: Relate the news of Abraham to them.

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: And recite to them the news of Ibrahim.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: And recite to them the tiding of Abraham

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: And recite unto them the story of Ibrahim.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: And recite to them the story of Ibrahim (Abraham).

69.author: Itani
69.text: And relate to them the story of Abraham.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: And recount to them the story of Abraham:

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: And recite to them the story of Ibrahim.

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: Recite unto them the story of Abraham:

69.author: Qarai
69.text: Relate to them the account of Abraham

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: And recite to them the news of Abraham.

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: And recite to them the news of Abraham,

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: Your Lord is certainly Majestic and All-merciful.

69.author: Shakir
69.text: And recite to them the story of Ibrahim.

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: Tell them the story of Abraham,

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: And rehearse to them (something of) Abraham's story.

70.author: Ahmed Ali

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: When he said to his father and his people, “What do you worship?”

70.author: Arberry
70.text: when he said to his father and his people, 'What do you serve?'

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: When he said unto his father and his people: what worship ye?

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: When he said to his father and his people: "What do you worship?"

70.author: Itani
70.text: When he said to his father and his people, “What do you worship?”

70.author: Maududi
70.text: when he asked his father and his people: "What do you worship?"

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: When he said to his father and his people: "What do you worship"

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: When he said unto his father and his folk: What worship ye?

70.author: Qarai
70.text: when he said to his father and his people, ‘What is it that you are worshiping?!’

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: He said to his father and to his nation: 'What do you worship'

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: When he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?"

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: Tell them the story of Abraham,

70.author: Shakir

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: Behold, he said to his father and his people: "What worship ye?"

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: They replied: "We worship idols and are devoted to them."

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: They said, “We worship idols, and we keep squatting in seclusion before them.”

71.author: Arberry
71.text: They said, 'We serve idols, and continue cleaving to them.

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: They said: we worship idols, and we are unto them ever devoted

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: They said: "We worship idols, and to them we are ever devoted."

71.author: Itani
71.text: They said, “We worship idols, and we remain devoted to them.”

71.author: Maududi
71.text: They answered: "There are some idols that we worship and are devoted to them with constancy."

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: They said: "We worship idols, and to them we are ever devoted."

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: They said: We worship idols, and are ever devoted unto them.

71.author: Qarai
71.text: They said, ‘We worship idols, and are constant in our devotion to them.’

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: They replied: 'We worship idols and continue cleaving to them'

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: They said, "We worship idols and remain to them devoted."

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: when he asked his father and others, "What do you worship?"

71.author: Shakir
71.text: They said: We worship idols, so we shall be their votaries.

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: They said, "We worship idols and will continue to cling to them."

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: They said: "We worship idols, and we remain constantly in attendance on them."

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Qaloo naAAbudu asnaman fanathallu laha AAakifeena

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: (Abraham) asked: "Do they hear when you call them?

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: He said, “Do they hear you when you call?”

72.author: Arberry
72.text: He said, 'Do they hear you when you call,

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: He said: hearken they unto you when ye cry?

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: He said: "Do they hear you, when you call (on them)?

72.author: Itani
72.text: He said, “Do they hear you when you pray?

72.author: Maududi
72.text: He asked: "Do they hear you when you call them

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: He said: "Do they hear you when you call"

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: He said: Do they hear you when ye cry?

72.author: Qarai
72.text: He said, ‘Do they hear you when you call them?

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: 'Do they hear you when you call on them' He asked.

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: He said, "Do they hear you when you supplicate?

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: They replied, "We worship idols and shall continue to worship them".

72.author: Shakir
72.text: He said: Do they hear you when you call?

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: He asked, "Do they hear you when you call to them?

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: He said: "Do they listen to you when ye call (on them)?"

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: Or do they benefit you, or do you harm?"

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: “Or do they benefit you or harm you?”

73.author: Arberry
73.text: or do they profit you, or harm?'

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: Or, benefit they you or hurt they you?

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: "Or do they benefit you or do they harm (you)?"

73.author: Itani
73.text: Or do they benefit you, or harm you?”

73.author: Maududi
73.text: or do they cause you any benefit or harm?"

73.author: Mubarakpuri

73.author: Qarai

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: 'Can they benefit you or harm you'

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: Or do they benefit you, or do they harm?"

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: He asked them, "Can the idols hear you when you pray to them

73.author: Shakir
73.text: Or do they profit you or cause you harm?

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: Do they help or harm you?"

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: They said: "No. But we found our fathers doing so."

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: They said, “In fact we found our forefathers doing likewise.”

74.author: Arberry
74.text: They said, 'Nay, but we found our fathers so doing.'

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: They said: nay; but we found our fathers doing in this wise.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: They said: "Nay, but we found our fathers doing so."

74.author: Itani
74.text: They said, “But we found our ancestors doing so.”

74.author: Maududi
74.text: They answered: "No; but we found our forefathers doing so."

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: They said: "(Nay) but we found our fathers doing so."

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: They said: Nay, but we found our fathers acting on this wise.

74.author: Qarai
74.text: They said, ‘Indeed, we found our fathers doing likewise.’

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: They replied: 'No, but we found our fathers doing so'

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: They said, "But we found our fathers doing thus."

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: He said: "Have you considered what you have been worshipping,

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: He said, “Do you see these – (the idols) whom you worship?”

75.author: Arberry
75.text: He said, 'And have you considered what you have been serving,

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: He said: have ye observed that which ye worship

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: He said: "Do you observe that which you have been worshipping,

75.author: Itani
75.text: He said, “Have you considered what you worship.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: Thereupon, Abraham said: "Have you seen (with your eyes) those whom you have been worshipping,

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: He said: "Do you observe that which you have been worshipping --"

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: He said: See now that which ye worship,

75.author: Qarai
75.text: He said, ‘Have you regarded what you have been worshipping,

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: He said: 'Have you considered what you worship,

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: He said, "Then do you see what you have been worshipping,

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: They said, "No, but our fathers worshipped them."

75.author: Shakir
75.text: He said: Have you then considered what you have been worshipping:

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: Abraham said, "Have you really thought about what you have been worshipping,

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: He said: "Do ye then see whom ye have been worshipping,-

76.author: Arberry
76.text: you and your fathers, the elders?

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: Ye and your fathers of old?

76.author: Maududi
76.text: you and your forefathers of yore?

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: (Abraham) said, "Do you know that what you worship and what your grandfathers worshipped

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: Yet they are my enemies except the Lord of all the worlds

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: “They are all my enemies, except the Lord Of The Creation.”

77.author: Arberry
77.text: They are an enemy to me, except the Lord of all Being

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: Verily they are an enemy unto me, save the Lord of the worlds.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: "Verily! They are enemies to me, save the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists);

77.author: Itani
77.text: They are enemies to me, but not so the Lord of the Worlds.

77.author: Maududi
77.text: They are all enemies to me; all, except the Lord of the Universe

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: "Verily, they are enemies to me, save the Lord of Al-`Alamin,"

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: Lo! they are (all) an enemy unto me, save the Lord of the Worlds,

77.author: Qarai
77.text: They are indeed enemies to me, but the Lord of all the worlds,

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: They are enemies to me except the Lord of all the Worlds

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: Indeed, they are enemies to me, except the Lord of the worlds,

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: are my enemies? Not so the Lord of the Universe.

77.author: Shakir
77.text: Surely they are enemies to me, but not (so) the Lord of the worlds;

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: they are all my enemies, not so the Lord of the Universe,

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: "For they are enemies to me; not so the Lord and Cherisher of the Worlds;

77.author: Transliteration
77.text: Fainnahum AAaduwwun lee illa rabba alAAalameena

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Who created me and showed me the way,

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: “The One Who created me, so He will guide me.”

78.author: Arberry
78.text: who created me, and Himself guides me,

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: Who hath created me, and He guideth me.

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: "Who has created me, and it is He Who guides me;

78.author: Itani
78.text: He who created me, and guides me.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: Who created me and Who guides me;

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: Who has created me, and it is He Who guides me.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: Who created me, and He doth guide me,

78.author: Qarai
78.text: who created me, it is He who guides me,

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: who created me; and He guides me,

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: Who created me, and He [it is who] guides me.

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: He created me and He will guide me.

78.author: Shakir
78.text: Who created me, then He has shown me the way:

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: who created me. It is He who guides me;

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: "Who created me, and it is He Who guides me;

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: Who gives me food and drink,

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: “And the One Who feeds me and gives me to drink.”

79.author: Arberry
79.text: and Himself gives me to eat and drink,

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: He Who feedeth me and giveth me to drink.

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: "And it is He Who feeds me and gives me to drink.

79.author: Itani
79.text: He who feeds me, and waters me.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: Who gives me food and drink,

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: And it is He Who feeds me and gives me to drink.

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: And Who feedeth me and watereth me.

79.author: Qarai
79.text: and provides me with food and drink,

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: and He gives me to eat and drink,

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: And it is He who feeds me and gives me drink.

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: It is He who gives me food and drink

79.author: Shakir
79.text: And He Who gives me to eat and gives me to drink:

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: He who gives me food and drink;

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: And heals me when I am sick,

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: “And when I fall ill, so it is He Who heals me.”

80.author: Arberry
80.text: and, whenever I am sick, heals me,

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: And when I sicken, then He healeth me.

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: "And when I am ill, it is He who cures me.

80.author: Itani
80.text: And when I get sick, He heals me.

80.author: Maududi
80.text: and Who, when I am ill, heals me;

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: And when I am ill, it is He Who cures me.

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: And when I sicken, then He healeth me,

80.author: Qarai
80.text: and when I get sick, it is He who cures me;

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: who, when I am sick, heals me;

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: And when I am ill, it is He who cures me

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: and heals me when I am sick.

80.author: Shakir
80.text: And when I am sick, then He restores me to health

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: He who cures me when I am ill;

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: "And when I am ill, it is He Who cures me;

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: Who will make me die, then give me life again,

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: “And He will give me death, then resurrect me.”

81.author: Arberry
81.text: who makes me to die, then gives me life,

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: And He Who shall cause me to die, and then shall quicken me.

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: "And Who will cause me to die, and then will bring me to life (again);

81.author: Itani
81.text: He who makes me die, and then revives me.

81.author: Maududi
81.text: Who will cause me to die and then will again restore me to life;

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: And Who will cause me to die, and then will bring me to life.

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: And Who causeth me to die, then giveth me life (again),

81.author: Qarai
81.text: who will make me die, then He will bring me to life,

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: who makes me to die and then revives me,

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: He will cause me to die and will bring me back to life.

81.author: Shakir
81.text: And He Who will cause me to die, then give me life;

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: He who will cause me to die and bring me back to life;

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: "Who will cause me to die, and then to life (again);

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: And who, I hope, will condone my faults on the Day of Judgement.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: “And the One Who, upon Whom I pin my hopes, will forgive me my mistakes on the Day of Judgement.”

82.author: Arberry
82.text: and who I am eager shall forgive me my offence on the Day of Doom.

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: And He Who, long, shall forgive me my faults, on the Day of Requital.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: "And Who, I hope will forgive me my faults on the Day of Recompense, (the Day of Resurrection),"

82.author: Itani
82.text: He who, I hope, will forgive my sins on the Day of the Reckoning.”

82.author: Maududi
82.text: Who, I hope, will forgive me my sins on the Day of Judgement."

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: And Who, I hope, will forgive me my faults on the Day of Recompense.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: And Who, I ardently hope, will forgive me my sin on the Day of Judgment.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: and who, I hope, will forgive me my faults on the Day of Retribution.’

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: and whom I am eager shall forgive me my sins on the Day of Recompense'

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: And who I aspire that He will forgive me my sin on the Day of Recompense."

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: It is He whom I expect to forgive my sins on the Day of Judgment.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: And Who, I hope, will forgive me my mistakes on the day of judgment.

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: and He who will, I hope, forgive me my faults on the Day of the Judgement.

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: "And who, I hope, will forgive me my faults on the day of Judgment.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Waallathee atmaAAu an yaghfira lee khateeatee yawma alddeeni

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: O Lord, give me wisdom and admit me among the righteous,

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: “My Lord, bestow me the command and join me with those who deserve your proximity.”

83.author: Arberry
83.text: My Lord, give me Judgment, and join me with the righteous,

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: My Lord! bestow on me wisdom and join me with the righteous.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: My Lord! Bestow Hukman (religious knowledge, right judgement of the affairs and Prophethood) on me, and join me with the righteous;

83.author: Itani
83.text: “My Lord! Grant me wisdom, and include me with the righteous.

83.author: Maududi
83.text: (And then Abraham prayed): "My Lord, endow me with knowledge and wisdom and join me with the righteous,

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: My Lord! Bestow Hukm on me, and join me with the righteous.

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: My Lord! Vouchsafe me wisdom and unite me to the righteous.

83.author: Qarai
83.text: ‘My Lord! Grant me [unerring] judgement, and unite me with the Righteous.

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: My Lord, give me judgment, and join me with the righteous.

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: [And he said], "My Lord, grant me authority and join me with the righteous.

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: Lord, grant me authority. Join me to the righteous ones.

83.author: Shakir
83.text: My Lord: Grant me wisdom, and join me with the good

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: My Lord, bestow wisdom upon me; unite me with the righteous;

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: "O my Lord! bestow wisdom on me, and join me with the righteous;

83.author: Transliteration
83.text: Rabbi hab lee hukman waalhiqnee bialssaliheena

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: And uphold my name with posterity,

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: “And give me proper fame among the succeeding generations.”

84.author: Arberry
84.text: and appoint me a tongue of truthfulness among the others.

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: And appoint for me an honourable mention among the posterity

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: And grant me an honourable mention in later generations;

84.author: Itani
84.text: And give me a reputation of truth among the others.

84.author: Maududi
84.text: and grant me an honourable reputation among posterity,

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: And grant me an honorable mention in later generations.

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: And give unto me a good report in later generations.

84.author: Qarai
84.text: Confer on me a worthy repute among the posterity,

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: And appoint me a tongue of truthfulness among the latter.

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: And grant me a reputation of honor among later generations.

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: Make my words come true in the future.

84.author: Shakir
84.text: And ordain for me a goodly mention among posterity

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: give me a good name among later generations;

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: "Grant me honourable mention on the tongue of truth among the latest (generations);

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: WaijAAal lee lisana sidqin fee alakhireena

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: And put me among the inheritors of Paradise.

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: “And make me among the inheritors of the Gardens of serenity.”

85.author: Arberry
85.text: Make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: And make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Delight.

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: And make me one of the inheritors of the Paradise of Delight;

85.author: Itani
85.text: And make me of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss.

85.author: Maududi
85.text: and make me of those who will inherit the Garden of Bliss,

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: And make me one of the inheritors of the Paradise of Delight.

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Delight,

85.author: Qarai
85.text: and make me one of the heirs to the paradise of bliss.

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: and place me amongst the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss.

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Pleasure.

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: Make me inherit the bountiful Paradise.

85.author: Shakir
85.text: And make me of the heirs of the garden of bliss

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: and make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss;

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: "Make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss;

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: Forgive me and my father: He was surely among those who went astray.

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: “And forgive my father* – he is indeed astray.” (His paternal uncle)

86.author: Arberry
86.text: and forgive my father, for he is one of those astray.

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: And forgive my father; verily he is of the erring.

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: And forgive my father, verily he is of the erring;

86.author: Itani
86.text: And forgive my father—he was one of the misguided.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: and forgive my father for he is among those who strayed,

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: And forgive my father, verily, he is of the erring.

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: And forgive my father. Lo! he is of those who err.

86.author: Qarai
86.text: Forgive my father, for he is one of those who are astray.

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: and forgive my father, for he was among the astray.

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: And forgive my father. Indeed, he has been of those astray.

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: Forgive my father. He has gone astray.

86.author: Shakir
86.text: And forgive my father, for surely he is of those who have gone astray;

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: and forgive my father; for he is one of the misguided;

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: "Forgive my father, for that he is among those astray;

86.author: Transliteration
86.text: Waighfir liabee innahu kana mina alddalleena

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: And do not disgrace me on the day when they are raised from the graves,

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: “And do not disgrace me on the day when everyone will be raised.”

87.author: Arberry
87.text: Degrade me not upon the day when they are raised up,

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: And humiliate me not on the Day whereon people shall be raised.

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: And disgrace me not on the Day when (all the creatures) will be resurrected;

87.author: Itani
87.text: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are resurrected.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: and disgrace me not on the Day when people will be raised to life,

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: And disgrace me not on the Day when they will be resurrected.

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: And abase me not on the day when they are raised,

87.author: Qarai
87.text: Do not disgrace me on the day that they will be resurrected,

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: Do not degrade me on the Day when they are resurrected.

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are [all] resurrected -

87.author: Shakir
87.text: And disgrace me not on the day when they are raised

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: and do not disgrace me on the Day when all people are resurrected,

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: "And let me not be in disgrace on the Day when (men) will be raised up;-

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: The day when neither wealth nor children will be of any avail

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: “The day when neither wealth will benefit nor will sons.”

88.author: Arberry
88.text: the day when neither wealth nor sons shall profit

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: The Day whereon will profit neither substance nor sons.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: The Day whereon neither wealth nor sons will avail,

88.author: Itani
88.text: The Day when neither wealth nor children will help.

88.author: Maududi
88.text: the Day when nothing will avail, neither wealth nor offspring,

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: The Day whereon neither wealth nor sons will avail,

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: The day when wealth and sons avail not (any man)

88.author: Qarai
88.text: the day when neither wealth nor children will avail,

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: The Day when neither wealth nor sons shall benefit

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: The Day when there will not benefit [anyone] wealth or children

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: on the Day of Judgment when neither wealth nor children will be of any benefit

88.author: Shakir
88.text: The day on which property will not avail, nor sons

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: the Day when wealth and sons will be of no avail,

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: "The Day whereon neither wealth nor sons will avail,

88.text: Yawma la yanfaAAu malun wala banoona

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: But to him who comes to God with a tranquil heart."

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: “Except he who presented himself before Allah, with a sound* heart.” (Intact or unblemished.)

89.author: Arberry
89.text: except for him who comes to God with a pure heart.

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: Unless it be he, who shall bring unto Allah a whole heart,

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: Except him who brings to Allah a clean heart [clean from Shirk (polytheism) and Nifaq (hypocrisy)].

89.author: Itani
89.text: Except for him who comes to God with a sound heart.”

89.author: Maududi
89.text: but only he that brings to Allah a sound heart will (attain to success)."

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: Except him who brings to Allah a clean heart.

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: Save him who bringeth unto Allah a whole heart.

89.author: Qarai
89.text: except him who comes to Allah with a sound heart,’

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: except him who comes before Allah with a pure heart;

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: But only one who comes to Allah with a sound heart."

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: except what is done in obedience to God with a submissive heart.

89.author: Shakir
89.text: Except him who comes to Allah with a heart free (from evil).

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: and when he alone will be saved who comes to God with a sound heart."

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: "But only he (will prosper) that brings to Allah a sound heart;

89.author: Transliteration
89.text: Illa man ata Allaha biqalbin saleemin

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: Paradise will be brought near those who take heed for themselves and follow the straight path;

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: And Paradise will be brought close for the pious.

90.author: Arberry
90.text: And Paradise shall be brought forward for the godfearing,

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: And the Garden shall be brought nigh to the God, fearing.

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: And Paradise will be brought near to the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2).

90.author: Itani
90.text: And Paradise will be brought near for the righteous.

90.author: Maududi
90.text: (On that Day) the Garden will be brought near to the God-fearing,

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: And Paradise will be brought near to those who had Taqwa.

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: And the Garden will be brought nigh for those who ward off (evil).

90.author: Qarai
90.text: and paradise will be brought near for the Godwary,

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: and Paradise shall be brought forward to the cautious.

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: And Paradise will be brought near [that Day] to the righteous.

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: On the Day of Judgment Paradise will be brought near

90.author: Shakir
90.text: And the garden shall be brought near for those who guard (against evil),

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: When Paradise shall be brought near to the God-fearing

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: "To the righteous, the Garden will be brought near,

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: And Hell made visible to those who had gone astray.

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: And hell will be revealed for the astray.

91.author: Arberry
91.text: and Hell advanced for the perverse.

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: And the Fierce Fire shall be made apparent unto the seduced ones.

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: And the (Hell) Fire will be placed in full view of the erring.

91.author: Itani
91.text: And the Blaze will be displayed to the deviators.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: and the Fire will be uncovered for those who strayed,

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: And the (Hell) Fire will be placed in full view of the astray.

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: And hell will appear plainly to the erring.

91.author: Qarai
91.text: and hell will be brought into view for the perverse,

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: And Hell is brought near to the perverse'

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: the pious and hell will be left open for the rebellious ones

91.author: Shakir
91.text: And the hell shall be made manifest to the erring ones,

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: and Hell shall be revealed to the misguided,

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: "And to those straying in Evil, the Fire will be placed in full view;

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: They will be asked: "Where are those you worshipped

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: And it will be said to them, “Where are those whom you used to worship?”

92.author: Arberry
92.text: It shall be said to them, 'Where is that you were serving

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: And it shall be said unto them where is that which ye were wont to worship?

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: And it will be said to them: "Where are those (the false gods whom you used to set up as rivals with Allah) that you used to worship

92.author: Itani
92.text: And it will be said to them, “Where are those you used to worship?”

92.author: Maududi
92.text: and they will be asked: "Where are the gods that you worshipped

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: And it will be said to them: "Where are those that you used to worship."

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: And it will be said unto them: Where is (all) that ye used to worship

92.author: Qarai
92.text: and they shall be told: ‘Where is that which you used to worship

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: It will be said to them: 'Where is that you worshipped,

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: And it will be said to them, "Where are those you used to worship

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: who will be asked, "What did you worship

92.author: Shakir
92.text: And it shall be said to them: Where are those that you used to worship;

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: they will be asked, "Where are those whom you worshipped

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: "And it shall be said to them: 'Where are the (gods) ye worshipped-

93.author: Ahmed Ali
93.text: Other than God? Can they save you or even save themselves?"

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: “Instead of Allah? Will they help you or retaliate?”

93.author: Arberry
93.text: apart from God? Do they help you or help themselves?'

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: Beside Allah? Can they succour you or succour themselves?

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: "Instead of Allah? Can they help you or (even) help themselves?"

93.author: Itani
93.text: Besides God? Can they help you, or help themselves?”

93.author: Maududi
93.text: beside Allah? Can they be of any help to you, or even be of any help to themselves?"

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: "Instead of Allah Can they help you or help themselves"

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: Instead of Allah? Can they help you or help themselves?

93.author: Qarai
93.text: besides Allah? Do they help you, or do they help each other?’

93.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
93.text: other than Allah? Do they help you or even help themselves'

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: Other than Allah? Can they help you or help themselves?"

93.author: Sarwar
93.text: besides God? Will the idols help you? Can they help themselves?"

93.author: Shakir
93.text: Besides Allah? Can they help you or yet help themselves?

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: besides God? Can they help you or even help themselves?"

93.author: Yusuf Ali
93.text: "'Besides Allah? Can they help you or help themselves?'

93.author: Transliteration
93.text: Min dooni Allahi hal yansuroonakum aw yantasiroona

94.author: Ahmed Ali
94.text: They will then be thrown into Hell with those who had gone astray

94.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
94.text: So they and all the astray were flung into hell.

94.author: Arberry
94.text: Then they shall be pitched into it, they and the perverse

94.author: Daryabadi
94.text: Then they shall be hurled therein, they and the seduced ones.

94.author: Hilali & Khan
94.text: Then they will be thrown on their faces into the (Fire), They and the Ghawun (devils, and those who were in error).

94.author: Itani
94.text: Then they will be toppled into it, together with the seducers.

94.author: Maududi
94.text: Then the idols and those who strayed will be hurled into the Fire headlong, one upon another,

94.author: Mubarakpuri
94.text: Then they will be thrown on their faces into it (the Fire), they and the astray.

94.author: Pickthall
94.text: Then will they be hurled therein, they and the seducers

94.author: Qarai
94.text: Then they will be cast into it on their faces—they and the perverse,

94.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
94.text: And they will be pitched into it, they and the perverse

94.author: Saheeh International
94.text: So they will be overturned into Hellfire, they and the deviators

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: The idol worshippers, the idols, the rebellious ones,

94.author: Shakir
94.text: So they shall be thrown down into it, they and the erring ones,

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: Then they will be thrown headlong into Hell, both they and the misguided ones,

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: "Then they will be thrown headlong into the (Fire),- they and those straying in Evil,

95.author: Ahmed Ali

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: And all the armies of Iblis. (Satan)

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: And the hosts of Iblis together.

95.author: Hilali & Khan

95.text: and the army of satan will all be thrown headlong into hell.

95.text: And the hosts of the Shaitan, all.

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: Disputing among themselves they will say:
