96.text: And they will say, and they will be quarrelling in it:

96.author: Arberry
96.text: They shall say, as they dispute there one with another,

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: And they, While contending therein, shall say:

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: They will say while contending therein,

96.author: Itani
96.text: They will say, as they feud in it.

96.author: Maududi
96.text: There they will quarrel with one another and the erring ones will say (to their deities):

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: They will say while contending therein,

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: And they will say, when they are quarrelling therein:

96.author: Qarai
96.text: They will say, as they wrangle in it [together],

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: And they will say while they dispute with one another,

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: They will say while they dispute therein,

96.author: Shakir
96.text: They shall say while they contend therein:

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: They will dispute between themselves therein, and will say,

96.author: Yusuf Ali
96.text: "They will say there in their mutual bickerings:

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: "By God, we were plainly in error

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: “By oath of Allah, we were indeed in open error.”

97.author: Arberry
97.text: 'By God, we were certainly in manifest error

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: By Allah, we have indeed been in an error manifest.

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: By Allah, we were truly in a manifest error,

97.author: Itani
97.text: “By God, We were in evident error.

97.author: Maududi
97.text: "By Allah, we were surely in clear error

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: "By Allah, we were truly in a manifest error,

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: By Allah, of a truth we were in error manifest

97.author: Qarai
97.text: ‘By Allah, we had indeed been in manifest error

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: 'By Allah, we were certainly in clear error,

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: "By Allah, we were indeed in manifest error

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: they will say, "By God, we were in clear error

97.author: Shakir
97.text: By Allah! we were certainly in manifest error,

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: "'By Allah, we were truly in an error manifest,

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: TaAllahi in kunna lafee dalalin mubeenin

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: In equating you with the Lord of all the worlds;

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: “When we considered you equal to the Lord Of The Creation.”

98.author: Arberry
98.text: when we made you equal with the Lord of all Being.

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: When we equalled you with the Lord of the worlds.

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: When We held you (false gods) as equals (in worship) with the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists);

98.author: Itani
98.text: For equating you with the Lord of the Worlds.

98.author: Maududi
98.text: when we assigned to you a position equal to that of the Lord of the Universe.

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: "When we held you as equals with the Lord of all that exists;"

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: When we made you equal with the Lord of the Worlds.

98.author: Qarai
98.text: when we equated you with the Lord of all the worlds!

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: when we made you equal with the Lord of the Worlds.

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: When we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: when we considered you equal to the Lord of the Universe.

98.author: Shakir
98.text: When we made you equal to the Lord of the worlds;

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: when we made you equal with the Lord of the Universe.

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: "'When we held you as equals with the Lord of the Worlds;

99.author: Ahmed Ali
99.text: No one but the sinners led us astray.

99.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
99.text: “And none misled us but the guilty.”

99.author: Arberry
99.text: It was naught but the sinners that led us astray;

99.author: Daryabadi
99.text: And none led us astray except the Culprits.

99.author: Hilali & Khan
99.text: And none has brought us into error except the Mujrimun [Iblis (Satan) and those of human beings who commit crimes, murderers, polytheists, oppressors, etc.].

99.author: Itani
99.text: No one misled us except the sinners.

99.author: Maududi
99.text: It is none but those steeped in guilt who led us into this error,

99.author: Mubarakpuri
99.text: "And none has brought us into error except the criminals."

99.author: Pickthall
99.text: It was but the guilty who misled us.

99.author: Qarai
99.text: No one led us astray except the guilty.

99.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
99.text: It was nothing but the evildoers who led us astray.

99.author: Saheeh International
99.text: And no one misguided us except the criminals.

99.author: Sarwar
99.text: Only the sinful ones made us go astray.

99.author: Shakir
99.text: And none but the guilty led us astray;

99.author: Wahiduddin Khan
99.text: It was the evildoers who led us astray,

99.author: Yusuf Ali
99.text: "'And our seducers were only those who were steeped in guilt.

100.author: Ahmed Ali
100.text: Now we have none to intercede for us,

100.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
100.text: “So now we do not have any intercessors.” (The believers shall have intercessors, the disbelievers none).

100.author: Arberry
100.text: so now we have no intercessors,

100.author: Daryabadi
100.text: So none we have as intercessors.

100.author: Maududi
100.text: and now we have none to intercede on our behalf,

100.author: Saheeh International
100.text: So now we have no intercessors

100.author: Sarwar
100.text: We have no one to intercede for us before God

100.author: Wahiduddin Khan
100.text: and we have no intercessors now,

100.author: Yusuf Ali
100.text: "'Now, then, we have none to intercede (for us),

101.author: Hilali & Khan
101.text: Nor a close friend (to help us).

101.author: Maududi
101.text: nor do we have a truly sincere friend.

101.author: Qarai
101.text: nor do we have any sympathetic friend.

101.author: Yusuf Ali
101.text: "'Nor a single friend to feel (for us).

102.author: Ahmed Ali
102.text: If only we could return and be among the believers!"

102.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
102.text: “So if only were we to go back, in order to become Muslims!”

102.author: Arberry
102.text: O that we might return again, and be among the believers!'

102.author: Daryabadi
102.text: Were there for us a return, we would be of the believers.

102.author: Hilali & Khan
102.text: (Alas!) If we only had a chance to return (to the world), we shall truly be among the believers!

102.author: Itani
102.text: If only we could have another chance, we would be among the faithful.”

102.author: Maududi
102.text: If only we could return we would be among the believers."

102.author: Mubarakpuri
102.text: "(Alas!) If we only had a chance to return, we shall truly be among the believers!"

102.author: Pickthall
102.text: Oh, that we had another turn (on earth), that we might be of the believers!

102.author: Qarai
102.text: Had there been another turn for us, we would be among the faithful.’

102.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
102.text: Would that we might return again, and be among the believers'

102.author: Saheeh International
102.text: Then if we only had a return [to the world] and could be of the believers... "

102.author: Sarwar
102.text: Would that we could have a chance to live again so that we might become believers."

102.author: Shakir
102.text: But if we could but once return, we would be of the believers.

102.author: Wahiduddin Khan
102.text: If we could only return to the world and be among the believers."

102.author: Yusuf Ali
102.text: "'Now if we only had a chance of return we shall truly be of those who believe!'"

102.author: Transliteration
102.text: Falaw anna lana karratan fanakoona mina almumineena

103.author: Ahmed Ali
103.text: Verily there is a sign in this, but many of them do not believe.

103.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
103.text: Indeed in this is a sign; and most of them were not believers.

103.author: Arberry
103.text: Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

103.author: Daryabadi
103.text: Verily herein is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

103.author: Hilali & Khan
103.text: Verily! In this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

103.author: Itani
103.text: Surely in this is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

103.author: Maududi
103.text: Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

103.author: Mubarakpuri
103.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

103.author: Pickthall
103.text: Lo! herein is indeed a portent, yet most of them are not believers!

103.author: Qarai
103.text: There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

103.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
103.text: Surely, in that there is a sign, yet most of them do not believe.

103.author: Saheeh International
103.text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

103.author: Sarwar
103.text: In this there is evidence (of the truth), but many of them do not have any faith.

103.author: Shakir
103.text: Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

103.author: Wahiduddin Khan
103.text: There is certainly a sign in that, but most of them would not believe:

103.author: Yusuf Ali
103.text: Verily in this is a Sign but most of them do not believe.

103.author: Transliteration
103.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

104.author: Ahmed Ali
104.text: Verily your Lord is mighty and merciful.

104.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
104.text: And indeed your Lord – only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

104.author: Arberry
104.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

104.author: Daryabadi
104.text: And verily thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

104.author: Hilali & Khan
104.text: And verily, your Lord! He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

104.author: Itani
104.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

104.author: Maududi
104.text: Verily Your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Ever Compassionate.

104.author: Mubarakpuri
104.text: And verily your Lord, He is truly the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

104.author: Pickthall
104.text: And lo, thy Lord! He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

104.author: Qarai
104.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

104.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
104.text: Surely, Your Lord is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

104.author: Saheeh International
104.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

104.author: Sarwar
104.text: Your Lord is certainly Majestic and All-merciful.

104.author: Shakir
104.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

104.author: Wahiduddin Khan
104.text: surely, your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

104.author: Yusuf Ali
104.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

105.text: Noah's folk denied the messengers (of Allah),

105.text: The people of Noah denied the messengers

105.text: The people of Nuh rejected the apostles.

105.text: The people of Noah rejected the messengers.

106.author: Ahmed Ali

106.text: Their brother Noah said to them, “Do you not fear?

106.author: Maududi

106.author: Mubarakpuri

107.author: Ahmed Ali
107.text: I have been sent as a trusted messenger to you.

107.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
107.text: “I am indeed a trustworthy Noble Messenger of Allah to you.”

107.author: Arberry
107.text: I am for you a faithful Messenger,

107.author: Daryabadi
107.text: Verily I am unto you an apostle trusted.

107.author: Hilali & Khan
107.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

107.author: Itani
107.text: I am to you a faithful messenger.

107.author: Maududi
107.text: I am a trustworthy Messenger to you;

107.author: Mubarakpuri
107.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you."

107.author: Pickthall
107.text: Lo! I am a faithful messenger unto you,

107.author: Qarai
107.text: Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you.

107.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
107.text: I am for you an honest Messenger,

107.author: Saheeh International
107.text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

107.author: Sarwar
107.text: I am a trustworthy Messengers sent to you.

107.author: Shakir
107.text: Surely I am a faithful apostle to you;

107.author: Wahiduddin Khan
107.text: I am a trustworthy messenger for you:

107.author: Yusuf Ali
107.text: "I am to you a messenger worthy of all trust:

108.author: Ahmed Ali

108.author: Arberry
108.text: so serve you God, and obey you me.

108.author: Daryabadi
108.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

108.author: Hilali & Khan
108.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

108.author: Itani
108.text: So fear God, and obey me.

108.author: Maududi
108.text: so fear Allah and obey me.

108.author: Mubarakpuri
108.text: "So have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

108.author: Pickthall
108.text: So keep your duty to Allah, and obey me.

108.author: Qarai
108.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me.

108.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
108.text: so fear Allah, and obey me.

108.author: Saheeh International
108.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

108.author: Sarwar
108.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

108.author: Shakir
108.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me

109.text: “And I do not ask from you any fee for it; my reward is only upon the Lord Of The Creation.”

109.author: Daryabadi

109.author: Hilali & Khan

109.author: Itani
109.text: I ask of you no payment for this. My payment is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

109.author: Maududi
109.text: I seek of you no reward for this: my reward is with none except the Lord of the Universe.

109.author: Mubarakpuri
109.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it; my reward is only from the Lord of all that exists."

109.author: Pickthall
109.text: And I ask of you no wage therefor; my wage is the concern only of the Lord of the Worlds.

109.author: Qarai
109.text: I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.

109.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
109.text: For this I ask of you no wage, for my wage falls only on the Lord of the Worlds.

109.text: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

109.author: Sarwar

109.text: I ask of you no recompense for it, for my only reward is with the Lord of the Universe,

111.author: Ahmed Ali
111.text: They said: "Should we place our trust in you when only the condemned follow you?"

111.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
111.text: They said, “Shall we believe in you, whereas the abject people are with you?”

111.author: Arberry
111.text: They said, 'Shall we believe thee, whom the vilest follow?'

111.author: Daryabadi
111.text: They said: shall We believe in thee when the meanest follow thee?

111.author: Hilali & Khan
111.text: They said: "Shall we believe in you, when the meanest (of the people) follow you?"

111.author: Itani
111.text: They said, “Shall we believe in you, when it is the lowliest who follow you?”

111.author: Maududi
111.text: They answered: "Shall we accept you even though it is the meanest of people who follow you?"

111.author: Mubarakpuri
111.text: They said: "Shall we believe in you, when the inferior follow you"

111.author: Pickthall
111.text: They said: Shall we put faith in thee, when the lowest (of the people) follow thee?

111.author: Qarai
111.text: They said, ‘Shall we believe in you, when it is the riffraff who follow you?’

111.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
111.text: They replied: 'Are we to believe you whom the lowliest follow'

111.author: Saheeh International
111.text: They said, "Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?"

111.author: Sarwar
111.text: They said, "Should we believe in you when no one has followed you except the lowest ones".

111.author: Shakir
111.text: They said: Shall we believe in you while the meanest follow you?

111.author: Wahiduddin Khan
111.text: They replied, "Are we to believe in you when your followers are but the lowest of the low?"

111.author: Yusuf Ali
111.text: They said: "Shall we believe in thee when it is the meanest that follow thee?"

112.author: Ahmed Ali
112.text: He said: "It is not for me to know what they were doing.

112.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
112.text: He said, “What do I know what their deeds are?”

112.author: Arberry
112.text: He said, 'What knowledge have I of that they have been doing?

112.author: Daryabadi
112.text: He said: I have no knowledge of that which they have been Working.

112.author: Hilali & Khan
112.text: He said: "And what knowledge have I of what they used to do?

112.author: Itani
112.text: He said, “What do I know about what they do?

112.author: Maududi
112.text: Noah said: "What knowledge do I have about their deeds?

112.author: Mubarakpuri
112.text: He said: "And what knowledge have I of what they used to do"

112.author: Pickthall
112.text: He said: And what knowledge have I of what they may have been doing (in the past)?

112.author: Qarai
112.text: He said, ‘What do I know as to what they used to do?

112.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
112.text: He said: 'I have no knowledge of what they have done.

112.author: Saheeh International
112.text: He said, "And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?

112.author: Sarwar
112.text: (Noah) said, "I have no knowledge of their deeds.

112.author: Shakir
112.text: He said: And what knowledge have I of what they do?

112.author: Wahiduddin Khan
112.text: He said, "What knowledge do I have of their doings?

112.author: Yusuf Ali
112.text: He said: "And what do I know as to what they do?

112.author: Transliteration
112.text: Qala wama AAilmee bima kanoo yaAAmaloona

113.author: Ahmed Ali
113.text: It is for my Lord to bring them to book, if you can understand.

113.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
113.text: “Indeed their account is only upon my Lord, if you perceive.”

113.author: Arberry
113.text: Their account falls only upon my Lord, were you but aware.

113.author: Daryabadi
113.text: Their reckoning is upon my Lord, if ye but knew.

113.author: Hilali & Khan
113.text: "Their account is only with my Lord, if you could (but) know.

113.author: Itani
113.text: Their account rests only with my Lord, if you have sense.

113.author: Maududi
113.text: It is only for my Lord to take account of them. Would that you made use of your understanding!

113.author: Mubarakpuri
113.text: "Their account is only with my Lord, if you could (but) know."

113.author: Pickthall
113.text: Lo! their reckoning is my Lord's concern, if ye but knew;

113.author: Qarai
113.text: Their reckoning is only with my Lord, should you be aware.

113.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
113.text: Their account falls only upon My Lord, if you were but aware.

113.author: Saheeh International
113.text: Their account is only upon my Lord, if you [could] perceive.

113.author: Sarwar
113.text: If only you would realize, their account is with my Lord.

113.author: Shakir
113.text: Their account is only with my Lord, if you could perceive

113.author: Wahiduddin Khan
113.text: My Lord alone can bring them to account, if only you could understand,

113.author: Yusuf Ali
113.text: "Their account is only with my Lord, if ye could (but) understand.

113.author: Transliteration
113.text: In hisabuhum illa AAala rabbee law tashAAuroona

114.author: Ahmed Ali
114.text: I am certainly not one to drive away the believers.

114.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
114.text: “And I will not repel the Muslims.”

114.author: Arberry
114.text: I would not drive away the believers;

114.author: Daryabadi
114.text: And I am not to drive away the believers.

114.author: Hilali & Khan
114.text: "And I am not going to drive away the believers.

114.author: Itani
114.text: And I am not about to drive away the believers.

114.author: Maududi
114.text: It is not for me to repel those who choose to believe.

114.author: Mubarakpuri
114.text: "And I am not going to drive away the believers."

114.author: Pickthall
114.text: And I am not (here) to repulse believers.

114.author: Qarai
114.text: I will not drive away the faithful.

114.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
114.text: I will not drive away the believers.

114.author: Saheeh International
114.text: And I am not one to drive away the believers.

114.author: Sarwar
114.text: I do not drive away the believers.

114.author: Shakir
114.text: And I am not going to drive away the believers;

114.author: Wahiduddin Khan
114.text: I am not going to drive away any believers.

114.author: Yusuf Ali
114.text: "I am not one to drive away those who believe.

115.author: Ahmed Ali
115.text: I am only a plain admonisher."

115.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
115.text: “I am not but clearly a Herald of Warning.”

115.author: Arberry
115.text: 'I am naught but a plain warner.'

115.author: Daryabadi
115.text: I am naught but a warner manifest.

115.author: Hilali & Khan
115.text: I am only a plain warner."

115.author: Itani
115.text: I am only a clear warner.”

115.author: Maududi
115.text: I am none but a plain warner."

115.author: Pickthall
115.text: I am only a plain warner.

115.author: Qarai
115.text: I am just a manifest warner.’

115.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
115.text: I am only a clear warner'

115.author: Saheeh International
115.text: I am only a clear warner."

115.author: Shakir
115.text: I am naught but a plain warner.

115.author: Wahiduddin Khan
115.text: I am only a plain warner."

115.author: Yusuf Ali
115.text: "I am sent only to warn plainly in public."

116.author: Ahmed Ali
116.text: They said: "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will be stoned to death."

116.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
116.text: They said, “O Nooh, if you do not desist you will surely be stoned.”

116.author: Arberry
116.text: They said, 'If thou givest not over, Noah, thou shalt assuredly be one of the stoned.'

116.author: Daryabadi
116.text: They said: if thou desistest not, thou shalt surely be of those stoned.

116.author: Hilali & Khan
116.text: They said: "If you cease not, O Nuh (Noah)! You will surely be among those stoned (to death)."

116.author: Itani
116.text: They said, “If you do not refrain, O Noah, you will be stoned.”

116.author: Maududi
116.text: They said: "O Noah! If you do not desist, you will certainly become one of the accursed."

116.author: Mubarakpuri
116.text: They said: "If you cease not, O Nuh you will surely be among those stoned."

116.author: Pickthall
116.text: They said: If thou cease not, O Noah, thou wilt surely be among those stoned (to death).

116.author: Qarai
116.text: They said, ‘Noah, if you do not desist, you will certainly be stoned [to death].’

116.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
116.text: 'Noah' they replied, 'if you do not desist you shall be of those stoned'

116.author: Saheeh International
116.text: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."

116.author: Sarwar
116.text: They said, "Noah, if you do not desist, you will, certainly, be stoned to death."

116.author: Shakir
116.text: They said: If you desist not, O Nuh, you shall most certainly be of those stoned to death.

116.author: Wahiduddin Khan
116.text: They said, "Noah, if you do not desist, you will be stoned."

116.author: Yusuf Ali
116.text: They said: "If thou desist not, O Noah! thou shalt be stoned (to death)."

116.author: Transliteration
116.text: Qaloo lain lam tantahi ya noohu latakoonanna mina almarjoomeena

117.author: Ahmed Ali
117.text: He prayed: "O Lord, verily my people accuse me of lies.

117.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
117.text: He said, “My Lord, my people have denied me.”

117.author: Arberry
117.text: He said, 'My Lord, my people have cried me lies,

117.author: Daryabadi
117.text: He said: my Lord! verily my people have belied me.

117.author: Hilali & Khan
117.text: He said: "My Lord! Verily, my people have belied me.

117.author: Itani
117.text: He said, “My Lord, my people have denied me.

117.author: Maududi
117.text: He said: "My Lord! My people have branded me a liar.

117.author: Mubarakpuri
117.text: He said: "My Lord! Verily, my people have denied me."

117.author: Pickthall
117.text: He said: My Lord! Lo! my own folk deny me.

117.author: Qarai
117.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed my people have impugned me.

117.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
117.text: He said: 'My Lord, my nation have belied me.

117.author: Saheeh International
117.text: He said, "My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.

117.author: Sarwar
117.text: Then Noah said, "Lord, my people have rejected me.

117.author: Shakir
117.text: He said: My Lord! Surely my people give me the lie!

117.author: Wahiduddin Khan
117.text: Noah said, "My Lord, my people have rejected me,

117.author: Yusuf Ali
117.text: He said: "O my Lord! truly my people have rejected me.

118.author: Ahmed Ali
118.text: So adjudge between me and them conclusively, and save me and the believers who are with me."

118.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
118.text: “Therefore make a decisive judgement between me and them, and rescue me and the believers along with me.”

118.author: Arberry
118.text: so give true deliverance between me and them, and deliver me and the believers that are with me.

118.author: Daryabadi
118.text: So decide Thou between me and them with a decision, and deliver me and those who are with me of the believers.

118.author: Hilali & Khan
118.text: Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me."

118.author: Itani
118.text: So judge between me and them decisively, and deliver me and the believers who are with me.

118.author: Maududi
118.text: So pass a clear judgement between me and them and rescue me and the believers with me."

118.author: Mubarakpuri
118.text: "Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me."

118.author: Pickthall
118.text: Therefor judge Thou between us, a (conclusive) judgment, and save me and those believers who are with me.

118.author: Qarai
118.text: So judge conclusively between me and them, and deliver me and the faithful who are with me.’

118.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
118.text: So open between me and them an opening, and save me and the believers who are with me'

118.author: Saheeh International
118.text: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."

118.author: Sarwar
118.text: Judge among us and save me and the believers with me".

118.author: Shakir
118.text: Therefore judge Thou between me and them with a (just) judgment, and deliver me and those who are with me of the believers.

118.author: Wahiduddin Khan
118.text: therefore, judge decisively between me and them; and save me and the believers who are with me."

118.author: Yusuf Ali
118.text: "Judge Thou, then, between me and them openly, and deliver me and those of the Believers who are with me."

118.author: Transliteration
118.text: Faiftah baynee wabaynahum fathan wanajjinee waman maAAiya mina almumineena

119.author: Ahmed Ali
119.text: So We delivered him and those with him in the loaded ark,

119.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
119.text: So We saved him and those with him in a laden ship.

119.author: Arberry
119.text: So We delivered him, and those with him, in the laden ship,

119.author: Daryabadi
119.text: Wherefore We delivered him and those with him in the laden ark.

119.author: Hilali & Khan
119.text: And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

119.author: Itani
119.text: So We delivered him and those with him in the laden Ark.

119.author: Maududi
119.text: Thereafter We rescued him and those who were with him in the laden Ark

119.author: Mubarakpuri
119.text: And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

119.author: Pickthall
119.text: And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

119.author: Qarai
119.text: Thereupon We delivered him and those who were with him in the laden ark.

119.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
119.text: We saved him and those who were with him in the laden ship,

119.author: Saheeh International
119.text: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

119.author: Sarwar
119.text: We saved him and those who were with him in a fully laden Ark,

119.author: Shakir
119.text: So We delivered him and those with him in the laden ark.

119.author: Wahiduddin Khan
119.text: So We saved him, and those who were with him in the laden ark,

119.author: Yusuf Ali
119.text: So We delivered him and those with him, in the Ark filled (with all creatures).

119.author: Transliteration
119.text: Faanjaynahu waman maAAahu fee alfulki almashhooni

120.author: Ahmed Ali
120.text: And drowned the rest of them.

120.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
120.text: Then after it, We drowned the rest.

120.author: Arberry
120.text: then afterwards We drowned the rest.

120.author: Daryabadi
120.text: Then We drowned the rest thereafter.

120.author: Hilali & Khan
120.text: Then We drowned the rest (disbelievers) thereafter.

120.author: Mubarakpuri
120.text: Then We drowned the rest thereafter.

120.author: Pickthall
120.text: Then afterward We drowned the others.

120.author: Saheeh International
120.text: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.

120.author: Shakir
120.text: Then We drowned the rest afterwards

120.author: Yusuf Ali
120.text: Thereafter We drowned those who remained behind.

121.author: Ahmed Ali
121.text: Verily there is a sign in this, yet many of them do not believe.

121.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
121.text: Indeed in this is a sign; and most of them were not believers.

121.author: Arberry
121.text: Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

121.author: Daryabadi
121.text: Verily herein is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

121.author: Hilali & Khan
121.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

121.author: Itani
121.text: In that is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

121.author: Maududi
121.text: Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

121.author: Mubarakpuri
121.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

121.author: Pickthall
121.text: Lo! herein is indeed a portent, yet most of them are not believers.

121.author: Qarai
121.text: There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

121.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
121.text: Surely, in that there is a sign; yet most of them do not believe.

121.author: Saheeh International
121.text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

121.author: Sarwar
121.text: In this there is evidence (of the truth) but most of them do not have any faith.

121.author: Shakir
121.text: Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

121.author: Wahiduddin Khan
121.text: There is certainly a sign in that; but most of them do not believe:

121.author: Yusuf Ali
121.text: Verily in this is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

121.author: Transliteration
121.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

122.author: Ahmed Ali
122.text: But surely your Lord is mighty and merciful.

122.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
122.text: And indeed your Lord, only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

122.author: Arberry
122.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

122.author: Daryabadi
122.text: And thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

122.author: Hilali & Khan
122.text: And verily! Your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

122.author: Itani
122.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

122.author: Maududi
122.text: Verily Your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Ever Compassionate.

122.author: Mubarakpuri
122.text: And verily your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

122.author: Pickthall
122.text: And lo, thy Lord, He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

122.author: Qarai
122.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

122.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
122.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

122.author: Saheeh International
122.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

122.author: Sarwar
122.text: Your Lord is Majestic and All-merciful.

122.author: Shakir
122.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

122.author: Wahiduddin Khan
122.text: surely your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

122.author: Yusuf Ali
122.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

123.author: Ahmed Ali
123.text: The people of 'Ad accused the messengers of lies.

123.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
123.text: (The tribe of) A’ad denied the Noble Messengers.

123.author: Arberry
123.text: Ad cried lies to the Envoys

123.author: Maududi
123.text: The Ad gave the lie to the Messengers.

123.author: Pickthall
123.text: (The tribe of) A'ad denied the messengers (of Allah).

123.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
123.text: (The nation of) Aad belied their Messengers.

123.author: Sarwar
123.text: The tribe of Ad rejected the Messengers.

123.author: Shakir
123.text: Ad gave the lie to the apostles.

123.author: Wahiduddin Khan
123.text: The people of 'Ad, too, rejected the messengers.

124.author: Ahmed Ali
124.text: When their brother Hud said to them: "Will you not take heed?

124.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
124.text: When their fellowman Hud said to them, “Do you not fear?”

124.author: Arberry
124.text: when their brother Hood said to them, 'Will you not be godfearing?

124.author: Daryabadi
124.text: When their brother Hud said Unto them: fear ye not?

124.author: Hilali & Khan
124.text: When their brother Hud said to them: "Will you not fear Allah and obey Him?

124.author: Itani
124.text: When their brother Hud said to them, “Do you not fear?

124.author: Maududi
124.text: Recall, when their brother Hud said to them: "Have you no fear?

124.author: Mubarakpuri
124.text: When their brother Hud said to them: "Will you not have Taqwa"

124.author: Pickthall
124.text: When their brother Hud said unto them: Will ye not ward off (evil)?

124.author: Qarai
124.text: when Hud, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]?

124.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
124.text: When their brother Hood said to them: 'Will you not be cautious?

124.author: Saheeh International
124.text: When their brother Hud said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?

124.author: Sarwar
124.text: Their brother Hud asked them, "Why do you not have fear of God?

124.author: Shakir
124.text: When their brother Hud said to them: Will you not guard (against evil)?

124.author: Wahiduddin Khan
124.text: Their brother Hud said to them, "Will you not fear God?

124.author: Yusuf Ali
124.text: Behold, their brother Hud said to them: "Will ye not fear (Allah)?

124.author: Transliteration
124.text: Ith qala lahum akhoohum hoodun ala tattaqoona

125.author: Ahmed Ali
125.text: I have been sent as a trusted messenger to you.

125.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
125.text: “I am indeed a trustworthy Noble Messenger of Allah to you.”

125.author: Arberry
125.text: I am for you a faithful Messenger,

125.author: Daryabadi
125.text: Verily I am unto you an apostle trusted.

125.author: Hilali & Khan
125.text: "Verily! I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

125.author: Itani
125.text: I am to you a faithful messenger.

125.author: Maududi
125.text: I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

125.author: Mubarakpuri
125.text: "Verily, I am a trustworthy Messenger to you."

125.author: Pickthall
125.text: Lo! I am a faithful messenger unto you,

125.author: Qarai
125.text: Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you.

125.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
125.text: I am for you an honest Messenger.

125.author: Saheeh International
125.text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

125.author: Sarwar
125.text: I am a trustworthy Messengers sent to you.

125.author: Shakir
125.text: Surely I am a faithful apostle to you;

125.author: Wahiduddin Khan
125.text: I am a trustworthy messenger for you:

125.author: Yusuf Ali
125.text: "I am to you a messenger worthy of all trust:

126.author: Ahmed Ali
126.text: So fear God and listen to me.

126.author: Arberry
126.text: so fear you God, and obey you me.

126.author: Daryabadi
126.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

126.author: Hilali & Khan
126.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

126.author: Itani
126.text: So fear God, and obey me.

126.author: Maududi
126.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

126.author: Mubarakpuri
126.text: "So, have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

126.author: Pickthall
126.text: So keep your duty to Allah and obey me.

126.author: Qarai
126.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me.

126.author: Saheeh International
126.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

126.author: Sarwar
126.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

126.author: Shakir
126.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me:

126.author: Wahiduddin Khan
126.text: fear God, then, and obey me.

127.author: Ahmed Ali
127.text: I ask no recompense of you for it. My reward is due from none but the Lord of all the worlds.

127.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
127.text: “And I do not ask from you any fee for it; my reward is only upon the Lord Of The Creation.”

127.author: Arberry
127.text: I ask of you no wage for this; my wage falls only upon the Lord of all Being.

127.author: Daryabadi
127.text: And I ask of you no hire therefor: my hire is but with the Lord of the worlds.

127.author: Hilali & Khan
127.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it (my Message of Islamic Monotheism), my reward is only from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns, and all that exists).

127.author: Itani
127.text: I ask of you no payment for this. My payment is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

127.author: Maududi
127.text: I seek of you no reward for this; my reward is with none but the Lord of the Universe.

127.author: Mubarakpuri
127.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it; my reward is only from the Lord of all that exists."

127.author: Pickthall
127.text: And I ask of you no wage therefor; my wage is the concern only of the Lord of the Worlds.

127.author: Qarai
127.text: I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.

127.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
127.text: For this I ask of you no wage, for my wage falls only on the Lord of the Worlds.

127.author: Saheeh International
127.text: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

127.author: Sarwar
127.text: I do not ask for any payment for my preaching. I shall receive my reward from the Lord of the Universe.

127.author: Shakir
127.text: And I do not ask you any reward for it; surely my reward is only with the Lord of the worlds

127.author: Wahiduddin Khan
127.text: I ask no recompense of you; my reward is only with the Lord of the Universe.

127.author: Yusuf Ali
127.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it: my reward is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

127.author: Transliteration
127.text: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena

128.author: Ahmed Ali
128.text: You construct monuments on every hill in vain,

128.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
128.text: “What! You build a structure on every height, to laugh at the passers-by?”

128.author: Arberry
128.text: What, do you build on every prominence a sign, sporting,

128.author: Daryabadi
128.text: Build ye on every eminence a landmark in vanity?

128.author: Hilali & Khan
128.text: "Do you build high palaces on every high place, while you do not live in them?

128.author: Itani
128.text: Do you build a monument on every height for vanity's sake?

128.author: Maududi
128.text: What, you build a monument on every hill merely for fun

128.author: Mubarakpuri
128.text: "Do you build on every Ri` an Ayah for your amusement"

128.author: Pickthall
128.text: Build ye on every high place a monument for vain delight?

128.author: Qarai
128.text: Do you build futile a sign on every prominence?

128.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
128.text: Do you build over each high place a sign to amuse yourselves!

128.author: Saheeh International
128.text: Do you construct on every elevation a sign, amusing yourselves,

128.author: Sarwar
128.text: "Do you build useless monuments on every mountain

128.author: Shakir
128.text: Do you build on every height a monument? Vain is it that you do:

128.author: Wahiduddin Khan
128.text: Do you build monuments on every high place in vanity,

128.author: Yusuf Ali
128.text: "Do ye build a landmark on every high place to amuse yourselves?

129.author: Ahmed Ali
129.text: Erect palaces (thinking) that you will live for ever,

129.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
129.text: “And prefer strong palaces, that perhaps you may live for ever?”

129.author: Arberry
129.text: and do you take to you castles, haply to dwell forever?

129.author: Daryabadi
129.text: And take ye for yourselves castles that haply ye may abide.

129.author: Hilali & Khan
129.text: "And do you get for yourselves palaces (fine buildings) as if you will live therein for ever.

129.author: Itani
129.text: And you set up fortresses, hoping to live forever?

129.author: Maududi
129.text: and erect huge palaces as though you will live for ever,

129.author: Mubarakpuri
129.text: "And do you get for yourselves Masani` as if you will live therein forever"

129.author: Pickthall
129.text: And seek ye out strongholds, that haply ye may last for ever?

129.author: Qarai
129.text: You set up structures as if you will be immortal,

129.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
129.text: And do you take to yourselves underground reservoirs, in order to live for ever!

129.author: Saheeh International
129.text: And take for yourselves palaces and fortresses that you might abide eternally?

129.author: Sarwar
129.text: and raise strong mansions as if you were to live forever?

129.author: Shakir
129.text: And you make strong fortresses that perhaps you may

129.author: Wahiduddin Khan
129.text: and erect castles hoping that you will live forever.

129.author: Yusuf Ali
129.text: "And do ye get for yourselves fine buildings in the hope of living therein (for ever)?

130.author: Ahmed Ali
130.text: And whenever you apply force become tyrannical.

130.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
130.text: “And when you apprehend someone, you seize him mercilessly?”

130.author: Arberry
130.text: When you assault, you assault like tyrants!

130.author: Daryabadi
130.text: And when ye seize, ye seize like unto tyrants.

130.author: Hilali & Khan
130.text: "And when you seize, seize you as tyrants?

130.author: Itani
130.text: And when you strike, you strike mercilessly?

130.author: Maududi
130.text: and when you strike you strike like tyrants?

130.author: Mubarakpuri
130.text: "And when you seize (somebody), seize you (him) as tyrants"

130.author: Pickthall
130.text: And if ye seize by force, seize ye as tyrants?

130.author: Qarai
130.text: and when you seize [someone for punishment], you seize [him] like tyrants.

130.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
130.text: When you assault, you assault like tyrants.

130.author: Saheeh International
130.text: And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.

130.author: Sarwar
130.text: When you attack, you attack as tyrants do.

130.author: Shakir
130.text: And when you lay hands (on men) you lay hands (like) tyrants;

130.author: Wahiduddin Khan
130.text: When you lay hands upon anyone, you do so as tyrants.

130.author: Yusuf Ali
130.text: "And when ye exert your strong hand, do ye do it like men of absolute power?

131.author: Ahmed Ali
131.text: So be fearful of God and follow me.

131.author: Arberry
131.text: So fear you God, and obey you me;

131.author: Daryabadi
131.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

131.author: Hilali & Khan
131.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

131.author: Itani
131.text: So fear God, and obey me.

131.author: Maududi
131.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

131.author: Mubarakpuri
131.text: "So have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

131.author: Pickthall
131.text: Rather keep your duty to Allah, and obey me.

131.author: Qarai
131.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me.

131.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
131.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

131.author: Saheeh International
131.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

131.author: Sarwar
131.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

131.author: Shakir
131.text: So guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me

131.author: Wahiduddin Khan
131.text: So fear God, and obey me;

132.author: Ahmed Ali
132.text: Fear Him who has bestowed on you what you know,

132.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
132.text: “Fear Him Who has aided you with the things you know.”

132.author: Arberry
132.text: and fear Him who has succoured you with what you know,

132.author: Daryabadi
132.text: And fear Him Who hath aided you with that which ye know.

132.author: Hilali & Khan
132.text: "And keep your duty to Him, fear Him Who has aided you with all (good things) that you know.

132.author: Itani
132.text: And reverence Him, who supplied you with everything you know.

132.author: Maududi
132.text: Have fear of Him Who has provided you with all the (good) things you know;

132.author: Mubarakpuri
132.text: "And have Taqwa of Him, fear Him Who has aided you with all (good things) that you know."

132.author: Pickthall
132.text: Keep your duty toward Him Who hath aided you with (the good things) that ye know,

132.author: Qarai
132.text: Be wary of Him who has provided you with whatever you know,

132.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
132.text: Fear Him who has given you all the things you know.

132.author: Saheeh International
132.text: And fear He who provided you with that which you know,

132.author: Sarwar
132.text: Have fear of the One who has bestowed upon you all that you know.

132.author: Shakir
132.text: And be careful of (your duty to) Him Who has given you abundance of what you know.

132.author: Wahiduddin Khan
132.text: fear Him who has aided you with all that you know --

132.author: Yusuf Ali
132.text: "Yea, fear Him Who has bestowed on you freely all that ye know.

133.author: Ahmed Ali
133.text: Gave you increase of cattle and sons,

133.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
133.text: “He aided you with cattle and sons.”

133.author: Arberry
133.text: succoured you with flocks and sons,

133.author: Daryabadi
133.text: He hath aided you With Cattle and sons.

133.author: Hilali & Khan
133.text: "He has aided you with cattle and children.

133.author: Itani
133.text: He supplied you with livestock and children.

133.author: Maududi
133.text: Who has provided you with flocks and children

133.author: Mubarakpuri
133.text: "He has aided you with cattle and children."

133.author: Pickthall
133.text: Hath aided you with cattle and sons.

133.author: Qarai
133.text: and aided you with sons and with cattle,

133.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
133.text: He has given you flocks and sons,

133.author: Saheeh International
133.text: Provided you with grazing livestock and children

133.author: Sarwar
133.text: He has given you cattle, children,

133.author: Shakir
133.text: He has given you abundance of cattle and children

133.author: Wahiduddin Khan
133.text: He has bestowed on you cattle, and sons,

133.author: Yusuf Ali
133.text: "Freely has He bestowed on you cattle and sons,-

135.author: Ahmed Ali
135.text: I fear the punishment of a terrible day for you.

135.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
135.text: “Indeed I fear upon you the punishment of a Great Day.”

135.author: Arberry
135.text: 'Indeed, I fear for you the chastisement of a dreadful day.'

135.author: Daryabadi
135.text: Verily I fear for you the torment of a Mighty Day.

135.author: Hilali & Khan
135.text: "Verily, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day."

135.author: Itani
135.text: I fear for you the punishment of an awesome Day.”

135.author: Maududi
135.text: I fear for you the chastisement of an Awesome Day."

135.author: Mubarakpuri
135.text: "Verily, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day."

135.author: Pickthall
135.text: Lo! I fear for you the retribution of an awful day.

135.author: Qarai
135.text: Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.’

135.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
135.text: Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a dreadful Day'

135.author: Saheeh International
135.text: Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day."

135.author: Sarwar
135.text: I am afraid that you will suffer the torment of the Day of Judgment."

135.author: Shakir
135.text: Surely I fear for you the chastisement of a grievous day

135.author: Wahiduddin Khan
135.text: indeed, I fear for you the torment of an awful day."

135.author: Yusuf Ali
135.text: "Truly I fear for you the Penalty of a Great Day."

135.author: Transliteration
135.text: Innee akhafu AAalaykum AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin

136.author: Ahmed Ali
136.text: They said: "It is the same to us if you warn us or do not warn.

136.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
136.text: They said, “It is the same for us, whether you advise us or not be of the preachers.”

136.author: Arberry
136.text: They said, 'Alike it is to us, whether thou admonishest, or art not one of the admonishers;

136.author: Daryabadi
136.text: They said: it is equal unto us whether thou admonishest or art not of the admonishers.

136.author: Hilali & Khan
136.text: They said: "It is the same to us whether you preach or be not of those who preach.

136.author: Itani
136.text: They said, “It is the same for us, whether you lecture us, or do not lecture.

136.author: Maududi
136.text: They replied: "It is all the same for us whether you admonish us or not.

136.author: Mubarakpuri
136.text: They said: "It is the same to us whether you preach or be not of those who preach."

136.author: Pickthall
136.text: They said: It is all one to us whether thou preachest or art not of those who preach;

136.author: Qarai
136.text: They said, ‘It is the same to us whether you exhort us or not.

136.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
136.text: They replied: 'It is the same to us whether you admonish or whether you are not one of the admonishers.

136.author: Saheeh International
136.text: They said, "It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not of the advisors.

136.author: Sarwar
136.text: They said, "Whether you preach to us or not,

136.author: Shakir
136.text: They said: It is the same to us whether you admonish or are not one of the admonishers

136.author: Wahiduddin Khan
136.text: They replied, "It makes no difference to us whether you preach or do not preach,

136.author: Yusuf Ali
136.text: They said: "It is the same to us whether thou admonish us or be not among (our) admonishers!

136.author: Transliteration
136.text: Qaloo sawaon AAalayna awaAAathta am lam takun mina alwaAAitheena

137.author: Ahmed Ali
137.text: This is just a habit of antiquated people.

137.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
137.text: “This is nothing but the tradition of former people.”

137.author: Arberry
137.text: this is nothing but the habit of the ancients,

137.author: Daryabadi
137.text: This in but a custom of the ancients.

137.author: Hilali & Khan
137.text: "This is no other than the false-tales and religion of the ancients, [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 19, Page 97]

137.author: Itani
137.text: This is nothing but morals of the ancients.

137.author: Maududi
137.text: This has been happening all along.

137.author: Mubarakpuri
137.text: "This is no other than Khuluq of the ancients,"

137.author: Pickthall
137.text: This is but a fable of the men of old,

137.author: Qarai
137.text: These are nothing but the traditions of the ancients,

137.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
137.text: That is nothing but a habit of the ancients,

137.author: Saheeh International
137.text: This is not but the custom of the former peoples,

137.author: Sarwar
137.text: your preaching is nothing but ancient legends and we shall not face any torment".

137.author: Shakir
137.text: This is naught but a custom of the ancients;

137.author: Wahiduddin Khan
137.text: this is nothing but a habit of the ancients:

137.author: Yusuf Ali
137.text: "This is no other than a customary device of the ancients,

138.author: Ahmed Ali
138.text: We are not going to be damned."

138.author: Arberry
138.text: and we shall not be chastised.'

138.author: Daryabadi
138.text: And we are not going to be tormented.

138.author: Hilali & Khan
138.text: "And we are not going to be punished."

138.author: Itani
138.text: And we will not be punished.”

138.author: Maududi
138.text: We will not be subjected to any chastisement."

138.author: Mubarakpuri
138.text: "And we are not going to be punished."

138.author: Pickthall
138.text: And we shall not be doomed.

138.author: Qarai
138.text: and we will not be punished.’

138.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
138.text: and we shall never be punished'

138.author: Saheeh International
138.text: And we are not to be punished."

138.author: Sarwar
138.text: They rejected him and We destroyed them.

138.author: Shakir
138.text: And we are not going to be punished.

138.author: Wahiduddin Khan
138.text: and we shall not be punished."

138.author: Yusuf Ali
138.text: "And we are not the ones to receive Pains and Penalties!"

139.author: Ahmed Ali
139.text: Then they accused him of lies; so We annihilated them. Verily there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

139.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
139.text: In response they denied him – We therefore destroyed them; indeed in this is a sign; and most of them were not believers.

139.author: Arberry
139.text: So they cried him lies; then We destroyed them. Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

139.author: Daryabadi
139.text: And they belied him; so We destroyed them. Verily herein is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

139.author: Hilali & Khan
139.text: So they belied him, and We destroyed them. Verily! In this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

139.author: Itani
139.text: So they denied him, and We destroyed them. Surely in this is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

139.author: Maududi
139.text: Eventually they gave the lie to him and We destroyed them. Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

139.author: Mubarakpuri
139.text: So they denied him, and We destroyed them. Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

139.author: Pickthall
139.text: And they denied him; therefor We destroyed them. Lo! herein is indeed a portent, yet most of them are not believers.

139.author: Qarai
139.text: So they impugned him, whereupon We destroyed them. There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

139.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
139.text: So they belied him, so We destroyed them. Surely, in that there is a sign; yet most of them do not believe.

139.author: Saheeh International
139.text: And they denied him, so We destroyed them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

139.author: Sarwar
139.text: In this there is evidence (of the Truth), yet most of them do not have any faith.

139.author: Shakir
139.text: So they gave him the lie, then We destroyed them. Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

139.author: Wahiduddin Khan
139.text: So they rejected him; and We destroyed them. There is certainly a sign in that; but most of them would not believe.

139.author: Yusuf Ali
139.text: So they rejected him, and We destroyed them. Verily in this is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

139.author: Transliteration
139.text: Fakaththaboohu faahlaknahum inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

140.author: Ahmed Ali
140.text: Yet surely your Lord is mighty and merciful.

140.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
140.text: And indeed your Lord – only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

140.author: Arberry
140.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

140.author: Daryabadi
140.text: And verily thy Lord? He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

140.author: Hilali & Khan
140.text: And verily! Your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

140.author: Itani
140.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

140.author: Maududi
140.text: Verily your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Most Compassionate.

140.author: Mubarakpuri
140.text: And verily your Lord, He is indeed the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

140.author: Pickthall
140.text: And lo! thy Lord, He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

140.author: Qarai
140.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

140.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
140.text: Surely, your Lord is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

140.author: Saheeh International
140.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

140.author: Sarwar
140.text: Your Lord is Majestic and All-merciful.

140.author: Shakir
140.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

140.author: Wahiduddin Khan
140.text: Surely your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

140.author: Yusuf Ali
140.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

141.author: Ahmed Ali
141.text: The Thamud accused the messengers of lies.

141.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
141.text: The Thamud tribe denied the Noble Messengers.

141.author: Arberry
141.text: Thamood cried lies to the Envoys

141.author: Daryabadi
141.text: The Thamud belied the sent ones

141.author: Maududi
141.text: The Thamud gave the lie to the Messengers.

141.author: Pickthall
141.text: (The tribe of) Thamud denied the messengers (of Allah)

141.author: Qarai
141.text: [The people of] Thamud impugned the apostles

141.author: Sarwar
141.text: The tribe of Thamud rejected the Messengers.

141.author: Shakir
141.text: Samood gave the lie to the apostles

141.author: Wahiduddin Khan
141.text: The tribe of Thamud also rejected the messengers.

142.author: Ahmed Ali
142.text: When their brother Saleh said to them: "Will you not take heed?"

142.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
142.text: When their fellowman Saleh said to them, “Do you not fear?”

142.author: Arberry
142.text: when their brother Salih said to them, 'Will you not be godfearing?

142.author: Daryabadi
142.text: When their brother Salih said unto them: fear ye not?

142.author: Hilali & Khan
142.text: When their brother Salih (Saleh) said to them: "Will you not fear Allah and obey Him?

142.author: Itani
142.text: When their brother Saleh said to them, “Do you not fear?

142.author: Maududi
142.text: Recall when their brother Salih said to them: "Have you no fear?"

142.author: Mubarakpuri
142.text: When their brother Salih said to them: "Will you not have Taqwa"

142.author: Pickthall
142.text: When their brother Salih said unto them: Will ye not ward off (evil)?

142.author: Qarai
142.text: when Salih, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]?

142.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
142.text: Their brother Salih said to them: 'Will you not be cautious?

142.author: Saheeh International
142.text: When their brother Salih said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?

142.author: Sarwar
142.text: Their brother Salih asked them, "Why do you not fear God?

142.author: Shakir
142.text: When their brother Salih said to them: Will you not guard (against evil)?

142.author: Wahiduddin Khan
142.text: When their brother Salih said to them, "Will you not fear God?

142.author: Yusuf Ali
142.text: Behold, their brother Salih said to them: "Will you not fear (Allah)?

142.author: Transliteration
142.text: Ith qala lahum akhoohum salihun ala tattaqoona

143.author: Ahmed Ali
143.text: I have been sent as a trusted messenger to you.

143.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
143.text: “I am indeed a trustworthy Noble Messenger of Allah to you.”

143.author: Arberry
143.text: I am for you a faithful Messenger,

143.author: Daryabadi
143.text: Verily I am unto you an apostle trusted.

143.author: Hilali & Khan
143.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

143.author: Itani
143.text: I am to you a faithful messenger.

143.author: Maududi
143.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

143.author: Mubarakpuri
143.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you."

143.author: Pickthall
143.text: Lo! I am a faithful messenger unto you,

143.author: Qarai
143.text: Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you.

143.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
143.text: I am for you an honest Messenger.

143.author: Saheeh International
143.text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

143.author: Sarwar
143.text: I am a trustworthy Messengers sent to you.

143.author: Shakir
143.text: Surely I am a faithful apostle to you

143.author: Wahiduddin Khan
143.text: Truly, I am a trustworthy messenger for you,

143.author: Yusuf Ali
143.text: "I am to you a messenger worthy of all trust.

144.author: Ahmed Ali
144.text: So fear God and follow me.

144.author: Arberry
144.text: so fear you God, and obey you me.'

144.author: Daryabadi
144.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

144.author: Hilali & Khan
144.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

144.author: Itani
144.text: So fear God, and obey me.

144.author: Maududi
144.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

144.author: Mubarakpuri
144.text: "So, have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

144.author: Pickthall
144.text: So keep your duty to Allah and obey me.

144.author: Qarai
144.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me.

144.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
144.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

144.author: Saheeh International
144.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

144.author: Sarwar
144.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

144.author: Shakir
144.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me:

144.author: Wahiduddin Khan
144.text: so fear God and obey me.

145.author: Ahmed Ali
145.text: I do not ask any recompense of you for this. My reward is due from none but the Lord of all the worlds.

145.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
145.text: “And I do not ask from you any fee for it; my reward is only upon the Lord Of The Creation.”

145.author: Arberry
145.text: 'I ask of you no wage for this; my wage falls only upon the Lord of all Being.

145.author: Daryabadi
145.text: And I ask of you no hire therefor; MY hire is but with the Lord of the worlds.

145.author: Hilali & Khan
145.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it (my Message of Islamic Monotheism), my reward is only from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

145.author: Itani
145.text: I ask of you no payment for it. My payment is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

145.author: Maududi
145.text: I ask of you no reward. My reward is with none but the Lord of the Universe.

145.author: Mubarakpuri
145.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it; my reward is only from the Lord of Al-`Alamin."

145.author: Pickthall
145.text: And I ask of you no wage therefor; my wage is the concern only of the Lord of the Worlds.

145.author: Qarai
145.text: I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.

145.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
145.text: For this I ask of you no wage; my wage falls only upon the Lord of the Worlds.

145.author: Saheeh International
145.text: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

145.author: Sarwar
145.text: I do not ask for any payment for my preaching. I shall receive my reward from the Lord of the Universe.

145.author: Shakir
145.text: And I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward is only with the Lord of the worlds:

145.author: Wahiduddin Khan
145.text: For this I demand no recompense from you; my reward is only with the Lord of the Universe.

145.author: Yusuf Ali
145.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it: my reward is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

145.author: Transliteration
145.text: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena

146.author: Ahmed Ali
146.text: (Do you think) you will be left secure here

146.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
146.text: “Will you be left peacefully in these favours over here?”

146.author: Arberry
146.text: Will you be left secure in this here,

146.author: Daryabadi
146.text: Shall ye be left secure in that which is here before us.

146.author: Hilali & Khan
146.text: "Will you be left secure in that which you have here?

146.author: Itani
146.text: Will you be left secure in what is here?

146.author: Maududi
146.text: Do you believe that you will be left here to live securely in the present state

146.author: Mubarakpuri
146.text: "Will you be left secure in that which you have here"

146.author: Pickthall
146.text: Will ye be left secure in that which is here before us,

146.author: Qarai
146.text: Will you be left secure in that which is here

146.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
146.text: Will you be left secure in this,

146.author: Saheeh International
146.text: Will you be left in what is here, secure [from death],

146.author: Sarwar
146.text: "Do you think that you will remain here peacefully forever

146.author: Shakir
146.text: Will you be left secure in what is here;

146.author: Wahiduddin Khan
146.text: Do you think that you will be left secure [forever] --

146.author: Yusuf Ali
146.text: "Will ye be left secure, in (the enjoyment of) all that ye have here?-

147.author: Ahmed Ali
147.text: In these gardens and these springs,

147.author: Wahiduddin Khan
147.text: in the midst of gardens and fountains,

148.author: Ahmed Ali
148.text: The fields of corn and date-palm trees with soft and tender spathes,

148.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
148.text: “And fields and palm-trees, with delicate tendrils?”

148.author: Arberry
148.text: sown fields, and palms with slender spathes?

148.author: Daryabadi
148.text: And corn-fields and palm-trees whereof the spathes are fine?

148.author: Hilali & Khan
148.text: And green crops (fields etc.) and date-palms with soft spadix.

148.author: Itani
148.text: And fields, and palm-trees whose fruits are delicious?

148.author: Maududi
148.text: and cornfields and date-palms laden with juicy fruits?

148.author: Mubarakpuri
148.text: "And crops and date palms with soft clusters."

148.author: Pickthall
148.text: And tilled fields and heavy-sheathed palm-trees,

148.author: Qarai
148.text: farms and date palms with dainty spathes

148.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
148.text: sown fields and palmtrees, with slender spathes.

148.author: Saheeh International
148.text: And fields of crops and palm trees with softened fruit?

148.author: Sarwar
148.text: farms, and palm-trees in thick groves,

148.author: Shakir
148.text: And cornfields and palm-trees having fine spadices?

148.author: Wahiduddin Khan
148.text: and cornfields, and palm-trees laden with fruit --

148.author: Yusuf Ali
148.text: "And corn-fields and date-palms with spathes near breaking (with the weight of fruit)?

149.author: Ahmed Ali
149.text: And dwellings hewed out of mountains ingeniously?

149.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
149.text: “And you carve out dwellings in the mountains, with skill?”

149.author: Arberry
149.text: Will you still skilfully hew houses out of the mountains?

149.author: Daryabadi
149.text: And hew ye out houses in the mountains skilfully! g

149.author: Hilali & Khan
149.text: "And you carve houses out of mountains with great skill.

149.author: Itani
149.text: And you skillfully carve houses in the mountains?

149.author: Maududi
149.text: You hew dwellings in mountains and exult in that.

149.author: Mubarakpuri
149.text: "And you hew out in the mountains, houses with great skill."

149.author: Pickthall
149.text: Though ye hew out dwellings in the mountain, being skilful?

149.author: Qarai
149.text: —while you skillfully hew out houses from the mountains?

149.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
149.text: Will you still hew your dwellings in the mountains?

149.author: Saheeh International
149.text: And you carve out of the mountains, homes, with skill.

149.author: Sarwar
149.text: carving comfortable houses out of the mountains?

149.author: Shakir
149.text: And you hew houses out of the mountains exultingly;

149.author: Wahiduddin Khan
149.text: hewing out houses in the mountains and taking pride in your skill?

149.author: Yusuf Ali
149.text: "And ye carve houses out of (rocky) mountains with great skill.

150.author: Ahmed Ali
150.text: So be mindful of God and listen to me.

150.author: Arberry
150.text: So fear you God, and obey you me;

150.author: Daryabadi
150.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

150.author: Hilali & Khan
150.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

150.author: Itani
150.text: So fear God, and obey me.

150.author: Mubarakpuri
150.text: "So, have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

150.author: Pickthall
150.text: Therefor keep your duty to Allah and obey me,

150.author: Qarai
150.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me,

150.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
150.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

150.author: Saheeh International
150.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

150.author: Sarwar
150.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

150.author: Shakir
150.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me;

150.author: Wahiduddin Khan
150.text: So fear God and obey me:

151.author: Ahmed Ali
151.text: Do not follow those who are extravagant,

151.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
151.text: “And do not follow those who exceed the limits.”

151.author: Arberry
151.text: and obey not the commandment of the prodigal

151.author: Daryabadi
151.text: And obey not the command of the extravagant.

151.author: Hilali & Khan
151.text: "And follow not the command of Al-Musrifun [i.e. their chiefs, leaders who were polytheists, criminals and sinners],

151.author: Itani
151.text: And do not obey the command of the extravagant.

151.author: Maududi
151.text: and do not follow the biddings of those that go to excesses

151.author: Mubarakpuri
151.text: "And follow not the command of the extravagant,"

151.author: Pickthall
151.text: And obey not the command of the prodigal,

151.author: Qarai
151.text: and do not obey the dictates of the transgressors,

151.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
151.text: Do not obey the order of the wasteful,

151.author: Saheeh International
151.text: And do not obey the order of the transgressors,

151.author: Sarwar
151.text: Do not obey the orders of the transgressors

151.author: Shakir
151.text: And do not obey the bidding of the extravagant,

151.author: Wahiduddin Khan
151.text: do not obey the bidding of those who are given to excesses,

151.author: Yusuf Ali
151.text: "And follow not the bidding of those who are extravagant,-

152.author: Ahmed Ali
152.text: Who corrupt the land and do not reform it."

152.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
152.text: “Those who spread turmoil in the earth, and do no reform.”

152.author: Arberry
152.text: who do corruption in the earth, and set not things aright.'

152.author: Daryabadi
152.text: Who work corruption in the land and rectify not.

152.author: Hilali & Khan
152.text: "Who make mischief in the land, and reform not."

152.author: Itani
152.text: Who spread turmoil on earth, and do not reform.”

152.author: Maududi
152.text: and spread mischief in the land rather than set things right."

152.author: Mubarakpuri
152.text: Who make mischief in the land, and reform not."

152.author: Pickthall
152.text: Who spread corruption in the earth, and reform not.

152.author: Qarai
152.text: who cause corruption in the land and do not bring about reform.’

152.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
152.text: who corrupt in the earth, and do not reform'

152.author: Saheeh International
152.text: Who cause corruption in the land and do not amend."

152.author: Sarwar
152.text: who spread evil in the land with no reform."

152.author: Shakir
152.text: Who make mischief in the land and do not act aright.

152.author: Wahiduddin Khan
152.text: those who spread corruption in the land instead of putting things right."

152.author: Yusuf Ali
152.text: "Who make mischief in the land, and mend not (their ways)."

152.author: Transliteration
152.text: Allatheena yufsidoona fee alardi wala yuslihoona

153.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
153.text: They said, “You are under a magic spell.”

153.author: Arberry
153.text: They said, 'Thou art merely one of those that are bewitched;

153.author: Daryabadi
153.text: They said: thou art but one of the bewitched;

153.author: Hilali & Khan
153.text: They said: "You are only of those bewitched!

153.author: Itani
153.text: They said, “You are surely one of the bewitched.

153.author: Maududi
153.text: They replied: "You are nothing but one of those who are bewitched;

153.author: Mubarakpuri
153.text: They said: "You are only of those bewitched!"

153.author: Pickthall
153.text: They said: Thou art but one of the bewitched;

153.author: Qarai
153.text: They said, ‘Indeed you are one of the bewitched.

153.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
153.text: They replied: 'Surely, you are one of those bewitched.

153.author: Saheeh International
153.text: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.

153.author: Sarwar
153.text: They said, "You are only bewitched and insane.

153.author: Shakir
153.text: They said: You are only of the deluded ones;

153.author: Yusuf Ali
153.text: They said: "Thou art only one of those bewitched!

154.author: Ahmed Ali
154.text: You are nothing but a man like us. Bring us a token if you speak the truth."

154.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
154.text: “You are just a human like us; therefore bring some sign if you are truthful.”

154.author: Arberry
154.text: thou art naught but a mortal, like us; then produce a sign, if thou art one of the truthful.'

154.author: Daryabadi
154.text: Thou art but a human being like unto us. So bring thou a sign if thou art of the truth-tellers.

154.author: Hilali & Khan
154.text: "You are but a human being like us. Then bring us a sign if you are of the truthful."

154.author: Itani
154.text: You are nothing but a man like us. So bring us a sign, if you are truthful.

154.author: Maududi
154.text: you are no different from a mortal like us. So produce a sign if you are truthful."

154.author: Mubarakpuri
154.text: "You are but a human being like us. Then bring us a sign if you are of the truthful."

154.author: Pickthall
154.text: Thou art but a mortal like us. So bring some token if thou art of the truthful.

154.author: Qarai
154.text: You are just a human being like us. So bring us a sign, should you be truthful.’

154.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
154.text: You are but a human like ourselves. Produce for us a sign, if you are of the truthful'

154.author: Saheeh International
154.text: You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful."

154.author: Sarwar
154.text: You are a mere mortal like us. Show us a miracle if you are telling the Truth".

154.author: Shakir
154.text: You are naught but a mortal like ourselves; so bring a sign if you are one of the truthful.

154.author: Wahiduddin Khan
154.text: You are only a human being like ourselves. Show us a sign, if you are telling the truth."

154.author: Yusuf Ali
154.text: "Thou art no more than a mortal like us: then bring us a Sign, if thou tellest the truth!"

154.author: Transliteration
154.text: Ma anta illa basharun mithluna fati biayatin in kunta mina alssadiqeena

155.author: Ahmed Ali
155.text: (Saleh) said: "Here is a she-camel. She has a right of drinking water, and you have a right of drinking it on appointed turns.

155.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
155.text: He said, “This is the she-camel – one day shall be her turn to drink, and on the other appointed day, shall be your turn.”

155.author: Arberry
155.text: He said, 'This is a she-camel; to her a draught and to you a draught, on a day appointed,

155.author: Daryabadi
155.text: He said: yonder is a she-camel; to her a drink, and to you a drink, each on a day known.

155.author: Hilali & Khan
155.text: He said: "Here is a she-camel; it has a right to drink (water), and you have a right to drink (water) (each) on a day, known.

155.author: Itani
155.text: He said, “This is a she-camel; she has her turn of drinking, and you have your turn of drinking—on a specified day.

155.author: Maududi
155.text: Salih said: "This is a she-camel. There is a day set for her to drink and there is a day set for you to drink.

155.author: Mubarakpuri
155.text: He said: "Here is a she-camel: it has a right to drink (water), and you have a right to drink (water) (each) on a day, known."

155.author: Pickthall
155.text: He said: (Behold) this she-camel. She hath the right to drink (at the well), and ye have the right to drink, (each) on an appointed day.

155.author: Qarai
155.text: He said, ‘This is a she-camel; she shall drink and you shall drink on known days.

155.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
155.text: He said: 'Here is a shecamel. She shall have her share of water as you have yours on an appointed day.

155.author: Saheeh International
155.text: He said, "This is a she-camel. For her is a [time of] drink, and for you is a [time of] drink, [each] on a known day.

155.author: Sarwar
155.text: He said, "This is a she-camel. She will have her share of water as you have your share, each on a certain day.

155.author: Shakir
155.text: He said: This is a she-camel; she shall have her portion of water, and you have your portion of water on an appointed time;

155.author: Wahiduddin Khan
155.text: He said, "Here is a she-camel. She shall have her turn of drinking, as you have yours, each on an appointed day,

155.author: Yusuf Ali
155.text: He said: "Here is a she-camel: she has a right of watering, and ye have a right of watering, (severally) on a day appointed.

155.author: Transliteration
155.text: Qala hathihi naqatun laha shirbun walakum shirbu yawmin maAAloomin

156.author: Ahmed Ali
156.text: Do not treat her unkindly or the punishment of a grievous day will fall on you."

156.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
156.text: “And do not touch her with evil intentions for the punishment of the Great Day will seize you.”

156.author: Arberry
156.text: and do not touch her with malice so that there seize you the chastisement of a dreadful day.'

156.author: Daryabadi
156.text: And touch her not with ill, lest there take hold of you the torment of a Mighty Day.

156.author: Hilali & Khan
156.text: "And touch her not with harm, lest the torment of a Great Day seize you."

156.author: Itani
156.text: And do not touch her with harm, lest the punishment of a great day seizes you.”

156.author: Maududi
156.text: Do not molest her lest the chastisement of an Awesome Day should seize you."

156.author: Mubarakpuri
156.text: "And touch her not with harm, lest the torment of a Great Day should seize you."

156.author: Pickthall
156.text: And touch her not with ill lest there come on you the retribution of an awful day.

156.author: Qarai
156.text: Do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by the punishment of a terrible day.’

156.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
156.text: Do not touch her with malice so that punishment of a dreadful day seizes you'

156.author: Saheeh International
156.text: And do not touch her with harm, lest you be seized by the punishment of a terrible day."

156.author: Sarwar
156.text: Do not cause her to suffer lest you become subject to the torment of the great day (of Judgment)."

156.author: Shakir
156.text: And do not touch her with evil, lest the punishment of a grievous day should overtake you.

156.author: Wahiduddin Khan
156.text: so do her no harm, or the punishment of an awful day will befall you."

156.author: Yusuf Ali
