156.text: "Touch her not with harm, lest the Penalty of a Great Day seize you."

156.author: Transliteration
156.text: Wala tamassooha bisooin fayakhuthakum AAathabu yawmin AAatheemin

157.author: Ahmed Ali
157.text: But they hamstrung her; and on the morrow were repentant,

157.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
157.text: So they hamstrung her, and in the morning could only regret.

157.author: Arberry
157.text: But they hamstrung her, and in the morning they were remorseful,

157.author: Daryabadi
157.text: Then they hamstrung her; and then they became regretful.

157.author: Hilali & Khan
157.text: But they killed her, and then they became regretful.

157.author: Itani
157.text: But they slaughtered her, and became full of remorse.

157.author: Maududi
157.text: But they hamstrung her and then regretted it.

157.author: Mubarakpuri
157.text: But they killed her, and then they became regretful.

157.author: Pickthall
157.text: But they hamstrung her, and then were penitent.

157.author: Qarai
157.text: But they hamstrung her, whereupon they became regretful.

157.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
157.text: Yet they hamstrung her, and in the morning they were remorseful,

157.author: Saheeh International
157.text: But they hamstrung her and so became regretful.

157.author: Sarwar
157.text: They slew the she-camel, but later became regretful

157.author: Shakir
157.text: But they hamstrung her, then regretted;

157.author: Wahiduddin Khan
157.text: Yet they hamstrung her, and then they became regretful:

157.author: Yusuf Ali
157.text: But they ham-strung her: then did they become full of regrets.

158.author: Ahmed Ali
158.text: For they were seized by the torment. Verily there was a sign in this, but most of them did not believe.

158.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
158.text: The punishment therefore seized them; indeed in this is a sign; and most of them were not believers.

158.author: Arberry
158.text: and the chastisement seized them. Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

158.author: Daryabadi
158.text: So the torment took hold of them: verily herein is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

158.author: Hilali & Khan
158.text: So the torment overtook them. Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

158.author: Itani
158.text: So the punishment overtook them. Surely in this is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

158.author: Maududi
158.text: So the chastisement seized them. Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

158.author: Mubarakpuri
158.text: So, the torment overtook them. Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

158.author: Pickthall
158.text: So the retribution came on them. Lo! herein is indeed a portent, yet most of them are not believers.

158.author: Qarai
158.text: So the punishment seized them. There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

158.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
158.text: and the punishment seized them. Surely, in that there is a sign. Yet most of them do not believe.

158.author: Saheeh International
158.text: And the punishment seized them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

158.author: Sarwar
158.text: and torment struck them. In this there is evidence of the Truth, yet many people do not have any faith.

158.author: Shakir
158.text: So the punishment overtook them. Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

158.author: Wahiduddin Khan
158.text: so the punishment came down upon them. Surely in that there is a sign, but most of them would not believe.

158.author: Yusuf Ali
158.text: But the Penalty seized them. Verily in this is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

158.author: Transliteration
158.text: Faakhathahumu alAAathabu inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

159.author: Ahmed Ali
159.text: Yet verily your Lord is mighty and merciful.

159.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
159.text: And indeed your Lord – only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

159.author: Arberry
159.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

159.author: Daryabadi
159.text: And verily thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

159.author: Hilali & Khan
159.text: And verily! Your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

159.author: Itani
159.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

159.author: Maududi
159.text: Verily Your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Most Compassionate.

159.author: Mubarakpuri
159.text: And verily, your Lord He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

159.author: Pickthall
159.text: And lo! thy Lord! He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

159.author: Qarai
159.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

159.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
159.text: Your Lord, He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

159.author: Saheeh International
159.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

159.author: Sarwar
159.text: Your Lord is Majestic and All-merciful.

159.author: Shakir
159.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

159.author: Wahiduddin Khan
159.text: Your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

159.author: Yusuf Ali
159.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

160.author: Ahmed Ali
160.text: The people of Lot accused the messengers of lies.

160.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
160.text: The people of Lut denied the Noble Messengers.

160.author: Arberry
160.text: The people of Lot cried lies to the Envoys

160.author: Daryabadi
160.text: The people of Lut belied the sent ones.

160.author: Hilali & Khan
160.text: The prople of Lout (Lot) (those dwelt in the towns of Sodom in Palestine) belied the Messengers.

160.author: Itani
160.text: The people of Lot disbelieved the messengers.

160.author: Maududi
160.text: The people of Lot rejected the Messengers, branding them liars.

160.author: Mubarakpuri
160.text: The people of Lut denied the Messengers.

160.author: Pickthall
160.text: The folk of Lot denied the messengers (of Allah),

160.author: Qarai
160.text: The people of Lot impugned the apostles

160.author: Saheeh International
160.text: The people of Lot denied the messengers

160.author: Sarwar
160.text: The people of Lot rejected the Messengers.

160.author: Shakir
160.text: The people of Lut gave the lie to the apostles.

160.author: Wahiduddin Khan
160.text: Then the people of Lot rejected the messengers.

160.author: Yusuf Ali
160.text: The people of Lut rejected the messengers.

161.author: Ahmed Ali
161.text: When their brother Lot said to them: "Will you not take heed?

161.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
161.text: When their fellowman Lut said to them, “Do you not fear?”

161.author: Arberry
161.text: when their brother Lot said to them, 'Will you not be godfearing?

161.author: Daryabadi
161.text: When their brother Lut said unto them: fear ye not?

161.author: Hilali & Khan
161.text: When their brother Lout (Lot) said to them: "Will you not fear Allah and obey Him?

161.author: Itani
161.text: When their brother Lot said to them, “Do you not fear?

161.author: Maududi
161.text: Recall, when their brother Lot said to them: "Have you no fear?

161.author: Mubarakpuri
161.text: When their brother Lut said to them: "Will you not have Taqwa"

161.author: Pickthall
161.text: When their brother Lot said unto them: Will ye not ward off (evil)?

161.author: Qarai
161.text: when Lot, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]?

161.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
161.text: When their brother Lot said to them: 'Will you not be cautious?

161.author: Saheeh International
161.text: When their brother Lot said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?

161.author: Sarwar
161.text: Their brother Lot asked them, "Why do you not have fear of God?

161.author: Shakir
161.text: When their brother Lut said to them: Will you not guard (against evil)?

161.author: Wahiduddin Khan
161.text: When their brother Lot said to them, "Will you not fear God?

161.author: Yusuf Ali
161.text: Behold, their brother Lut said to them: "Will ye not fear (Allah)?

161.author: Transliteration
161.text: Ith qala lahum akhoohum lootun ala tattaqoona

162.author: Ahmed Ali
162.text: I have been sent as a trusted messenger to you.

162.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
162.text: “I am indeed a trustworthy Noble Messenger of Allah to you.”

162.author: Arberry
162.text: I am for you a faithful Messenger,

162.author: Daryabadi
162.text: Verily I am unto you an apostle trusted.

162.author: Hilali & Khan
162.text: "Verily! I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

162.author: Itani
162.text: I am to you a faithful messenger.

162.author: Maududi
162.text: I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

162.author: Mubarakpuri
162.text: "Verily, I am a trustworthy Messenger to you."

162.author: Pickthall
162.text: Lo! I am a faithful messenger unto you,

162.author: Qarai
162.text: Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you.

162.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
162.text: I am for you an honest Messenger.

162.author: Saheeh International
162.text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

162.author: Shakir
162.text: Surely I am a faithful apostle to you;

162.author: Wahiduddin Khan
162.text: I am a trustworthy messenger to you:

162.author: Yusuf Ali
162.text: "I am to you a messenger worthy of all trust.

163.author: Ahmed Ali
163.text: So fear God and listen to me.

163.author: Arberry
163.text: so fear you God, and obey you me.

163.author: Daryabadi
163.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

163.author: Hilali & Khan
163.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

163.author: Itani
163.text: So fear God, and obey me.

163.author: Maududi
163.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

163.author: Mubarakpuri
163.text: "So, have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

163.author: Pickthall
163.text: So keep your duty to Allah and obey me.

163.author: Qarai
163.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me.

163.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
163.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

163.author: Saheeh International
163.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

163.author: Sarwar
163.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

163.author: Shakir
163.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me:

163.author: Wahiduddin Khan
163.text: so fear God and obey me.

164.author: Ahmed Ali
164.text: I ask no recompense of you for this. My reward is due from none but the Lord of all the worlds.

164.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
164.text: “And I do not ask from you any fee for it; my reward is only upon the Lord Of The Creation.”

164.author: Arberry
164.text: I ask of you no wage for this; my wage falls only upon the Lord of all Being.

164.author: Daryabadi
164.text: And I ask of you no hire there for; my hire is but with the Lord of the worlds.

164.author: Hilali & Khan
164.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it (my Message of Islamic Monotheism), my reward is only from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

164.author: Itani
164.text: I ask of you no payment for it. My payment is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

164.author: Maududi
164.text: I seek of you no reward. My reward is with none but the Lord of the Universe.

164.author: Mubarakpuri
164.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it; my reward is only from the Lord of all that exits."

164.author: Pickthall
164.text: And I ask of you no wage therefor; my wage is the concern only of the Lord of the Worlds.

164.author: Qarai
164.text: I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.

164.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
164.text: I ask of you no wage for this; my wage is only with the Lord of the Worlds.

164.author: Saheeh International
164.text: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

164.author: Sarwar
164.text: I do not ask any payment for my preaching. I shall receive my reward from the Lord of the Universe.

164.author: Shakir
164.text: And I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward is only with the Lord of the worlds;

164.author: Wahiduddin Khan
164.text: I ask of you no recompense for this; my reward is only with the Lord of the Universe.

164.author: Yusuf Ali
164.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it: my reward is only from the lord of the Worlds.

164.author: Transliteration
164.text: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena

165.author: Ahmed Ali
165.text: Why do you go for males unlike all other creatures

165.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
165.text: “What! Among all the creatures, you commit the immoral acts with men?”

165.author: Arberry
165.text: What, do you come to male beings,

165.author: Daryabadi
165.text: Go ye in, of all creatures, unto the males?

165.author: Hilali & Khan
165.text: "Go you in unto the males of the 'Alamin (mankind),

165.author: Itani
165.text: Do you approach the males of the world?

165.author: Maududi
165.text: What, of all creation will you go to (fornicate with) the males,

165.author: Mubarakpuri
165.text: "Go you in unto the males of Al-`Alamin (people),"

165.author: Pickthall
165.text: What! Of all creatures do ye come unto the males,

165.author: Qarai
165.text: What! Of all people do you come to males,

165.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
165.text: What, do you come to the males of the world,

165.author: Saheeh International
165.text: Do you approach males among the worlds

165.author: Sarwar
165.text: Do you, in the world, want to have carnal relations with males

165.author: Shakir
165.text: What! do you come to the males from among the creatures

165.author: Wahiduddin Khan
165.text: Do you, of all people, approach males,

165.author: Yusuf Ali
165.text: "Of all the creatures in the world, will ye approach males,

166.author: Ahmed Ali
166.text: Leaving the consorts your Lord has made for you? But you are a people who exceed the bounds."

166.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
166.text: “And leave the wives your Lord has created for you? In fact you are people who exceed the limits.”

166.author: Arberry
166.text: leaving your wives that your Lord created for you? Nay, but you are a people of transgressors.

166.author: Daryabadi
166.text: And leave ye your spouses your Lord hath created for you? Aye! ye are a people trespassing.

166.author: Hilali & Khan
166.text: "And leave those whom Allah has created for you to be your wives? Nay, you are a trespassing people!"

166.author: Itani
166.text: And forsake the wives your Lord created for you? Indeed, you are intrusive people.”

166.author: Maududi
166.text: leaving aside those whom Allah has created for you as your mates. Nay, you are a people that has transgressed all limits."

166.author: Mubarakpuri
166.text: "And leave those whom Allah has created for you to be your wives Nay, you are a trespassing people!"

166.author: Pickthall
166.text: And leave the wives your Lord created for you? Nay, but ye are froward folk.

166.author: Qarai
166.text: abandoning your wives your Lord has created for you? Indeed, you are a transgressing lot.’

166.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
166.text: and leave your wives whom your Lord has created for you? No, but you are a transgressing nation'

166.author: Saheeh International
166.text: And leave what your Lord has created for you as mates? But you are a people transgressing."

166.author: Sarwar
166.text: instead of your wives, whom your Lord has created specially for you? You are a transgressing people."

166.author: Shakir
166.text: And leave what your Lord has created for you of your wives? Nay, you are a people exceeding limits.

166.author: Wahiduddin Khan
166.text: and leave your wives whom your Lord has created for you? You are a people who transgress all bounds."

166.author: Yusuf Ali
166.text: "And leave those whom Allah has created for you to be your mates? Nay, ye are a people transgressing (all limits)!"

166.author: Transliteration
166.text: Watatharoona ma khalaqa lakum rabbukum min azwajikum bal antum qawmun AAadoona

167.author: Ahmed Ali
167.text: They said: "If you do not desist, O Lot, you will be expelled (from the city)."

167.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
167.text: They said, “O Lut, if you do not desist, you will be expelled.”

167.author: Arberry
167.text: They said, 'If thou givest not over, Lot, thou shalt assuredly be one of the expelled.'

167.author: Daryabadi
167.text: They said: if thou desistest not, O Lut! thou shalt surely be of those driven forth.

167.author: Hilali & Khan
167.text: They said: "If you cease not. O Lout (Lot)! Verily, you will be one of those who are driven out!"

167.author: Itani
167.text: They said, “Unless you refrain, O Lot, you will be expelled.”

167.author: Maududi
167.text: They said: "O Lot! If you do not desist, you will be one of those expelled (from our towns)."

167.author: Mubarakpuri
167.text: They said: "If you cease not, O Lut, verily, you will be one of those who are driven out!"

167.author: Pickthall
167.text: They said: If thou cease not, O Lot, thou wilt soon be of the outcast.

167.author: Qarai
167.text: They said, ‘Lot, if you do not desist, you will surely be banished.’

167.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
167.text: 'Lot' they replied, 'if you do not desist, you shall be thrown out'

167.author: Saheeh International
167.text: They said, "If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be of those evicted."

167.author: Sarwar
167.text: They said, "Lot, if you do not give up preaching, you will certainly be expelled (from this town)".

167.author: Shakir
167.text: They said: If you desist not, O Lut! you shall surely be of those who are expelled.

167.author: Wahiduddin Khan
167.text: They said, "If you do not desist, Lot, you will surely be banished."

167.author: Yusuf Ali
167.text: They said: "If thou desist not, O Lut! thou wilt assuredly be cast out!"

167.author: Transliteration
167.text: Qaloo lain lam tantahi ya lootu latakoonanna mina almukhrajeena

168.author: Ahmed Ali
168.text: (Lot) said: "I am disgusted with your actions,"

168.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
168.text: He said, “I am disgusted with your works.”

168.author: Arberry
168.text: He said, 'Truly I am a detester of what you do.

168.author: Daryabadi
168.text: He said: verily I am of those who abhor your work.

168.author: Hilali & Khan
168.text: He said: "I am, indeed, of those who disapprove with severe anger and fury your (this evil) action (of sodomy).

168.author: Itani
168.text: He said, “I certainly deplore your conduct.”

168.author: Maududi
168.text: He said: "I am one of those who abhor your practice.

168.author: Mubarakpuri
168.text: He said: "I am indeed of those who disapprove with severe anger and fury your behavior."

168.author: Pickthall
168.text: He said: I am in truth of those who hate your conduct.

168.author: Qarai
168.text: He said, ‘Indeed I detest your conduct.’

168.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
168.text: He said: 'Truly, I am a detester of what you do'

168.author: Saheeh International
168.text: He said, "Indeed, I am, toward your deed, of those who detest [it].

168.author: Sarwar
168.text: He said, "I certainly hate what you practice.

168.author: Shakir
168.text: He said: Surely I am of those who utterly abhor your doing;

168.author: Wahiduddin Khan
168.text: He said, "I am one of those who abhors your ways.

168.author: Yusuf Ali
168.text: He said: "I do detest your doings."

169.author: Ahmed Ali
169.text: (And prayed:) "O Lord, save me and my family from what they do."

169.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
169.text: “My Lord, rescue me and my family from their deeds.”

169.author: Arberry
169.text: My Lord, deliver me and my people from that they do.'

169.author: Daryabadi
169.text: My Lord! deliver me and my household from that which they work.

169.author: Hilali & Khan
169.text: "My Lord! Save me and my family from what they do."

169.author: Itani
169.text: “My Lord, save me and my family from what they do.”

169.author: Maududi
169.text: My Lord, deliver me and my family from their wicked deeds."

169.author: Mubarakpuri
169.text: "My Lord! Save me and my family from what they do."

169.author: Pickthall
169.text: My Lord! Save me and my household from what they do.

169.author: Qarai
169.text: ‘My Lord! Deliver me and my family from what they do.’

169.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
169.text: 'My Lord, save me and my people from that they are doing'

169.author: Saheeh International
169.text: My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do."

169.author: Sarwar
169.text: Lord, save me and my family from their deeds."

169.author: Shakir
169.text: My Lord! deliver me and my followers from what they do.

169.author: Wahiduddin Khan
169.text: My Lord, save me and my family from their evil doings."

169.author: Yusuf Ali
169.text: "O my Lord! deliver me and my family from such things as they do!"

170.author: Ahmed Ali
170.text: So We saved him and his whole family

170.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
170.text: We therefore rescued him and his entire family.

170.author: Arberry
170.text: So We delivered him and his people all together,

170.author: Daryabadi
170.text: So We delivered him and his household all.

170.author: Hilali & Khan
170.text: So We saved him and his family, all,

170.author: Itani
170.text: So We saved him and his family, altogether.

170.author: Maududi
170.text: Then We delivered him and all his family

170.author: Mubarakpuri
170.text: So, We saved him and his family, all,

170.author: Pickthall
170.text: So We saved him and his household, every one,

170.author: Qarai
170.text: So We delivered him and all his family,

170.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
170.text: So We saved him and all his people,

170.author: Saheeh International
170.text: So We saved him and his family, all,

170.author: Sarwar
170.text: We saved him and all of his family

170.author: Shakir
170.text: So We delivered him and his followers all,

170.author: Wahiduddin Khan
170.text: We saved him and all of his family,

170.author: Yusuf Ali
170.text: So We delivered him and his family,- all

171.author: Ahmed Ali
171.text: Except one old woman who remained behind.

171.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
171.text: Except one old woman, who stayed behind.

171.author: Arberry
171.text: save an old woman among those that tarried;

171.author: Daryabadi
171.text: Save an old woman among the lingerers.

171.author: Hilali & Khan
171.text: Except an old woman (his wife) among those who remained behind.

171.author: Itani
171.text: Except for an old woman among those who tarried.

171.author: Maududi
171.text: except an old woman who was among those that stayed behind.

171.author: Mubarakpuri
171.text: Except an old woman among those who remained behind.

171.author: Pickthall
171.text: Save an old woman among those who stayed behind.

171.author: Qarai
171.text: except an old woman who remained behind.

171.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
171.text: except an old woman who stayed behind,

171.author: Saheeh International
171.text: Except an old woman among those who remained behind.

171.author: Sarwar
171.text: except an old woman who remained behind.

171.author: Shakir
171.text: Except an old woman, among those who remained behind.

171.author: Wahiduddin Khan
171.text: except for an old woman who stayed behind,

171.author: Yusuf Ali
171.text: Except an old woman who lingered behind.

172.author: Ahmed Ali
172.text: Then We destroyed the rest of them,

172.author: Hilali & Khan
172.text: Then afterward We destroyed the others.

172.author: Maududi
172.text: Thereafter, We utterly destroyed the rest,

172.author: Mubarakpuri
172.text: Then afterward We destroyed the others.

172.author: Pickthall
172.text: Then afterward We destroyed the others.

172.author: Shakir
172.text: Then We utterly destroyed the others.

172.author: Wahiduddin Khan
172.text: then We totally destroyed the rest,

172.author: Yusuf Ali
172.text: But the rest We destroyed utterly.

173.author: Ahmed Ali
173.text: And rained on them a shower (of Stones). How terrible was the rain (that fell) on those who had been warned!

173.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
173.text: And We showered a rain upon them; so what a wretched rain for those who were warned!

173.author: Arberry
173.text: and We rained on them a rain; and evil is the rain of them that are warned.

173.author: Daryabadi
173.text: And We rained on them a rain. So ill was the rain on those warned?

173.author: Hilali & Khan
173.text: And We rained on them a rain (of torment). And how evil was the rain of those who had been warned.

173.author: Itani
173.text: And We rained down on them a rain. Dreadful is the rain of those forewarned.

173.author: Maududi
173.text: and We sent upon them a rain, an evil rain that fell on those who had been warned.

173.author: Mubarakpuri
173.text: And We rained on them a rain, and how evil was the rain of those who had been warned!

173.author: Pickthall
173.text: And We rained on them a rain. And dreadful is the rain of those who have been warned.

173.author: Qarai
173.text: and rained down upon them a rain [of stones]. Evil was the rain of those who were warned!

173.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
173.text: We rained upon them a rain, and evil is the rain (of stones) on those that are warned.

173.author: Saheeh International
173.text: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.

173.author: Sarwar
173.text: by pouring upon them a terrible shower of rain. How evil was the rain for those who had been warned!

173.author: Shakir
173.text: And We rained down upon them a rain, and evil was the rain on those warned.

173.author: Wahiduddin Khan
173.text: and We poured a rain [of destruction] down upon them -- and how evil was the rain which fell on those who were forewarned.

173.author: Yusuf Ali
173.text: We rained down on them a shower (of brimstone): and evil was the shower on those who were admonished (but heeded not)!

173.author: Transliteration
173.text: Waamtarna AAalayhim mataran fasaa mataru almunthareena

174.author: Ahmed Ali
174.text: Verily in this was a sign, but most of them do not believe.

174.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
174.text: Indeed in this is a sign; and most of them were not Muslims.

174.author: Arberry
174.text: Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

174.author: Daryabadi
174.text: Verily herein is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

174.author: Hilali & Khan
174.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

174.author: Itani
174.text: Surely in this is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

174.author: Maududi
174.text: Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

174.author: Mubarakpuri
174.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

174.author: Pickthall
174.text: Lo! herein is indeed a portent, yet most of them are not believers.

174.author: Qarai
174.text: There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

174.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
174.text: Surely, in that there is a sign. Yet most of them do not believe.

174.author: Saheeh International
174.text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

174.author: Sarwar
174.text: In this there is an evidence of the Truth, but many of them did not have any faith.

174.author: Shakir
174.text: Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

174.author: Wahiduddin Khan
174.text: Surely in this there is a sign: but most of them would not believe:

174.author: Yusuf Ali
174.text: Verily in this is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

174.author: Transliteration
174.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

175.author: Ahmed Ali
175.text: Yet surely your Lord is mighty and powerful.

175.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
175.text: And indeed your Lord – only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

175.author: Arberry
175.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

175.author: Daryabadi
175.text: And verily thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

175.author: Hilali & Khan
175.text: And verily! Your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

175.author: Itani
175.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

175.author: Maududi
175.text: Verily Your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Most Compassionate.

175.author: Mubarakpuri
175.text: And verily your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

175.author: Pickthall
175.text: And lo! thy Lord, He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

175.author: Qarai
175.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

175.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
175.text: Your Lord, He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

175.author: Saheeh International
175.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

175.author: Sarwar
175.text: Your Lord is Majestic and All-merciful.

175.author: Shakir
175.text: And most surely your Lord is the Mighty, the Merciful.

175.author: Wahiduddin Khan
175.text: your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

175.author: Yusuf Ali
175.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might Most Merciful.

176.author: Ahmed Ali
176.text: The people of the Wood too called the messengers liars.

176.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
176.text: The People of the Woods denied the Noble Messengers.

176.author: Arberry
176.text: The men of the Thicket cried lies to the Envoys

176.author: Daryabadi
176.text: The dwellers of the wood belied the sent ones.

176.author: Hilali & Khan
176.text: The dwellers of Al-Aiyka [near Madyan (Midian)] belied the Messengers.

176.author: Itani
176.text: The People of the Woods disbelieved the messengers.

176.author: Maududi
176.text: The people of Aykah also gave the lie to the Messengers.

176.author: Mubarakpuri
176.text: The companions of Al-Aykah denied the Messengers.

176.author: Pickthall
176.text: The dwellers in the wood (of Midian) denied the messengers (of Allah),

176.author: Qarai
176.text: The inhabitants of Aykah impugned the apostles,

176.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
176.text: The dwellers of the Thicket belied their Messengers.

176.author: Saheeh International
176.text: The companions of the thicket denied the messengers

176.author: Sarwar
176.text: The dwellers of the forest also rejected the Messengers.

176.author: Shakir
176.text: The dwellers of the thicket gave the lie to the apostles.

176.author: Wahiduddin Khan
176.text: The dwellers of the forest also rejected the messengers.

176.author: Yusuf Ali
176.text: The Companions of the Wood rejected the messengers.

177.author: Ahmed Ali
177.text: When Shu'aib said to them: "Will you not take heed?

177.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
177.text: When Shuaib said to all of them, “Do you not fear?”

177.author: Arberry
177.text: when Shuaib said to them, 'Will you not be godfearing?

177.author: Daryabadi
177.text: What time Shu'aib, said unto them: fear ye not?

177.author: Hilali & Khan
177.text: When Shu'aib (Shuaib) said to them: "Will you not fear Allah (and obey Him)?

177.author: Itani
177.text: When Shuaib said to them, “Do you not fear?

177.author: Maududi
177.text: Recall, when Shuayb said to them: "Have you no fear?

177.author: Mubarakpuri
177.text: When Shu`ayb said to them: "Will you not have Taqwa"

177.author: Pickthall
177.text: When Shu'eyb said unto them: Will ye not ward off (evil)?

177.author: Qarai
177.text: when Shu‘ayb said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]?

177.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
177.text: Shu'aib said to them: 'Will you not be cautious?

177.author: Saheeh International
177.text: When Shu'ayb said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?

177.author: Sarwar
177.text: Shu'ayb asked them, "Why do you not have fear of God?

177.author: Shakir
177.text: When Shu'aib said to them: Will you not guard (against evil)?

177.author: Wahiduddin Khan
177.text: Shu'ayb said to them, "Will you not fear God?

177.author: Yusuf Ali
177.text: Behold, Shu'aib said to them: "Will ye not fear (Allah)?

177.author: Transliteration
177.text: Ith qala lahum shuAAaybun ala tattaqoona

178.author: Ahmed Ali
178.text: I have been sent as a trusted messenger to you.

178.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
178.text: “I am indeed a trustworthy Noble Messenger of Allah to you.”

178.author: Arberry
178.text: I am for you a faithful Messenger, so fear you God, and obey you me.

178.author: Daryabadi
178.text: Verily I am unto you an apostle trusted.

178.author: Hilali & Khan
178.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

178.author: Itani
178.text: I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

178.author: Maududi
178.text: I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

178.author: Mubarakpuri
178.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you."

178.author: Pickthall
178.text: Lo! I am a faithful messenger unto you,

178.author: Qarai
178.text: Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you.

178.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
178.text: I am for you an honest Messenger.

178.author: Saheeh International
178.text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

178.author: Shakir
178.text: Surely I am a faithful apostle to you;

178.author: Wahiduddin Khan
178.text: I am a trustworthy messenger to you:

178.author: Yusuf Ali
178.text: "I am to you a messenger worthy of all trust.

179.author: Ahmed Ali
179.text: So fear God and follow me.

179.author: Arberry
179.text: I ask of you no wage for this;

179.author: Daryabadi
179.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

179.author: Hilali & Khan
179.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

179.author: Itani
179.text: So fear God, and obey me.

179.author: Maududi
179.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

179.author: Mubarakpuri
179.text: "So, have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

179.author: Pickthall
179.text: So keep your duty to Allah and obey me.

179.author: Qarai
179.text: So be wary of Allah and obey me.

179.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
179.text: So fear Allah, and obey me.

179.author: Saheeh International
179.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

179.author: Sarwar
179.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

179.author: Shakir
179.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me:

179.author: Wahiduddin Khan
179.text: so fear God, and obey me.

180.author: Ahmed Ali
180.text: I ask no recompense of you for this. My reward is due from none but the Lord of all the worlds.

180.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
180.text: “And I do not ask from you any fee for it; my reward is only upon the Lord Of The Creation.”

180.author: Arberry
180.text: my wage falls only upon the Lord of all Being.

180.author: Daryabadi
180.text: And ask of you no hire therefor; and my hire is but with the Lord of the worlds.

180.author: Hilali & Khan
180.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it (my Message of Islamic Monotheism), my reward is only from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

180.author: Itani
180.text: I ask of you no payment for it. My payment is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

180.author: Maududi
180.text: I ask of you no reward for this. My reward is with none but the Lord of the Universe.

180.author: Mubarakpuri
180.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it; my reward is only from the Lord of Al-`Alamin."

180.author: Pickthall
180.text: And I ask of you no wage for it; my wage is the concern only of the Lord of the Worlds.

180.author: Qarai
180.text: I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.

180.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
180.text: I ask of you no wage for this; my wage is only with the Lord of the Worlds.

180.author: Saheeh International
180.text: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

180.author: Sarwar
180.text: I do not ask any payment for my preaching. I shall receive my reward from the Lord of the Universe.

180.author: Shakir
180.text: And I do not ask you any reward for it, my reward is only with the Lord of the worlds;

180.author: Wahiduddin Khan
180.text: I ask of you no recompense for this; my reward is only with the Lord of the Universe.

180.author: Yusuf Ali
180.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it: my reward is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

180.author: Transliteration
180.text: Wama asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin in ajriya illa AAala rabbi alAAalameena

181.author: Ahmed Ali
181.text: Give full measure and do not cheat;

181.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
181.text: “Measure in full, and do not be of those who reduce.”

181.author: Arberry
181.text: Fill up the measure, and be not cheaters,

181.author: Daryabadi
181.text: Give full measure, and be not of those who cause others to lose.

181.author: Hilali & Khan
181.text: "Give full measure, and cause no loss (to others).

181.author: Itani
181.text: Give full measure, and do not cheat.

181.author: Maududi
181.text: Fill up the measure and do not diminish the goods of people,

181.author: Mubarakpuri
181.text: "Give full measure, and cause no loss (to others)."

181.author: Pickthall
181.text: Give full measure, and be not of those who give less (than the due).

181.author: Qarai
181.text: Observe the full measure, and do not be of those who give short measure.

181.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
181.text: Fill up the measure, do not be among the cheats,

181.author: Saheeh International
181.text: Give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss.

181.author: Sarwar
181.text: "Maintain just measure in your business and do not cause loss to others.

181.author: Shakir
181.text: Give a full measure and be not of those who diminish;

181.author: Wahiduddin Khan
181.text: Give full measure, and cause no loss to others.

181.author: Yusuf Ali
181.text: "Give just measure, and cause no loss (to others by fraud).

181.author: Transliteration
181.text: Awfoo alkayla wala takoonoo mina almukhsireena

182.author: Arberry
182.text: and weigh with the straight balance,

182.author: Daryabadi
182.text: And weigh with a balance straight.

182.author: Hilali & Khan
182.text: "And weigh with the true and straight balance.

182.author: Mubarakpuri
182.text: "And weigh with the true and straight balance."

182.author: Pickthall
182.text: And weigh with the true balance.

182.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
182.text: and weigh with the straight scale,

182.author: Saheeh International
182.text: And weigh with an even balance.

182.author: Sarwar
182.text: Weigh your goods with proper balance

182.author: Shakir
182.text: And weigh (things) with a right balance,

182.author: Yusuf Ali
182.text: "And weigh with scales true and upright.

183.author: Ahmed Ali
183.text: And do not withhold from people what belongs to them, and do not corrupt the land;

183.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
183.text: “And do not give the people their goods diminished, and do not roam the earth causing turmoil.”

183.author: Arberry
183.text: and diminish not the goods of the people, and do not mischief in the earth, working corruption.

183.author: Daryabadi
183.text: And defraud not people of their things, and commit not corruption on the earth.

183.author: Hilali & Khan
183.text: "And defraud not people by reducing their things, nor do evil, making corruption and mischief in the land.

183.author: Itani
183.text: And do not defraud people of their belongings, and do not work corruption in the land.

183.author: Maududi
183.text: and do not deliver short, and do not go about creating mischief in the land,

183.author: Mubarakpuri
183.text: "And defraud not people by reducing their things, nor do evil, making corruption and mischief in the land."

183.author: Pickthall
183.text: Wrong not mankind in their goods, and do not evil, making mischief, in the earth.

183.author: Qarai
183.text: and do not cheat the people of their goods. Do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.

183.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
183.text: and do not diminish the goods of the people, and do not make mischief in the earth, working corruption.

183.author: Saheeh International
183.text: And do not deprive people of their due and do not commit abuse on earth, spreading corruption.

183.author: Sarwar
183.text: and do not defraud people in their property or spread evil in the land.

183.author: Shakir
183.text: And do not wrong men of their things, and do not act corruptly in the earth, making mischief.

183.author: Wahiduddin Khan
183.text: do not defraud people of what is rightfully theirs; and do not spread corruption in the land.

183.author: Yusuf Ali
183.text: "And withhold not things justly due to men, nor do evil in the land, working mischief.

183.author: Transliteration
183.text: Wala tabkhasoo alnnasa ashyaahum wala taAAthaw fee alardi mufsideena

184.author: Ahmed Ali
184.text: And fear Him who created you and all the earlier people."

184.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
184.text: “And fear Him Who created you and the earlier creations.”

184.author: Arberry
184.text: Fear Him who created you, and the generations of the ancients.'

184.author: Daryabadi
184.text: So fear Him Who created you and the former generations.

184.author: Hilali & Khan
184.text: "And fear Him Who created you and the generations of the men of old."

184.author: Itani
184.text: And fear Him who created you and the masses of old.”

184.author: Maududi
184.text: and have fear of Him Who created you and the earlier generations."

184.author: Mubarakpuri
184.text: "And have Taqwa of Him Who created you and the generations of the men of old."

184.author: Pickthall
184.text: And keep your duty unto Him Who created you and the generations of the men of old.

184.author: Qarai
184.text: Be wary of Him who created you and the earlier generations.’

184.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
184.text: Fear He who created you, and the generations of the ancient'

184.author: Saheeh International
184.text: And fear He who created you and the former creation."

184.author: Sarwar
184.text: Have fear of the One who has created you and the generations that lived before you."

184.author: Shakir
184.text: And guard against (the punishment of) Him who created you and the former nations.

184.author: Wahiduddin Khan
184.text: Fear Him who created you and those who have gone before you."

184.author: Yusuf Ali
184.text: "And fear Him Who created you and (who created) the generations before (you)"

185.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
185.text: They said, “You are under a magic spell.”

185.author: Arberry
185.text: They said, 'Thou art merely one of those that are bewitched;

185.author: Daryabadi
185.text: They said: thou art but of the bewitched;

185.author: Hilali & Khan
185.text: They said: "You are only one of those bewitched!

185.author: Itani
185.text: They said, “You are one of those bewitched.

185.author: Maududi
185.text: They said: "You are no more than one of those who have been bewitched,

185.author: Mubarakpuri
185.text: They said: "You are only one of those bewitched!"

185.author: Pickthall
185.text: They said: Thou art but one of the bewitched;

185.author: Qarai
185.text: They said, ‘Indeed you are one of the bewitched.

185.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
185.text: They replied: 'You are surely one of those bewitched.

185.author: Saheeh International
185.text: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.

185.author: Sarwar
185.text: They said, "You are no more than a bewitched and insane man

185.author: Shakir
185.text: They said: You are only of those deluded;

185.author: Yusuf Ali
185.text: They said: "Thou art only one of those bewitched!

186.author: Ahmed Ali
186.text: You are nothing but a man like us, and we think you are a liar.

186.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
186.text: “You are just a human like us, and indeed we consider you a liar.”

186.author: Arberry
186.text: thou art naught but a mortal, like us; indeed, we think that thou art one of the liars.

186.author: Daryabadi
186.text: And thou art but a human being like unto us, and we deem thee to be of the liars.

186.author: Hilali & Khan
186.text: "You are but a human being like us and verily, we think that you are one of the liars!

186.author: Itani
186.text: And you are nothing but a man like us; and we think that you are a liar.

186.author: Maududi
186.text: you are only a mortal like us. Indeed we believe that you are an utter liar.

186.author: Mubarakpuri
186.text: "You are but a human being like us and verily, we think that you are one of the liars!"

186.author: Pickthall
186.text: Thou art but a mortal like us, and lo! we deem thee of the liars.

186.author: Qarai
186.text: You are just a human being like us, and we indeed consider you to be a liar.

186.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
186.text: You are but a human like ourselves, we think that you are one of the liars.

186.author: Saheeh International
186.text: You are but a man like ourselves, and indeed, we think you are among the liars.

186.author: Sarwar
186.text: and a mere mortal like us. We think you are a liar.

186.author: Shakir
186.text: And you are naught but a mortal like ourselves, and we know you to be certainly of the liars.

186.author: Wahiduddin Khan
186.text: You are only a human being like ourselves. Indeed we think you are a liar.

186.author: Yusuf Ali
186.text: "Thou art no more than a mortal like us, and indeed we think thou art a liar!

186.author: Transliteration
186.text: Wama anta illa basharun mithluna wain nathunnuka lamina alkathibeena

187.author: Ahmed Ali
187.text: But in case you are speaking the truth, then make a segment of the sky to fall upon us."

187.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
187.text: “Therefore cause a part of the sky to fall upon us, if you are of the truthful.”

187.author: Arberry
187.text: Then drop down on us lumps from heaven, if thou art one of the truthful.'

187.author: Daryabadi
187.text: So cause thou a fragment of the heaven to fall upon us, if thou art of the truth-tellers.

187.author: Hilali & Khan
187.text: "So cause a piece of the heaven to fall on us, if you are of the truthful!"

187.author: Itani
187.text: So bring down on us pieces from the sky, if you are truthful.”

187.author: Maududi
187.text: So cause a piece of the sky to fall upon us if you are truthful."

187.author: Mubarakpuri
187.text: "So, cause a piece of the heaven to fall on us, if you are of the truthful!"

187.author: Pickthall
187.text: Then make fragments of the heaven fall upon us, if thou art of the truthful.

187.author: Qarai
187.text: Make a fragment of the sky falls upon us, should you be truthful.’

187.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
187.text: Drop down on us lumps from heaven, if you are one of the truthful'

187.author: Saheeh International
187.text: So cause to fall upon us fragments of the sky, if you should be of the truthful."

187.author: Sarwar
187.text: Let a part of the sky fall on us if what you say is true".

187.author: Shakir
187.text: Therefore cause a portion of the heaven to come down upon us, if you are one of the truthful.

187.author: Wahiduddin Khan
187.text: So cause a fragment of the sky to fall on us, if you are truthful."

187.author: Yusuf Ali
187.text: "Now cause a piece of the sky to fall on us, if thou art truthful!"

187.author: Transliteration
187.text: Faasqit AAalayna kisafan mina alssamai in kunta mina alssadiqeena

188.author: Ahmed Ali
188.text: (Shu'aib) said: "My Lord knows well what you are doing."

188.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
188.text: He said, “My Lord is Well Aware of what you do.”

188.author: Arberry
188.text: He said, 'My Lord knows very well what you are doing.'

188.author: Daryabadi
188.text: He. said: My Lord is the Best Knower of that which ye work.

188.author: Hilali & Khan
188.text: He said: "My Lord is the Best Knower of what you do."

188.author: Itani
188.text: He said, “My Lord is Well Aware of what you do.”

188.author: Maududi
188.text: Shuayb said: "My Lord knows well all what you do."

188.author: Mubarakpuri
188.text: He said: "My Lord is the Best Knower of what you do."

188.author: Pickthall
188.text: He said: My Lord is Best Aware of what ye do.

188.author: Qarai
188.text: He said, ‘My Lord knows best what you are doing.’

188.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
188.text: He said: 'My Lord knows what you are doing'

188.author: Saheeh International
188.text: He said, "My Lord is most knowing of what you do."

188.author: Sarwar
188.text: He said, "My Lord knows all that you do."

188.author: Shakir
188.text: He said: My Lord knows best what you do.

188.author: Wahiduddin Khan
188.text: He said, "My Lord has full knowledge of all your actions."

188.author: Yusuf Ali
188.text: He said: "My Lord knows best what ye do."

189.author: Ahmed Ali
189.text: But they denied him, and We seized them with the torment of the Day of Shadowing (when the cloud had rained down fire). It was indeed the torment of a terrible day!

189.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
189.text: In response they denied him – therefore the punishment of the day of the tent* seized them; that was indeed a punishment of a Great Day. (The clouds formed a tent and rained fire upon them).

189.author: Arberry
189.text: But they cried him lies; then there seized them the chastisement of the Day of Shadow; assuredly it was the chastisement of a dreadful day.

189.author: Daryabadi
189.text: Thenn they belied him; wherefore there laid hold of -them the torment of day of shadow. Verily it was the torment of a Mighty Day.

189.author: Hilali & Khan
189.text: But they belied him, so the torment of the day of shadow (a gloomy cloud) seized them, indeed that was the torment of a Great Day.

189.author: Itani
189.text: But they denied him. So the punishment of the day of gloom gripped them. It was the punishment of a great day.

189.author: Maududi
189.text: Then they branded him a liar, whereupon the chastisement of the Day of Canopy overtook them. It was the chastisement of a very awesome day.

189.author: Mubarakpuri
189.text: But they denied him, so the torment of the Day of Shadow (a gloomy cloud) seized them. Indeed that was the torment of a Great Day.

189.author: Pickthall
189.text: But they denied him, so there came on them the retribution of the day of gloom. Lo! it was the retribution of an awful day.

189.author: Qarai
189.text: So they impugned him, and then they were overtaken by the punishment of the day of the overshadowing cloud. It was indeed the punishment of a terrible day.

189.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
189.text: But they belied him, then the punishment of the Day of Shadow (raining fire) seized them. Truly, it was the punishment of a dreadful day.

189.author: Saheeh International
189.text: And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.

189.author: Sarwar
189.text: They rejected him and then the torment of the gloomy day struck them. It was certainly a great torment.

189.author: Shakir
189.text: But they called him a liar, so the punishment of the day of covering overtook them; surely it was the punishment of a grievous day.

189.author: Wahiduddin Khan
189.text: They rejected him, and then had to suffer the punishment of a day of overshadowing gloom. That was indeed the punishment of an awful day.

189.author: Yusuf Ali
189.text: But they rejected him. Then the punishment of a day of overshadowing gloom seized them, and that was the Penalty of a Great Day.

189.author: Transliteration
189.text: Fakaththaboohu faakhathahum AAathabu yawmi alththullati innahu kana AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin

190.author: Ahmed Ali
190.text: Verily in this was a sign, but most of them do not believe.

190.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
190.text: Indeed in this is a sign; and most of them were not Muslims.

190.author: Arberry
190.text: Surely in that is a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

190.author: Daryabadi
190.text: Verily herein is a sign; but most of them are not believers.

190.author: Hilali & Khan
190.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

190.author: Itani
190.text: Surely in this is a sign, but most of them are not believers.

190.author: Maududi
190.text: Surely there is a Sign in this, but most of them would not believe.

190.author: Mubarakpuri
190.text: Verily, in this is indeed a sign, yet most of them are not believers.

190.author: Pickthall
190.text: Lo! herein is indeed a portent; yet most of them are not believers.

190.author: Qarai
190.text: There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith.

190.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
190.text: Surely, in that there is a sign; yet most of them do not believe.

190.author: Saheeh International
190.text: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.

190.author: Sarwar
190.text: In this there is an evidence of the Truth, but many of them did not have any faith.

190.author: Shakir
190.text: Most surely there is a sign in this, but most of them do not believe.

190.author: Wahiduddin Khan
190.text: Surely, in this is indeed a sign; but most of them would not believe:

190.author: Yusuf Ali
190.text: Verily in that is a Sign: but most of them do not believe.

190.author: Transliteration
190.text: Inna fee thalika laayatan wama kana aktharuhum mumineena

191.author: Ahmed Ali
191.text: Yet verily your Lord is mighty and merciful.

191.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
191.text: And indeed your Lord – only He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

191.author: Arberry
191.text: Surely thy Lord, He is the All-mighty, the All-compassionate.

191.author: Daryabadi
191.text: And verily thy Lord! He is the Mighty, the Merciful.

191.author: Hilali & Khan
191.text: And verily! Your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

191.author: Itani
191.text: Your Lord is the Almighty, the Merciful.

191.author: Maududi
191.text: Verily Your Lord is Immensely Mighty, Most Compassionate.

191.author: Mubarakpuri
191.text: And verily your Lord, He is indeed the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful.

191.author: Pickthall
191.text: And lo! thy Lord! He is indeed the Mighty, the Merciful.

191.author: Qarai
191.text: Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

191.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
191.text: Your Lord, He is the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

191.author: Saheeh International
191.text: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

191.author: Sarwar
191.text: Your Lord is Majestic and All-merciful.

191.author: Shakir
191.text: And most surely your Lord is Mighty, the Merciful.

191.author: Wahiduddin Khan
191.text: your Lord is the Mighty One, the Merciful.

191.author: Yusuf Ali
191.text: And verily thy Lord is He, the Exalted in Might, Most Merciful.

192.author: Ahmed Ali
192.text: And this (Qur'an) is a revelation from the Lord of all the worlds,

192.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
192.text: And indeed this Qur’an has been sent down by the Lord Of The Creation.

192.author: Arberry
192.text: Truly it is the revelation of the Lord of all Being,

192.author: Daryabadi
192.text: And verily it is a revelation of the Lord of the worlds.

192.author: Hilali & Khan
192.text: And truly, this (the Quran) is a revelation from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists),

192.author: Itani
192.text: It is a revelation from the Lord of the Worlds.

192.author: Maududi
192.text: Indeed this is a revelation from the Lord of the Universe;

192.author: Mubarakpuri
192.text: And truly, this is a revelation from the Lord of all that exists,

192.author: Pickthall
192.text: And lo! it is a revelation of the Lord of the Worlds,

192.author: Qarai
192.text: This is indeed [a Book] sent down by the Lord of all the worlds,

192.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
192.text: Truly, it is the sending of the Lord of the Worlds.

192.author: Saheeh International
192.text: And indeed, the Qur'an is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds.

192.author: Sarwar
192.text: This, (Quran), is certainly the revelation from the Lord of the Universe.

192.author: Shakir
192.text: And most surely this is a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.

192.author: Wahiduddin Khan
192.text: This surely is a revelation from the Lord of the Universe:

192.author: Yusuf Ali
192.text: Verily this is a Revelation from the Lord of the Worlds:

193.author: Ahmed Ali
193.text: Which the trusted spirit descended with

193.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
193.text: The Trustworthy Spirit brought it down. (Angel Jibreel – peace be upon him.)

193.author: Arberry
193.text: brought down by the Faithful Spirit

193.author: Daryabadi
193.text: The Trusted spirit hath brought it down.

193.author: Hilali & Khan
193.text: Which the trustworthy Ruh [Jibrael (Gabriel)] has brought down;

193.author: Itani
193.text: The Honest Spirit came down with it.

193.author: Maududi
193.text: which the truthful spirit has carried down

193.author: Mubarakpuri
193.text: Which the trustworthy Ruh (Jibril) has brought down.

193.author: Pickthall
193.text: Which the True Spirit hath brought down

193.author: Qarai
193.text: brought down by the Trustworthy Spirit

193.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
193.text: The honest Spirit (Gabriel) brought it down

193.author: Saheeh International
193.text: The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down

193.author: Sarwar
193.text: It has been revealed through the trustworthy Spirit

193.author: Shakir
193.text: The Faithful Spirit has descended with it,

193.author: Wahiduddin Khan
193.text: the Faithful Spirit has brought it down

193.author: Yusuf Ali
193.text: With it came down the spirit of Faith and Truth-

194.author: Ahmed Ali
194.text: To (communicate) to your heart that you may be a warner

194.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
194.text: Upon your heart, for you to convey warning.

194.author: Arberry
194.text: upon thy heart, that thou mayest be one of the warners,

194.author: Daryabadi
194.text: Upon thy heart, that thou mayest be of the warners,

194.author: Hilali & Khan
194.text: Upon your heart (O Muhammad SAW) that you may be (one) of the warners,

194.author: Itani
194.text: Upon your heart, that you may be one of the warners.

194.author: Maududi
194.text: to your heart that you might become one of those who warn (others on behalf of Allah),

194.author: Mubarakpuri
194.text: Upon your heart that you may be (one) of the warners,

194.author: Pickthall
194.text: Upon thy heart, that thou mayst be (one) of the warners,

194.author: Qarai
194.text: upon your heart (so that you may be one of the warners),

194.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
194.text: upon your heart (Prophet Muhammad), in order to be one of the warners,

194.author: Saheeh International
194.text: Upon your heart, [O Muhammad] - that you may be of the warners -

194.author: Sarwar
194.text: to your heart, so that you will warn (the people of the dangers of disobeying God).

194.author: Shakir
194.text: Upon your heart that you may be of the warners

194.author: Wahiduddin Khan
194.text: into your heart, so that you may be a warner,

194.author: Yusuf Ali
194.text: To thy heart and mind, that thou mayest admonish.

195.author: Sarwar
195.text: It has been revealed in plain Arabic.

196.author: Ahmed Ali
196.text: This is (indicated) in the Books of earlier people.

196.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
196.text: And indeed it is mentioned in the earlier Books.

196.author: Arberry
196.text: Truly it is in the Scriptures of the ancients.

196.author: Daryabadi
196.text: And verily it is in the Scriptures of the ancients.

196.author: Hilali & Khan
196.text: And verily, it (the Quran, and its revelation to Prophet Muhammad SAW) is (announced) in the Scriptures [i.e. the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] of former people.

196.author: Itani
196.text: And it is in the scriptures of the ancients.

196.author: Maududi
196.text: (a revelation embodied) in the scriptures of the ancients.

196.author: Mubarakpuri
196.text: And verily, it is in the Zubur of the former people.

196.author: Pickthall
196.text: And lo! it is in the Scriptures of the men of old.

196.author: Qarai
196.text: It is indeed [foretold] in the scriptures of the ancients.

196.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
196.text: Truly, it is in the Books of the ancients.

196.author: Saheeh International
196.text: And indeed, it is [mentioned] in the scriptures of former peoples.

196.author: Sarwar
196.text: Its news was also mentioned in the ancient Books.

196.author: Shakir
196.text: And most surely the same is in the scriptures of the ancients.

196.author: Wahiduddin Khan
196.text: Surely, it is foretold in the ancient scriptures.

196.author: Yusuf Ali
196.text: Without doubt it is (announced) in the mystic Books of former peoples.

197.author: Ahmed Ali
197.text: Was it not a proof for them that the learned men of Israel knew about this?

197.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
197.text: And was this not a sign for them, that the scholars of the Descendants of Israel may recognise this Prophet?

197.author: Arberry
197.text: Was it not a sign for them, that it is known to the learned of the Children of Israel?

197.author: Daryabadi
197.text: Is it not a sign unto them that the learned among the Children of Isra'il know it?

197.author: Hilali & Khan
197.text: Is it not a sign to them that the learned scholars (like 'Abdullah bin Salam who embraced Islam) of the Children of Israel knew it (as true)?

197.author: Itani
197.text: Is it not a sign for them that the scholars of the Children of Israel recognized it?

197.author: Maududi
197.text: Is it not a Sign to them - (to wit, the Makkans) - that the learned men of the Children of Israel know that?

197.author: Mubarakpuri
197.text: Is it not a sign to them that the learned scholars of the Children of Israel knew it

197.author: Pickthall
197.text: Is it not a token for them that the doctors of the Children of Israel know it?

197.author: Qarai
197.text: Is it not a sign for them that the learned of the Children of Israel recognize it?

197.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
197.text: Was it not a sign for them known to the learned of the Children of Israel?

197.author: Saheeh International
197.text: And has it not been a sign to them that it is recognized by the scholars of the Children of Israel?

197.author: Sarwar
197.text: Is not the fact (that the Israelite scholars already knew about the Quran through their Book) sufficient evidence for the pagans of the truthfulness (of the Quran)?

197.author: Shakir
197.text: Is it not a sign to them that the learned men of the Israelites know it?

197.author: Wahiduddin Khan
197.text: Is it not evidence enough for them that the learned among the Children of Israel have recognized this [as true]?

197.author: Yusuf Ali
197.text: Is it not a Sign to them that the Learned of the Children of Israel knew it (as true)?

197.author: Transliteration
197.text: Awalam yakun lahum ayatan an yaAAlamahu AAulamao banee israeela

198.author: Ahmed Ali
198.text: Had We revealed it to a man of obscure tongue

198.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
198.text: And had We sent it down upon a person other than an Arab,

198.author: Arberry
198.text: If We had sent it down on a barbarian

198.author: Daryabadi
198.text: And had We revealed it unto any of the ncn-Arabs,

198.author: Hilali & Khan
198.text: And if We had revealed it (this Quran) unto any of the non-Arabs,

198.author: Itani
198.text: Had We revealed it to one of the foreigners.

198.author: Maududi
198.text: (But such is their adamance) that had We revealed it to one of the non-Arabs and even if he had recited (this clear Arabic discourse) to them

198.author: Mubarakpuri
198.text: And if We had revealed it unto any of the non-Arabs,

198.author: Pickthall
198.text: And if We had revealed it unto one of any other nation than the Arabs,

198.author: Qarai
198.text: Had We sent it down upon some non-Arab

198.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
198.text: If We had revealed it to a nonArab,

198.author: Saheeh International
198.text: And even if We had revealed it to one among the foreigners

198.author: Sarwar
198.text: Had We revealed it to a non-Arab

198.author: Shakir
198.text: And if we had revealed it to any of the foreigners

198.author: Wahiduddin Khan
198.text: Had We revealed it to any one of the non-Arabs,

198.author: Yusuf Ali
198.text: Had We revealed it to any of the non-Arabs,

199.author: Ahmed Ali
199.text: And he had recited it to them, and they had not believed (it would have been different).

199.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
199.text: In order that he read it to them, even then they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Arberry
199.text: and he had recited it to them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Daryabadi
199.text: And he had-read it unto them, even then they would not have been believers therein.

199.author: Hilali & Khan
199.text: And he had recited it unto them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Itani
199.text: And he had recited it to them, they still would not have believed in it.

199.author: Maududi
199.text: they would still not have believed in it.

199.author: Mubarakpuri
199.text: And he had recited it unto them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Pickthall
199.text: And he had read it unto them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Qarai
199.text: and had he recited it to them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
199.text: and he had recited it to them, they would not have believed.

199.author: Saheeh International
199.text: And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.

199.author: Sarwar
199.text: who would have read it to them, they (pagans) would not have believed in it.

199.author: Shakir
199.text: So that he should have recited it to them, they would not have believed therein.

199.author: Wahiduddin Khan
199.text: and he had recited it to them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Yusuf Ali
199.text: And had he recited it to them, they would not have believed in it.

199.author: Transliteration
199.text: Faqaraahu AAalayhim ma kanoo bihi mumineena

200.author: Ahmed Ali
200.text: Thus do We cause (unbelief) to enter the hearts of sinners.

200.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
200.text: This is how We have made (the habit of) denying embedded in the hearts of the guilty.

200.author: Arberry
200.text: Even so We have caused it to enter into the hearts of the sinners,

200.author: Daryabadi
200.text: In this wise have We made way for it into the hearts of the culprits.

200.author: Hilali & Khan
200.text: Thus have We caused it (the denial of the Quran) to enter the hearts of the Mujrimun (criminals, polytheists, sinners, etc.).

200.author: Itani
200.text: Thus We make it pass through the hearts of the guilty.

200.author: Maududi
200.text: Thus have We caused this (Admonition) to penetrate the hearts of the culprits (like a hot rod).

200.author: Mubarakpuri
200.text: Thus have We caused it to enter the hearts of the criminals.

200.author: Pickthall
200.text: Thus do We make it traverse the hearts of the guilty.

200.author: Qarai
200.text: This is how We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty:

200.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
200.text: Even so, We have caused it to enter into the hearts of harmdoers:

200.author: Saheeh International
200.text: Thus have We inserted disbelief into the hearts of the criminals.

200.author: Sarwar
200.text: Thus it passes through the hearts of the criminals.

200.author: Shakir
200.text: Thus have We caused it to enter into the hearts of the guilty.

200.author: Wahiduddin Khan
200.text: We have thus caused denial of truth to enter into the hearts of the sinners:

200.author: Yusuf Ali
200.text: Thus have We caused it to enter the hearts of the sinners.

201.author: Ahmed Ali
201.text: They will not believe it until they behold the painful punishment.

201.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
201.text: They will not believe in it to the extent that they see the painful punishment.

201.author: Arberry
201.text: who will not believe in it, until they see the painful chastisement

201.author: Daryabadi
201.text: They will not believe therein until they behold the torment afflictive.

201.author: Hilali & Khan
201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the painful torment;

201.author: Itani
201.text: They will not believe in it until they witness the painful punishment.

201.author: Maududi
201.text: They will not believe in it until they clearly see the grievous chastisement.

201.author: Mubarakpuri
201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the painful torment.

201.author: Pickthall
201.text: They will not believe in it till they behold the painful doom,

201.author: Qarai
201.text: they do not believe in it until they sight the painful punishment.

201.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
201.text: they shall not believe in it until they see the painful punishment

201.author: Saheeh International
201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.

201.author: Sarwar
201.text: They will not believe in it until they suffer the painful torment.

201.author: Shakir
201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.

201.author: Wahiduddin Khan
201.text: they will not believe in it until they see the grievous punishment.

201.author: Yusuf Ali
201.text: They will not believe in it until they see the grievous Penalty;

201.author: Transliteration
201.text: La yuminoona bihi hatta yarawoo alAAathaba alaleema

202.author: Ahmed Ali
202.text: It will come upon them unawares, and they will not comprehend it.

202.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
202.text: So it will come upon them suddenly, whilst they will be unaware.

202.author: Arberry
202.text: so that it will come upon them suddenly, while they are not aware,

202.author: Daryabadi
202.text: It shall come unto them on a sudden, and they shall not perceive.

202.author: Hilali & Khan
202.text: It shall come to them of a sudden, while they perceive it not;

202.author: Itani
202.text: It will come to them suddenly, while they are unaware.

202.author: Maududi
202.text: But when it comes upon them suddenly, taking them unawares,

202.author: Mubarakpuri
202.text: It shall come to them of a sudden, while they perceive it not.

202.author: Pickthall
202.text: So that it will come upon them suddenly, when they perceive not.

202.author: Qarai
202.text: It will overtake them suddenly while they are unaware.

202.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
202.text: so that it will come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware,

202.author: Saheeh International
202.text: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.

202.author: Sarwar
202.text: The torment will strike them suddenly without their knowledge.

202.author: Shakir
202.text: And it shall come to them all of a sudden, while they shall not perceive;

202.author: Wahiduddin Khan
202.text: It will come upon them suddenly when they are not expecting it.

202.author: Yusuf Ali
202.text: But the (Penalty) will come to them of a sudden, while they perceive it not;

203.author: Ahmed Ali
203.text: Then will they say: "Can we be given respite?"

203.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
203.text: They will therefore say, “Will we get some respite?”

203.author: Arberry
203.text: and they will say, 'Shall we be respited?'

203.author: Daryabadi
203.text: Then they will say: are we to be respited?

203.author: Hilali & Khan
203.text: Then they will say: "Can we be respited?"

203.author: Itani
203.text: Then they will say, “Are we given any respite?”

203.author: Maududi
203.text: they say: "Can we be granted some respite?"

203.author: Mubarakpuri
203.text: Then they will say: "Can we be respited"

203.author: Pickthall
203.text: Then they will say: Are we to be reprieved?

203.author: Qarai
203.text: Thereupon they will say, ‘Shall we be granted any respite?’

203.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
203.text: and then they will say: 'Shall we be respited'

203.author: Saheeh International
203.text: And they will say, "May we be reprieved?"

203.author: Sarwar
203.text: They will say, "Can we be granted any respite?"

203.author: Shakir
203.text: Then they will say: Shall we be respited?

203.author: Wahiduddin Khan
203.text: Then they will exclaim, "Could we have some respite?"

203.author: Yusuf Ali
203.text: Then they will say: "Shall we be respited?"

204.author: Ahmed Ali
204.text: Do they wish Us then to hasten the punishment?

204.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
204.text: So do they wish to hasten Our punishment?

204.author: Arberry
204.text: What, do they seek to hasten Our chastisement?

204.author: Daryabadi
204.text: Seek haste then they with our torment?

204.author: Hilali & Khan
204.text: Would they then wish for Our Torment to be hastened on?

204.author: Itani
204.text: Do they seek to hasten Our punishment?

204.author: Maududi
204.text: Do they really want Our chastisement to be expedited?

204.author: Mubarakpuri
204.text: Would they then wish for Our torment to be hastened on

204.author: Pickthall
204.text: Would they (now) hasten on Our doom?

204.author: Qarai
204.text: So do they seek to hasten on Our punishment?

204.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
204.text: Do they wish to hasten Our punishment?

204.author: Saheeh International
204.text: So for Our punishment are they impatient?

204.author: Sarwar
204.text: Do they want to hasten Our torment?

204.author: Shakir
204.text: What! do they still seek to hasten on Our punishment?

204.author: Wahiduddin Khan
204.text: Do they want to hasten Our punishment?

204.author: Yusuf Ali
204.text: Do they then ask for Our Penalty to be hastened on?

205.author: Ahmed Ali
205.text: Just think: If We let them enjoy (the good things of life) for a few years more,

205.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
205.text: Therefore observe, that if We give them some comforts for some years, –

205.author: Arberry
205.text: What thinkest thou? If We give them enjoyment of days for many years,

205.author: Daryabadi
205.text: Beholdest thou? - if We let them enjoy for years.

205.author: Hilali & Khan
205.text: Tell Me, if We do let them enjoy for years,

205.author: Itani
205.text: Have you considered: if We let them enjoy themselves for some years.

205.author: Maududi
205.text: Did you consider that if We were to let them enjoy life for many years

205.author: Mubarakpuri
205.text: Think, if We do let them enjoy for years,

205.author: Pickthall
205.text: Hast thou then seen, if We content them for (long) years,

205.author: Qarai
205.text: Tell me, should We let them enjoy for some years,

205.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
205.text: What do you see? If We gave them enjoyment for years,

205.author: Saheeh International
205.text: Then have you considered if We gave them enjoyment for years

205.author: Sarwar
205.text: Do you not see that even if We give them respite for years

205.author: Shakir
205.text: Have you then considered if We let them enjoy themselves for years,

205.author: Wahiduddin Khan
205.text: Think! If We let them enjoy life for some years,

205.author: Yusuf Ali
205.text: Seest thou? If We do let them enjoy (this life) for a few years,

206.author: Ahmed Ali
206.text: And then what they were promised comes upon them,

206.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
206.text: And then the promise that is given to them, does come upon them-

206.author: Arberry
206.text: then there comes on them that they were promised,

206.author: Daryabadi
206.text: And then there cometh unto them that which they had been promised.

206.author: Hilali & Khan
206.text: And afterwards comes to them that (punishment) which they had been promised!

206.author: Itani
206.text: Then there comes to them what they were promised.

206.author: Maududi
206.text: and then the chastisement of which they were being warned were to come upon them,

206.author: Mubarakpuri
206.text: And afterwards comes to them that which they had been promised.

206.author: Pickthall
206.text: And then cometh that which they were promised,

206.author: Qarai
206.text: then there comes to them what they have been promised,

206.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
206.text: and then what they were promised comes to them,

206.author: Saheeh International
206.text: And then there came to them that which they were promised?

206.author: Sarwar
206.text: and then Our torment will strike them,

206.author: Shakir
206.text: Then there comes to them that with which they are threatened,

206.author: Wahiduddin Khan
206.text: and then the promised punishment fell upon them,

206.author: Yusuf Ali
206.text: Yet there comes to them at length the (Punishment) which they were promised!

207.author: Ahmed Ali
207.text: Of what avail shall their enjoyment be to them?

207.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
207.text: So of what benefit will be the comforts that they were using?

207.author: Arberry
207.text: what will it then avail them, the enjoyment of days they were given?

207.author: Daryabadi
207.text: What shall that which they enjoyed avail them?

207.author: Hilali & Khan
207.text: All that with which they used to enjoy shall not avail them.

207.author: Itani
207.text: Of what avail to them will be their past enjoyments?

207.author: Maududi
207.text: of what avail will be the provisions of life which they have been granted to enjoy?

207.author: Mubarakpuri
207.text: All that with which they used to enjoy shall not avail them.

207.author: Pickthall
207.text: (How) that wherewith they were contented naught availeth them?

207.author: Qarai
207.text: of what avail to them will be that which they were given to enjoy?

207.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
207.text: what avail will their past enjoyments be to them?

207.author: Saheeh International
207.text: They would not be availed by the enjoyment with which they were provided.

207.author: Sarwar
207.text: none of their luxuries will be able to save them from the torment?

207.author: Shakir
207.text: That which they were made to enjoy shall not avail them?

207.author: Wahiduddin Khan
207.text: of what avail would their past enjoyment be to them?

207.author: Yusuf Ali
207.text: It will profit them not that they enjoyed (this life)!

207.author: Transliteration
207.text: Ma aghna AAanhum ma kanoo yumattaAAoona

208.author: Ahmed Ali
208.text: Never have We destroyed a habitation till We had sent admonishers to them

208.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
208.text: And never did We destroy a township which did not have Heralds of warning, –

208.author: Arberry
208.text: Never a city We destroyed, but it had warners

208.author: Daryabadi
208.text: And We destroyed not a city but it had its warners.

208.author: Hilali & Khan
208.text: And never did We destroy a township, but it had its warners

208.author: Itani
208.text: Never did We destroy a town, but it had warners.

208.author: Maududi
208.text: We never destroyed any habitation but that it had warners

208.author: Mubarakpuri
208.text: And never did We destroy a township but it had its warners

208.author: Pickthall
208.text: And We destroyed no township but it had its warners

208.author: Qarai
208.text: We have not destroyed any town without its having warners,

208.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
208.text: We never destroyed a village that did not have warners

208.author: Saheeh International
208.text: And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners

208.author: Sarwar
208.text: We never destroyed any town without first sending to them warning and guidance.

208.author: Shakir
208.text: And We did not destroy any town but it had (its) warners,

208.author: Wahiduddin Khan
208.text: Never have We destroyed a town without sending down messengers to warn it,

208.author: Yusuf Ali
208.text: Never did We destroy a population, but had its warners -

208.author: Transliteration
208.text: Wama ahlakna min qaryatin illa laha munthiroona

209.author: Ahmed Ali
209.text: To warn. For We are never unjust.

209.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
209.text: To advise; and We never oppress.

209.author: Arberry
