209.text: for a reminder; and never did We wrong.

209.author: Daryabadi
209.text: By way of admonition, and We have never been oppressors.

209.author: Hilali & Khan
209.text: By way of reminder, and We have never been unjust.

209.author: Itani
209.text: As a reminder—We are never unjust.

209.author: Maududi
209.text: to admonish them. We have never been unjust.

209.author: Mubarakpuri
209.text: By way of reminder, and We have never been unjust.

209.author: Pickthall
209.text: For reminder, for We never were oppressors.

209.author: Qarai
209.text: for the sake of admonition, and We were not unjust.

209.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
209.text: for a reminder, and We never harmed.

209.author: Saheeh International
209.text: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.

209.author: Sarwar
209.text: We have never been unjust to anyone.

209.author: Shakir
209.text: To remind, and We are never unjust.

209.author: Wahiduddin Khan
209.text: as a reminder from Us: We are never unjust.

209.author: Yusuf Ali
209.text: By way of reminder; and We never are unjust.

210.author: Ahmed Ali
210.text: The devils did not bring it down:

210.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
210.text: And this Qur’an was not brought down by the devils.

210.author: Arberry
210.text: Not by the Satans has it been brought down;

210.author: Daryabadi
210.text: And the satans have not brought it down.

210.author: Hilali & Khan
210.text: And it is not the Shayatin (devils) who have brought it (this Quran) down,

210.author: Itani
210.text: It was not the devils that revealed it.

210.author: Maududi
210.text: The satans did not bring down this (Clear Book),

210.author: Mubarakpuri
210.text: And it is not the Shayatin (devils) who have brought it down.

210.author: Pickthall
210.text: The devils did not bring it down.

210.author: Qarai
210.text: It has not been brought down by the devils.

210.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
210.text: It was not the satans who brought it down:

210.author: Saheeh International
210.text: And the devils have not brought the revelation down.

210.author: Sarwar
210.text: The satans have not revealed the Quran;

210.author: Shakir
210.text: And the Shaitans have not come down with it.

210.author: Wahiduddin Khan
210.text: It was not the devils who brought down the Quran:

210.author: Yusuf Ali
210.text: No evil ones have brought down this (Revelation):

211.author: Ahmed Ali
211.text: They are not worthy of it, nor have they power.

211.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
211.text: They are unworthy of it, nor can they do it.

211.author: Arberry
211.text: it behoves them not, neither are they able.

211.author: Daryabadi
211.text: It behoveth them not, nor they can.

211.author: Hilali & Khan
211.text: Neither would it suit them, nor they can (produce it).

211.author: Itani
211.text: It is not in their interests, nor in their power.

211.author: Maududi
211.text: nor does it behove them, nor does it lie in their power.

211.author: Mubarakpuri
211.text: Neither would it suit them nor are they able.

211.author: Pickthall
211.text: It is not meet for them, nor is it in their power,

211.author: Qarai
211.text: Neither does it behoove them, nor are they capable [of doing that].

211.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
211.text: it is not for them, nor are they able.

211.author: Saheeh International
211.text: It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.

211.author: Sarwar
211.text: they are not supposed to do so. Nor do have they the ability for such a task.

211.author: Shakir
211.text: And it behoves them not, and they have not the power to do (it).

211.author: Wahiduddin Khan
211.text: neither are they worthy of it, nor are they capable of it,

211.author: Yusuf Ali
211.text: It would neither suit them nor would they be able (to produce it).

212.author: Ahmed Ali
212.text: They have been precluded from hearing it.

212.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
212.text: Indeed they have been banished from the place of hearing.

212.author: Arberry
212.text: Truly, they are expelled from hearing.

212.author: Daryabadi
212.text: Verily far from hearing are they removed.

212.author: Hilali & Khan
212.text: Verily, they have been removed far from hearing it.

212.author: Maududi
212.text: Indeed they are debarred from even hearing it.

212.author: Mubarakpuri
212.text: Verily, they have been removed far from hearing it.

212.author: Pickthall
212.text: Lo! verily they are banished from the hearing.

212.author: Qarai
212.text: Indeed, they are kept at bay [even] from hearing it.

212.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
212.text: Truly, they are expelled from hearing.

212.author: Saheeh International
212.text: Indeed they, from [its] hearing, are removed.

212.author: Sarwar
212.text: The satans are barred from listening to anything from the heavens.

212.author: Shakir
212.text: Most surely they are far removed from the hearing of it.

212.author: Wahiduddin Khan
212.text: indeed they are debarred from overhearing it.

212.author: Yusuf Ali
212.text: Indeed they have been removed far from even (a chance of) hearing it.

213.author: Ahmed Ali
213.text: So call on no other god but God, lest you are condemned to torment;

213.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
213.text: Therefore do not worship another deity along with Allah, for you will be punished.

213.author: Arberry
213.text: So call thou not upon another god with God, lest thou shouldst be one of those that are chastised.

213.author: Daryabadi
213.text: So call not thou unto anot her god along with Allah, lest thou be of the doomed.

213.author: Hilali & Khan
213.text: So invoke not with Allah another ilah (god) lest you be among those who receive punishment.

213.author: Itani
213.text: So do not pray to another god with God, else you will be of those tormented.

213.author: Maududi
213.text: So do not call any other god beside Allah lest you become of those who will be punished,

213.author: Mubarakpuri
213.text: So, invoke not with Allah another god lest you should be among those who receive punishment.

213.author: Pickthall
213.text: Therefor invoke not with Allah another god, lest thou be one of the doomed.

213.author: Qarai
213.text: So do not invoke any god besides Allah, lest you should be among the punished.

213.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
213.text: So do not call upon another god with Allah, lest you should be one of those who are punished.

213.author: Saheeh International
213.text: So do not invoke with Allah another deity and [thus] be among the punished.

213.author: Sarwar
213.text: (Muhammad), do not worship anything besides God lest you suffer the punishment.

213.author: Shakir
213.text: So call not upon another god with Allah, lest you be of those who are punished.

213.author: Wahiduddin Khan
213.text: So do not call on any deity besides God, lest you incur His punishment.

213.author: Yusuf Ali
213.text: So call not on any other god with Allah, or thou wilt be among those under the Penalty.

213.author: Transliteration
213.text: Fala tadAAu maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara fatakoona mina almuAAaththabeena

214.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
214.text: And O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), warn your closest relatives.

214.author: Arberry
214.text: And warn thy clan, thy nearest kin.

214.author: Daryabadi
214.text: And warn thou thy clan, the nearest ones.

214.author: Hilali & Khan
214.text: And warn your tribe (O Muhammad SAW) of near kindred.

214.author: Mubarakpuri
214.text: And warn your tribe of near kindred.

214.author: Pickthall
214.text: And warn thy tribe of near kindred,

214.author: Qarai
214.text: Warn the nearest of your kinsfolk,

214.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
214.text: Warn your tribe and your near kinsmen.

214.author: Saheeh International
214.text: And warn, [O Muhammad], your closest kindred.

215.author: Ahmed Ali
215.text: And take those believers under your wing who follow you.

215.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
215.text: And spread your wing of mercy for the believers following you.

215.author: Arberry
215.text: Lower thy wing to those who follow thee, being believers;

215.author: Daryabadi
215.text: And lower thou thy Wlng Unto those who follow thee as believers.

215.author: Hilali & Khan
215.text: And be kind and humble to the believers who follow you.

215.author: Itani
215.text: And lower your wing to those of the believers who follow you.

215.author: Maududi
215.text: and be meek to the believers who follow you.

215.author: Mubarakpuri
215.text: And be kind and humble to the believers who follow you.

215.author: Pickthall
215.text: And lower thy wing (in kindness) unto those believers who follow thee.

215.author: Qarai
215.text: and lower your wing to the faithful who follow you.

215.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
215.text: and lower your wing to the believers who follow you.

215.author: Saheeh International
215.text: And lower your wing to those who follow you of the believers.

215.author: Sarwar
215.text: and be kind to your believing followers.

215.author: Shakir
215.text: And be kind to him who follows you of the believers.

215.author: Wahiduddin Khan
215.text: and extend kindness and affection to those of the believers who follow you.

215.author: Yusuf Ali
215.text: And lower thy wing to the Believers who follow thee.

215.author: Transliteration
215.text: Waikhfid janahaka limani ittabaAAaka mina almumineena

216.author: Ahmed Ali
216.text: If they do not obey you, tell them: "I am not responsible for what you do."

216.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
216.text: So if they do not obey you, then say, “Indeed I am unconcerned with what you do.”

216.author: Arberry
216.text: then, if they disobey thee, say, 'I am quit of that you do.'

216.author: Daryabadi
216.text: And if they disobey thee, say thou: verily I am quit of that which ye work.

216.author: Hilali & Khan
216.text: Then if they disobey you, say: "I am innocent of what you do."

216.author: Itani
216.text: And if they disobey you, say, “I am innocent of what you do.”

216.author: Maududi
216.text: Then if they disobey you say to them: "I am quit of what you do."

216.author: Mubarakpuri
216.text: Then if they disobey you, say: "I am innocent of what you do."

216.author: Pickthall
216.text: And if they (thy kinsfolk) disobey thee, say: Lo! I am innocent of what they do.

216.author: Qarai
216.text: But if they disobey you, say, ‘I am absolved of what you do.’

216.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
216.text: If they disobey you, say: 'I am quit of what you do'

216.author: Saheeh International
216.text: And if they disobey you, then say, "Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing."

216.author: Sarwar
216.text: If they disobey you, tell them, "I condemn your disobedient deeds".

216.author: Shakir
216.text: But if they disobey you, then say: Surely I am clear of what you do.

216.author: Wahiduddin Khan
216.text: If they disobey you, say, "I bear no responsibility for what you do."

216.author: Yusuf Ali
216.text: Then if they disobey thee, say: "I am free (of responsibility) for what ye do!"

216.author: Transliteration
216.text: Fain AAasawka faqul innee bareeon mimma taAAmaloona

217.author: Ahmed Ali
217.text: Have trust in the Mighty, the Merciful,

217.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
217.text: And rely upon (Allah) the Almighty, the Most Merciful.

217.author: Arberry
217.text: Put thy trust in the All-mighty, the All-compassionate

217.author: Daryabadi
217.text: And rely thou upon the Mighty, the Merciful.

217.author: Hilali & Khan
217.text: And put your trust in the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful,

217.author: Itani
217.text: And put your trust in the Almighty, the Merciful.

217.author: Maududi
217.text: And put your trust in Him Who is Immensely Mighty, Most Compassionate,

217.author: Mubarakpuri
217.text: And put your trust in the All-Mighty, the Most Merciful,

217.author: Pickthall
217.text: And put thy trust in the Mighty, the Merciful.

217.author: Qarai
217.text: And put your trust in the All-mighty, the All-merciful,

217.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
217.text: Put your trust in the Almighty, the Most Merciful,

217.author: Saheeh International
217.text: And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,

217.author: Sarwar
217.text: Have trust in the Majestic and All-merciful God,

217.author: Shakir
217.text: And rely on the Mighty, the Merciful,

217.author: Wahiduddin Khan
217.text: Put your trust in the Mighty One, the Merciful,

217.author: Yusuf Ali
217.text: And put thy trust on the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,-

218.author: Ahmed Ali
218.text: Who watches you when you get up,

218.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
218.text: Who watches you when you stand up.

218.author: Arberry
218.text: who sees thee when thou standest

218.author: Daryabadi
218.text: Who seeth thee when thou standest up

218.author: Hilali & Khan
218.text: Who sees you (O Muhammad SAW) when you stand up (alone at night for Tahajjud prayers).

218.author: Itani
218.text: He Who sees you when you rise.

218.author: Maududi
218.text: Who observes you when you rise (to pray)

218.author: Mubarakpuri
218.text: Who sees you when you stand up.

218.author: Pickthall
218.text: Who seeth thee when thou standest up (to pray)

218.author: Qarai
218.text: who sees you when you stand [for prayer],

218.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
218.text: who sees you when you stand

218.author: Saheeh International
218.text: Who sees you when you arise

218.author: Sarwar
218.text: who can see whether you stand up

218.author: Shakir
218.text: Who sees you when you stand up.

218.author: Wahiduddin Khan
218.text: who sees you when you stand up [for prayer],

218.author: Yusuf Ali
218.text: Who seeth thee standing forth (in prayer),

219.author: Ahmed Ali
219.text: And your movements among those who bow in homage to God.

219.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
219.text: And watches your movements among those who prostrate in prayer.

219.author: Arberry
219.text: and when thou turnest about among those who bow.

219.author: Daryabadi
219.text: And thy movements among those who fall prostrate.

219.author: Hilali & Khan
219.text: And your movements among those who fall prostrate (along with you to Allah in the five compulsory congregational prayers).

219.author: Itani
219.text: And your devotions amidst the worshipers.

219.author: Maududi
219.text: and observes your movements among those who prostrate themselves.

219.author: Mubarakpuri
219.text: And your movements among those who fall prostrate.

219.author: Pickthall
219.text: And (seeth) thine abasement among those who fall prostrate (in worship).

219.author: Qarai
219.text: and your going about among those who prostrate.

219.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
219.text: and when you turn among those who prostrate themselves.

219.author: Saheeh International
219.text: And your movement among those who prostrate.

219.author: Sarwar
219.text: or move during your prostration with the worshippers.

219.author: Shakir
219.text: And your turning over and over among those who prostrate themselves before Allah.

219.author: Wahiduddin Khan
219.text: and sees your movements among those who prostrate themselves:

219.author: Yusuf Ali
219.text: And thy movements among those who prostrate themselves,

220.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
220.text: Indeed only He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

220.author: Arberry
220.text: Surely He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

220.author: Daryabadi
220.text: Verily He! He is the Hearer, the Knower.

220.author: Hilali & Khan
220.text: Verily! He, only He, is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

220.author: Itani
220.text: He is indeed the Hearer, the Aware.

220.author: Mubarakpuri
220.text: Verily, He, only He, is the All-Hearer, the All-Knower.

220.author: Pickthall
220.text: Lo! He, only He, is the Hearer, the Knower.

220.author: Qarai
220.text: Indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing.

220.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
220.text: Surely, He is the Hearer, the Knower.

220.author: Saheeh International
220.text: Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

220.author: Shakir
220.text: Surely He is the Hearing, the Knowing.

220.author: Wahiduddin Khan
220.text: He is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

220.author: Yusuf Ali
220.text: For it is He Who heareth and knoweth all things.

221.author: Ahmed Ali
221.text: Should I tell you on whom the devils descend?

221.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
221.text: Shall I inform you upon whom do the devils descend?

221.author: Arberry
221.text: Shall I tell you on whom the Satans come down?

221.author: Daryabadi
221.text: Shall declare unto you upon whom the satans descend!

221.author: Hilali & Khan
221.text: Shall I inform you (O people!) upon whom the Shayatin (devils) descend?

221.author: Itani
221.text: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?

221.author: Maududi
221.text: O people, shall I tell you on whom it is that satans descend?

221.author: Mubarakpuri
221.text: Shall I inform you upon whom the Shayatin descend

221.author: Pickthall
221.text: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?

221.author: Qarai
221.text: Should I inform you on whom the devils descend?

221.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
221.text: Shall I tell you on whom the satans descend?

221.author: Saheeh International
221.text: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?

221.author: Sarwar
221.text: Should I tell you to whom the satans come?

221.author: Shakir
221.text: Shall I inform you (of him) upon whom the Shaitans descend?

221.author: Wahiduddin Khan
221.text: Shall I tell you upon whom the devils descend?

221.author: Yusuf Ali
221.text: Shall I inform you, (O people!), on whom it is that the evil ones descend?

221.author: Transliteration
221.text: Hal onabbiokum AAala man tanazzalu alshshayateenu

222.author: Ahmed Ali
222.text: They come down to every mendacious sinner,

222.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
222.text: They descend on every great accuser, extreme sinner. (The magicians)

222.author: Arberry
222.text: They come down on every guilty impostor.

222.author: Daryabadi
222.text: They descend upon every calumniator, sinner.

222.author: Hilali & Khan
222.text: They descend on every lying (one who tells lies), sinful person.

222.author: Itani
222.text: They descend upon every sinful liar.

222.author: Maududi
222.text: They descend on every forgerer steeped in sin,

222.author: Mubarakpuri
222.text: They descend on every lying, sinful person (Athim).

222.author: Pickthall
222.text: They descend on every sinful, false one.

222.author: Qarai
222.text: They descend on every sinful liar.

222.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
222.text: They descend on every guilty impostor.

222.author: Saheeh International
222.text: They descend upon every sinful liar.

222.author: Sarwar
222.text: They come to every sinful liar.

222.author: Shakir
222.text: They descend upon every lying, sinful one,

222.author: Wahiduddin Khan
222.text: They descend on every lying sinner,

222.author: Yusuf Ali
222.text: They descend on every lying, wicked person,

223.author: Ahmed Ali
223.text: Who listens to you (for spreading rumours); but most of them are liars.

223.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
223.text: The devils convey upon them what they heard, but most of them are liars.

223.author: Arberry
223.text: They give ear, but most of them are liars.

223.author: Daryabadi
223.text: Who give ear, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Hilali & Khan
223.text: Who gives ear (to the devils and they pour what they may have heard of the unseen from the angels), and most of them are liars.

223.author: Itani
223.text: They give ear, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Maududi
223.text: on those who whisper hearsay in the ears of people; and most of them are liars.

223.author: Mubarakpuri
223.text: Who gives ear, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Pickthall
223.text: They listen eagerly, but most of them are liars.

223.author: Qarai
223.text: They eavesdrop, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
223.text: They listen, but most of them are liars.

223.author: Saheeh International
223.text: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Sarwar
223.text: The satans try to listen to the heavens but many of them are liars.

223.author: Shakir
223.text: They incline their ears, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Wahiduddin Khan
223.text: who lends an ear to them, and most of them are liars.

223.author: Yusuf Ali
223.text: (Into whose ears) they pour hearsay vanities, and most of them are liars.

224.author: Ahmed Ali
224.text: As for the poets, only those who go astray follow them.

224.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
224.text: And the astray follow the poets.

224.author: Arberry
224.text: And the poets -- the perverse follow them;

224.author: Daryabadi
224.text: As for the poets -it is the seduced who follow them.

224.author: Hilali & Khan
224.text: As for the poets, the erring follow them,

224.author: Itani
224.text: And as for the poets—the deviators follow them.

224.author: Maududi
224.text: As for poets, only the wayward follow them.

224.author: Mubarakpuri
224.text: As for the poets, the astray follow them,

224.author: Pickthall
224.text: As for poets, the erring follow them.

224.author: Qarai
224.text: As for the poets, [only] the perverse follow them.

224.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
224.text: Poets are followed by the perverse.

224.author: Saheeh International
224.text: And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them;

224.author: Sarwar
224.text: Only the erring people follow the poets.

224.author: Shakir
224.text: And as to the poets, those who go astray follow them.

224.author: Wahiduddin Khan
224.text: And as for the poets -- it is the misled who follow them.

224.author: Yusuf Ali
224.text: And the Poets,- It is those straying in Evil, who follow them:

225.author: Ahmed Ali
225.text: Have you not seen that they wander distract in every valley,

225.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
225.text: Did you not see that they keep wandering in every valley?

225.author: Arberry
225.text: hast thou not seen how they wander in every valley

225.author: Daryabadi
225.text: Observest thou not, that they Wander about every vale.

225.author: Hilali & Khan
225.text: See you not that they speak about every subject (praising others right or wrong) in their poetry?

225.author: Itani
225.text: Do you not see how they ramble in every style?

225.author: Maududi
225.text: Do you not see that they wander about in every valley

225.author: Mubarakpuri
225.text: See you not that they speak about every subject in their poetry

225.author: Pickthall
225.text: Hast thou not seen how they stray in every valley,

225.author: Qarai
225.text: Have you not regarded that they rove in every valley,

225.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
225.text: Have you not seen how they wander in every valley,

225.author: Saheeh International
225.text: Do you not see that in every valley they roam

225.author: Sarwar
225.text: Have you not seen them wandering and bewildered in every valley

225.author: Shakir
225.text: Do you not see that they wander about bewildered in every valley?

225.author: Wahiduddin Khan
225.text: Do you not see how they wander aimlessly in every valley,

225.author: Yusuf Ali
225.text: Seest thou not that they wander distracted in every valley?-

225.author: Transliteration
225.text: Alam tara annahum fee kulli wadin yaheemoona

226.author: Ahmed Ali
226.text: And they say what they do not do,

226.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
226.text: And they speak what they do not do?

226.author: Arberry
226.text: and how they say that which they do not?

226.author: Daryabadi
226.text: And that they say that which they do not?

226.author: Hilali & Khan
226.text: And that they say what they do not do.

226.author: Itani
226.text: And how they say what they do not do?

226.author: Maududi
226.text: and say things which they do not act upon,

226.author: Mubarakpuri
226.text: And that they say what they do not do.

226.author: Pickthall
226.text: And how they say that which they do not?

226.author: Qarai
226.text: and that they say what they do not do?

226.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
226.text: and they say what they do not do?

226.author: Saheeh International
226.text: And that they say what they do not do? -

226.author: Sarwar
226.text: and preaching what they themselves never practice.

226.author: Shakir
226.text: And that they say that which they do not do,

226.author: Wahiduddin Khan
226.text: preaching what they do not practice.

226.author: Yusuf Ali
226.text: And that they say what they practise not?-

227.author: Ahmed Ali
227.text: Except those who believe and do the right, and remember God a great deal, who retaliate only when they have been wronged. Yet the oppressors will now come to know through what reversals they will be overthrown!

227.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
227.text: Except those who believed and did good deeds, and profusely remembered Allah, and took revenge after they had been wronged*; and soon the unjust will come to know upon which side they will be overturned**. (* The Muslim poets who praise Allah and the Prophet. ** The disbelievers will be punished.)

227.author: Arberry
227.text: Save those that believe, and do righteous deeds, and remember God oft, and help themselves after being wronged; and those who do wrong shall surely know by what overturning they will he overturned.

227.author: Daryabadi
227.text: Save those who believed and worked righteous works and remembered Allah much, and vindicated themselves after they had been wronged. And anon those who do wrong shall come to know with what a translating they shall be translated.

227.author: Hilali & Khan
227.text: Except those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), and do righteous deeds, and remember Allah much, and reply back (in poetry) to the unjust poetry (which the pagan poets utter against the Muslims). And those who do wrong will come to know by what overturning they will be overturned.

227.author: Itani
227.text: Except for those who believe, and do good deeds, and remember God frequently, and defend themselves after they are wronged. As for those who do wrong, they will know by what overturning they will be overturned.

227.author: Maududi
227.text: except those who believed and acted righteously and remembered Allah much, and when they themselves were subjected to wrong, they exacted retribution no more than to the extent of the wrong? Soon will the wrong-doers know the end that they shall reach.

227.author: Mubarakpuri
227.text: Except those who believe and do righteous deeds, and remember Allah much and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged. And those who do wrong will come to know by what overturning they will be overturned.

227.author: Pickthall
227.text: Save those who believe and do good works, and remember Allah much, and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged. Those who do wrong will come to know by what a (great) reverse they will be overturned!

227.author: Qarai
227.text: Barring those who have faith, do righteous deeds, and remember Allah much often, and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged. And the wrongdoers will soon know at what goal they will end up.

227.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
227.text: Except those who believe, and do good works and remember Allah in abundance and became victorious after they had been wronged. The wrongdoers will surely know which turn they will be returning to (Hell).

227.author: Saheeh International
227.text: Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allah often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.

227.author: Sarwar
227.text: The righteously striving believers among them who remember God very often and use their talent to seek help after they have been wronged are the exceptional. The unjust will soon know how terrible their end will be.

227.author: Shakir
227.text: Except those who believe and do good and remember Allah much, and defend themselves after they are oppressed; and they who act unjustly shall know to what final place of turning they shall turn back.

227.author: Wahiduddin Khan
227.text: Not so the true believers who do good works and remember God with fervour and defend themselves only after they are wronged. The wrongdoers will soon know how evil a turn their affairs will take.

227.author: Yusuf Ali
227.text: Except those who believe, work righteousness, engage much in the remembrance of Allah, and defend themselves only after they are unjustly attacked. And soon will the unjust assailants know what vicissitudes their affairs will take!

227.author: Transliteration
227.text: Illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati wathakaroo Allaha katheeran waintasaroo min baAAdi ma thulimoo wasayaAAlamu allatheena thalamoo ayya munqalabin yanqaliboona
